documents.title: RESOLUTION CONTRACT AWARD - ENERGY AGGREGATION 12-3-13 FINAL

pages.text: 227-13  December 3, 2013  RESOLUTION OF THE TOWNSHIP OF   WEST ORANGE , COUNTY OF   ESSEX ,  STATE OF NEW JERSEY, AUTHORIZING THE AWARD OF A CONTRACT TO A  THIRD PARTY POWER SUPPLIER TO PROVIDE ELECTRIC GENERATION  SERVICE TO   WEST ORANGE   COMMUNITY ENERGY   AGGREGATION  (WO CEA) PROGRAM PARTICIPANTS AND TO PROVIDE GOVERNMENT  ENERGY AGGREGATION SERVICES IN CONNECTION WITH THE   WO CEA  PROGRAM PURSUANT TO N.J.A.C. 14:4-6 .1   ET.   SEQ .  WHEREAS,   the Government Energy Aggregation Act of 2003 (P . L. 2003, c.24 ;  N.J.S.A.   48:3-93.1   et.   seq. ) governs the establishment of an government energy aggregation  program, which is a government-operated purchasing cooperative through which multiple  energy consumers join together for the purchase of electric utility services by a government  aggregator; and  WHEREAS,   the New Jersey Board of Public Utilities has promulgated rules  ( N.J.A.C.   14:4-6 .1   et.   seq )   for the implementation of government energy aggregation  programs; and  WHEREAS,   the Township of   West Orange   passed an ordinance (Ordinance No.  2381-13 ) on   August 20 , 2013 authorizing the formation of a Government Energy  Aggregation in the Township of   West Orange,   New Jersey, named the   West Orange  Community Energy Aggregation (" WO CEA") Program; and  WHEREAS,   the Township is acting as the Lead Agency of the   West Orange  Community Energy Aggregation; and  WHEREAS,   the residents of   West Orange   Township are included in the   WO CEA  on an "opt - out" basis, and the businesses of   West Orange   Township are included in the  WO CEA on an "opt-in" basis, as set forth in applicable rules ( N.J.A.C.   14:4-6 .1   et.   seq. );  and  WHEREAS,   by virtue of Ordinance No.   2381-13   and in accordance with New  Jersey Board of Public Utilities rules ( N.J.A.C.   14:4-6 .1   et.   seq. ) and applicable law (PL.  2003, C24)   West Orange   Township is authorized to award a contract for electric generation  service and government energy aggregation services on behalf of participating residential  and business customers; and  WHEREAS,   the Townshi p   of West Orange , in its capacity as Lead Agency,  publicly noticed and issued on   November 11 , 2013 a Request for Proposals (RFP) for the  Provision of Electric Generation Service and Government Energy Aggregation Services  for the   WO CEA Program has solicited proposals from New Jersey Board of Public  Utilities (NJBPU)-licensed and qualified electric power suppliers in New Jersey; and

WHEREAS,   the RFP provided for a two stage process that included: Stage l for the  submission of supplier questions and comments and the submission of qualification  documents by interested, NJBPU -licensed electric power suppliers; and Stage 2 for the  submission of bid prices; and  WHEREAS,   the RFP provides for the submission of separate pricing for electric  generation service for two distinct bid groups, including i) Participating Residential  Accounts; 2) Participating Business Commercial Accounts,   for   two   potential contract terms,  including 1 5 -month, and 2 1 -month,   and the RFP provides for the award of a contract for  both , none or one of the bid groups at the discretion of   West Orange   Township and in  accordance with the evaluation criteria set forth in the RFP; and  WHEREAS , during Stage I   non-binding Notices of Intent and other  qualification documents were received from   s i x (6 ) NJBPU-licensed electric power  suppliers; and  WHEREAS,   a number of supplier questions and comments were received and,  as a result, on   November 20 , 2013 a Responses to Supplier Questions and Addendum  No.1 to the RF P was issued and distributed to all suppliers that had submitted a Notice  of Intent to Bid   and/or requested a copy of the RFP ; and  WHEREAS,   Stage 2 price bids and accompanying Transmittal Letters were  submitted by   6   NJBPU-licensed electric power supplie rs by the 2 :00 p.m.   December 2 ,  2013   deadline set forth in the RFP; and  WHEREAS,   the   To wnship , in consultation with its energy agent, has conducted  extensive and in-depth evaluation of qualifications and analysis of bid prices, in accordance  with the evaluation criteria set fo rth in the RFP and Addendum No.1 ; and  WHEREAS,   as a result of the evaluation of qualifications and proposals and analysis  of bid prices submitted, the Township has determined that the proposal submitted by  ConEdison   Solutions , for a contract term of   21   months   i) provides the most overall benefit  for the Township and   WO CEA participants; and 2) will provide for contract power supply  prices that are lower than the applicable Public Service Gas and Electric Co. (PSE&G) Basic  Generation Service tariff prices for power supply , and make it feasible to award a contract,  for the Residential Account bid group; and, 3) provides the opportunity for residents  currently receiving budget billing from   PSE&G   to continue to receive that service from the  third party supplier;   and  WHEREAS  ,   as   a   result   of   the   evaluation   of   qualifications   and   proposals   and   analysis  of   bid   prices   submitted,   the   West   Orange   Township   Council   has   determined   that   none   of   the  proposals submitted for the   WO CEA business participants   warrant an award;   and

NOW, THEREFORE, BE IT RESOLVED   that the   Township of   West Orange  hereby awards a contract for the provision of electric generation service to the Participating  Residential Accounts bid group and the provision of government energy aggregation s  ervices in connection with the   WO CEA Program   to   ConEdison   Solutions   in accordance  with the terms and conditions of the RFP (including the applicable form of Master  Performance Agreement included therein), as am ended pursuant to Addendum No. 1   to the  RFP , ; and  FURTHER RESOLVED   that government energy aggregation services to be  provided by   ConEdison   Solutions ,   including implementation of the opt-out process for  residential customers as set forth in the RFP and applicable NJBPU rules, shall commence  immediately upon execution of the Master Performance Agreement, and that electric  generation service to be provided by   ConEdison   Solutions   to the   WO CEA-participating  customers shall commence with the   March   201 4   meter read date for each electric account  served, and shall continue for a   21 month term; and  FURTHER RESOLVED   that the following contract prices are awarded for  electric generation service   for the   21 month   term beginning in   March 2014 :  Bid Group   Bid Price   Term  Residential Accounts   $0 .1045/ kwh   21 months  FURTHER RESOLVED   that the   Mayor   is authorized to execute a Notice of Award  Letter to   ConEdison   Solutions ,   and   the Mayor   is authorized to execute and the Clerk to attest  to the execution of the Master Performance Agreement, in the form included in the RFP (as  amended pursuant to Addendum No. 1).  CERTIFICATION  I,   Karen J. Carnevale,   Municipal Clerk of the Township of West Orange, do hereby  certify that the foregoing resolution was duly adopted by the West Orange Township Council at  the meeting held on the   3rd   day of   December, 2013 .  Susan McCartney, Council President  Adopted: December 3, 2013

2391 -13  AN   ORDINANCE   AMENDING   CHAPTER   4,   SECTION   31   OF   THE   REVISED   GENERAL  ORDINANCES   OF   THE   TOWNSHIP   OF   WEST   ORANGE,   ENTITLED   “ REGISTRATION   OF  STREET ADDRESSES OF NONRESIDENT PROPERTY OWNERS ”  BE   IT   ORDAINED   BY   THE   TOWNSHIP   COUNCIL   OF   THE   TOWNSHIP   OF   WEST  ORANGE ,   in   the   County   of   Essex   and   State   of   New   Jersey,   that   Chapter   4,   Section   31   of   the  Revised   General   Ordinances   of   the   Township   of   West   Orange   (the   “ Revised   General  Ordinances ” ) is hereby amended as follows:  I.   PURPOSE  The   purpose   of   this   Ordinance   is   to   require   that   non-resident   owners   of   one-   and   two-  dwelling   unit   rental   properties   file   a   statutorily- prescribed   registration   form   with   the   Township  Clerk   simultaneously   with   the   filing   of   the   registration   form   prescribed   by   the   Township   for   non-  resident owners of real property within the Township .  II.   THE   TITLE   OF   CHAPTER   4,   SECTION   31   SHALL   BE   AND   HEREBY   IS   AMENDED  AND SUPERERSEDED TO READ AS FOLLOWS :  “ Registration of Non-Resident Property Owners. ”  I I I.   CHAPTER   4,   SECTION   31.1   SHALL   BE   AND   HEREBY   IS   AMENDED   AND  SUPERSEDED TO READ AS FOLLOWS :  The   purpose   of   this   section   is   to   obtain   information   and   maintain   a   register   of   the   actual  residential   addresses   for   non-owner   occupied   residential   dwellings   and   commercial  properties.   This   section   is   enacted   pursuant   to   N.J.S.A.   40:48-2.53   and   N.J.S.A. 46:8-  28.  I V .   CHAPTER   4,   SECTION   31.3   SHALL   BE   AND   HEREBY   IS   AMENDED   AND  SUPERSEDED TO READ AS FOLLOWS :  a.   As   provided   herein,   any   Non-Occupant   Owner   of   Real   Property   located   within   the  Township   of   West   Orange   shall   register   said   owner ’ s   street   address   with   the  Township Clerk   by completing the form provided by the Township Clerk .  b.   In   addition   to   filing   the   form   required   by   subsection   a.,   any   Non-Occupant   Owner   of   a  one-   or   two-dwelling   unit   rental   property   shall   also   simultaneously   file   with   the  Township   Clerk   a   certificate   of   registration   on   the   form   prescribed   by   the  Commissioner of Community Affairs pursuant to   N.J.S.A.   46:8-28.  V.   REPEAL OF CONFLICTING ORDINANCES  Any   Ordinances   of   the   Township   in   conflict   with   this   Ordinance   are   herby   repealed   to   the  extent of such conflict.

2  V I .   SEVERABILITY  If   any   part   of   this   Ordinance   shall   be   deemed   invalid,   such   parts   shall   be   severed   and  the invalidity thereof shall not affect the remaining parts of this Ordinance.  V I I.   EFFECTIVE DATE  This   Ordinance   shall   take   effect   upon   final   passage   and   publication   in   accordance   with  the law.  __________________________________  Honorable Susan McCartney  Council President  ___________________________________  Honorable Robert D. Parisi  Mayor  ___________________________________  Karen J. Carnevale  Municipal Clerk  Introduced:   November   12,   2013  Adopted: December 3, 2013

3  2391-13  LEGISLATIVE HISTORY  Section   31   of   Chapter   4   of   the   Revised   General   Ordinances   was   created   by   adoption   of  Ordinance   #1519-98   on   April   7,   1998.   Ordinance   #1519-98   was   adopted   pursuant   to   N.J.S.A.  40:48-2.53,   which   authorizes   a   municipality   to   enact   an   ordinance   requiring   an   owner   of   real  property   situated   within   the   municipality   to   register   with   the   clerk   of   the   municipality   the   street  address   of   the   owner ’ s   residence   when   the   owner   does   not   reside   at   the   real   property.   The  registration   form   prescribed   by   the   Township   in   connection   with   Section   4-31   of   the   Revised  General   Ordinances   (the   “ Township   Form ” )   calls   for   three   (3)   general   categories   of   information:  (1)   the   name,   address,   and   contact   information   of   the   non-resident   owner;   (2)   the   address   of   the  West   Orange   property   owned   by   the   non-resident   owner;   and   (3)   the   current   use   of   the   West  Orange   property   owned   by   the   non-resident   owner   –   e.g.,   industrial,   single   family   home,   or  commercial .  In   the   more   specific   context   of   non-resident   owners   of   one-   and   two-dwelling   unit   rental  properties,   N.J.S.A.   46:8-28   requires   such   non-resident   owners   to   file   with   the   municipal   clerk   a  certificate   of   registration   on   a   form   prescribed   by   the   Commissioner   of   Community   Affairs   (the  “ State   Form ” ).   The   State   Form   call s   for   information   above   and   beyond   the   three   (3)   general  categories   of   information   called   for   by   the   Township   Form   –   e.g.,   name   and   address   of   the  managing   agent   of   the   property,   name   and   address   of   every   holder   of   a   recorded   mortgage   on  the property, and name and address of any fuel oil dealer servicing the property.  This   Ordinance   amends   Section   4-31   of   the   Revised   General   Ordinances   in   order   to  comply   with   the   requirements   of   N.J.S.A.   46:8-28   as   well   as   N.J.S.A.   40:48-2.53.   All   non-  resident   owners   of   real   property   within   the   Township   will   continue   to   be   required   to   file   the  Township   Form   with   the   Township   Clerk,   and   non-resident   owners   of   one-   and   two-dwelling   unit  rental   properties   will   be   required   to   file   the   State   Form   simultaneously   with   the   filing   of   the  Township   Form.   In   addition   to   the   benefit   of   compliance   with   State   regulations,   the   Ordinance

4  will   further   promote   the   purpose   underlying   the   adoption   of   Ordinance   #1519-98,   which   is   to  enhance   the   Township ’ s   ability   to   respond   to   and   address   emergent   issues   relating   to   non-  owner-occupied rental properties within the Township.

2392-13  AN ORDINANCE ESTABLISHING   THE   NOVEMBER GENERAL ELECTION DAY  AS THE DATE FOR THE TOWNSHIP OF WEST ORANGE ’ S  NONPARTISAN MUNICIPAL ELECTIONS  WHEREAS ,   nonpartisan   municipal   elections   in   the   Township   of   West   Orange   ( “ Township ” )   are  currently held on the second Tuesday during the month of May; and  WHEREAS ,   pursuant   to   N.J.S.A.   40:45-7.1,   the   New   Jersey   Legislature   has   amended   the  applicable   statutes   to   permit   municipalities   to   hold   nonpartisan   municipal   elections   on   the   same   day  as the general elections in the month of November; and  WHEREAS ,   it   will   be   more   efficient,   cost-effective,   improve   voter   turnout,   and   be   in   the   best  interests   of   the   Township   and   its   citizens   to   hold   municipal   elections   in   the   month   of   November   at   the  same time as the General Election ;  NOW,   THEREFORE,   BE   IT   ORDAINED   BY   THE   TOWNSHIP   COUNCIL   OF   THE   TOWNSHIP  OF WEST ORANGE , in the County of Essex and State of New Jersey, as follows:  SECTION   ONE :   In   accordance   with   the   Uniform   Nonpartisan   Elections   Law,   N.J.S.A.   40:45-5,  et   seq. ,   regular   municipal   elections   in   the   Township   of   West   Orange   shall   be   conducted   on   the   day   of  the   G eneral   E lection, specifically the Tuesday after the first Monday in November.  SECTION   TWO :   Pursuant   to   the   Uniform   Nonpartisan   Elections   Law,   the   term   of   any   person  in   office   on   the   date   of   adoption   of   this   Ordinance   shall   be   extended   until   the   beginning   of   the   term   of  the person elected to that office on the day of the   G eneral   E lection.  SECTION   THREE :   The   term   of   any   officer   elected   in   the   November   G eneral   E lection   shall  begin on the first day of January subsequent to the   G eneral   E lection.  SECTION   FOUR :   Any   Ordinances   of   the   Township   which   conflict   with   this   Ordinance   are  hereby repealed to the extent of such conflict.  SECTION   FIVE :   If   any   part   of   this   Ordinance   shall   be   deemed   invalid,   such   parts   shall   be  severed and the invalidity thereof shall not affect the remaining parts of this Ordinance.

2  SECTION   SIX :   The   Ordinance   shall   take   effect   upon   final   passage   and   publication   in  accordance with the law.  __________________________________  Honorable Susan McCartney  Council President  ___________________________________  Honorable Robert D. Parisi  Mayor  _________________________________________  Karen J. Carnevale, R.M.C.  Municipal Clerk  Introduced: November 12, 2013  Adopted: December 3, 2013  809546_1

3  2392-13  LEGISLATIVE HISTORY  This   Ordinance   is   intended   to   employ   a   mechanism   authorized   by   the   State   L egislature   for   streamlining   the  holding   of   elections   in   municipalities   in   the   State   of   New   Jersey.   Specifically,   i n   January   2010,   the   New   Jersey   Legislature  amended   the   Uniform   Nonpartisan   Elections   Law,   N.J.S.A.   40:45-5,   et   seq.   (the   “ UNEL ” ).   Among   other   things,   the  amendments   to   the   UNEL   authorized   municipalities   which   hold   nonpartisan   municipal   elections   in   May   to   choose   to   hold  regular   municipal   elections   on   the   day   of   the   G eneral   E lection,   the   Tuesday   after   the   first   Monday   in   November.  Specifically,   N.J.S.A.   40:45-7.1(a)   explicitly   authorizes   municipalities   to   adopt   this   format   for   its   regular   municipal elections.  The adoption of this   Ordinance   formally   adopts   the mechanism created by the Legislature.  To   that   end,   t his   legislation   is   intended   to   promote   economy,   efficiency   and   cost   savings   in   the   administration   of  municipal   elections.   T he   Township   budgets   approximately   $80,000   for   each   regular   municipal   election   held   in   May.   In   the  past,   the   Township   has   spent   close   to   all   of   that   amount   on,   among   other   things,   poll-workers,   mailing   and   printing   of  sample   ballots,   polling   place   fees,   required   advertisements,   and   delivery   of   voting   machines.   These   costs   often   increase  from   election   to   election.   Consolidating   the   regular   municipal   election   with   the   General   Election   will   defray   much   of   these  costs.  Furthermore,   adoption   of   this   Ordinance   is   intended   to   promote   and   enhance   public   safety.   Because   municipal  elections   are   currently   held   separate   and   apart   from   the   General   Election,   the   Township ’ s   public   schools   and   other   such  polling   places   remain   open   to   all   visitors   and   the   public   at   large   for   one   (1)   extra   day   each   year .   Consolidation   of   the  elections   will   mean   that   all   polling   places,   and   more   specifically   schools,   and   the   children,   teachers   and   administrators   in  them,   will   be   accessible   to   the   public   at   large   for   one   (1)   fewer   day   per   year.   Also,   in   presidential   election   years,   public  schools are closed and there will be no dates on which the public at large is authorized to access the schools.  T his   legislation   is   also   intended   to   assist   in   improving   voter   turnout,   which   has   historically   been   lower   for   regular  municipal   elections   than   General   Elections .   Finally,   i t   must   be   noted   that,   pursuant   to   N.J.S.A.   40:45-7.1(b),   adopting   this  Ordinance   means   the   Township   cannot   change   the   timing   of   the   regular   municipal   elections   back   to   the   month   of   May  until:   (i)   at   least   ten   (10)   years   have   passed   since   the   adoption   of   this   Ordinance;   and   (ii)   a   new   ordinance   providing   for  elections to occur on the second Tuesday in May has been adopted by the governing body.

#2014164 v1  100181-33129  2393-13  REFUNDING   BOND   ORDINANCE   PROVIDING   FOR  PAYMENT   OF   AMOUNTS   OWING   TO   OTHERS   FOR  TAXES   LEVIED   IN   AND   BY   THE   TOWNSHIP   OF   WEST  ORANGE ,   IN   THE   COUNTY   OF   ESSEX ,   NEW   JERSEY,  APPROPRIATING   $ 3,350,000   THEREFOR   AND  AUTHORIZING   THE   ISSUANCE   OF   $ 3,350,000   BONDS   OR  NOTES   OF   THE   TOWNSHIP   FOR   FINANCING   THE   COST  THEREOF.  BE   IT   ORDAINED   BY   THE   TOWNSHIP   COUNCIL   OF   THE   TOWNSHIP   OF   WEST  ORANGE ,   IN   THE   COUNTY   OF   ESSEX ,   NEW   JERSEY   (not   less   than   two-thirds   of   a ll  members thereof affirmatively concurring) AS FOLLOWS:  Section   1.   The   Township   of   West   Orange ,   New   Jersey   (the   “ Township ” )   is   hereby  authorized   to   pay   an   aggregate   amount   not   exceeding   $ 3,102,362.17   for   amounts   owed   by   the  Township   to   the   owners   of   various   properties   for   taxes   levied   in   the   Township  ,   as   set   forth   in   a  schedule   on   file   with   the   T ax   Collector   (the   “ Schedule ” ) ,   pursuant   to   the   Local   Bond   Law   of   the  State   of   New   Jersey,   constituting   Chapter   2   of   Title   40A   of   the   New   Jersey   Statutes,   as   amended  and supplemented (the   “ Local Bond Law ” ) .  Section   2.   An   aggregate   amount   not   exceeding   $ 247,637  .  83   for   items   of   expense   listed  in   and   permitted   under   N.J.S.A.   40A:2-51(b)   has   been   included   in   the   aggregate   principal  amount of refunding bonds authorized herein.  Section   3.   In   order   to   finance   the   cost   of   the   project   described   in   Section   1   hereof,  negotiable   refunding   bonds   are   hereby   authorized   to   be   issued   in   the   principal   amount   not  exceeding $ 3,350,000   pursuant to the Local Bond Law.  Section   4.   In   anticipation   of   the   issuance   of   the   refunding   bonds,   negotiable   bond  anticipation   notes   are   hereby   authorized   to   be   issued   pursuant   to   and   within   the   limitations  prescribed   by   the   Local   Bond   Law.   All   refunding   bond   anticipation   notes   issued   hereunder   shall  mature   at   such   times   as   may   be   determined   by   the   Chief   Financial   Officer ,   provided   that   no   note  shall   mature   later   than   one   year   from   its   date.   The   notes   shall   bear   interest   at   such   rate   or   rates  and   be   in   such   form   as   may   be   determined   by   the   Chief   Financial   Officer .   The   Chief   Financial  Officer   shall   determine   all   matters   in   connection   with   notes   issued   pursuant   to   this   ordinance,  and   the   Chief   Financial   Officer ’ s   signature   upon   the   notes   shall   be   conclusive   evidence   as   to   all  such determinations.  All   notes   issued   hereunder   may   be   renewed   from   time   to   time,   but   all   such   notes  including   renewals   shall   mature   and   be   paid   no   later   than   the   seventh   anniversary   of   the   date   of  the   original   notes;   provided,   however,   that   no   notes   shall   be   renewed   beyond   the   first   or   any  succeeding   anniversary   date   of   the   original   notes   unless   an   amount   of   such   notes,   at   least   equal  to   the   first   legally   payable   installment   of   the   bonds   in   anticipation   of   which   the   notes   are   issued,  determined   in   accordance   with   the   maturity   schedule   for   the   bonds   approved   by   the   Local

#2014164 v1  100181-33129  Finance   Board,   is   paid   and   retired   on   or   before   such   anniversary   date;   and   provided,   further,   that  the   period   during   which   the   bond   anticipation   notes   and   any   renewals   thereof   and   any   permanent  bonds   ore   outstanding,   shall   not   exceed   the   period   set   for   the   maturity   of   the   bonds   by   the   Local  Finance Board.  The   Chief   Financial   Officer   is   hereby   authorized   to   sell   part   or   all   of   the   notes   from   time  to   time   at   public   or   private   sale   and   to   deliver   them   to   the   purchasers   thereof   upon   receipt   of  payment   of   the   purchase   price   plus   accrued   interest   from   their   dates   to   the   date   of   delivery  thereof.   The   Chief   Financial   Officer   is   directed   to   report   in   writing   to   the   governing   body   at   the  meeting   next   succeeding   the   date   when   any   sale   or   delivery   of   the   notes   pursuant   to   this  ordinance   is   made.   Such   report   must   include   the   amount,   the   description,   the   interest   rate   and  the maturity schedule of the notes sold, the price obtained and the name of the purchaser.  Section   5.   The   Supplemental   Debt   Statement   required   by   the   Local   Bond   Law   has   been  duly   prepared   and   filed   in   the   office   of   the   Clerk   of   the   Township   and   a   complete   executed  duplicate   thereof   has   been   filed   in   the   office   of   the   Director   of   the   Division   of   Local   Government  Services.   Such   statement   shows   that   the   gross   debt   of   the   Township   as   defined   in   the   Local  Bond   Law   is   increased   by   the   authorization   of   the   debt   provided   in   this   bond   ordinance   by  $  3,350,000  .   The   obligations   authorized   herein   will   be   within   all   debt   limitations   prescribed   by  the Local Bond Law.  Section   6  .   A   certified   copy   of   this   refunding   bond   ordinance   as   adopted   on   first   reading  has   been   filed   with   the   Director   of   the   Division   of   Local   Government   Services   in   the   Department  of   Community   Affairs   of   the   State   of   New   Jersey   prior   to   final   adoption,   together   with   a  complete   statement   in   the   form   prescribed   by   the   Director   of   the   Division   of   Local   Government  Services   and   signed   by   the   Chief   Financial   Officer   of   the   Township   as   to   the   indebtedness   to   be  financed by the issuance of the refunding bonds authorized herein.  Section   7  .   The   full   faith   and   credit   of   the   Township   are   hereby   pledged   to   the   punctual  payment   of   the   principal   of   and   interest   on   the   obligations   authorized   by   this   bond   ordinance.  The   obligations   shall   be   direct,   unlimited   obligations   of   the   Township  ,   and   the   Township   shall   be  obligated   to   levy   ad   valorem   taxes   upon   all   the   taxable   property   within   the   Township   for   the  payment of the obligations and the interest thereon without limitation of rate or amount.  Section   8  .   This   b  ond   o  rdinance   constitutes   a   declaration   of   official   intent   under   Treasury  Regulation   Section   1.150-2.   The   Township   reasonably   expects   to   pay   expenditures   with   respect  to   the   tax   amounts   owing   as   set   forth   on   the   Schedule   prior   to   the   date   that   Township   incurs   debt  obligations   under   this   b  ond   o  rdinance.   The   Township   reasonably   expects   to   reimburse   such  expenditures   with   the   proceeds   of   debt   to   be   incurred   by   the   Township   under   this   Bond  Ordinance.   The   maximum   principal   amount   of   debt   expected   to   be   issued   for   payment   of   the  costs of   tax amounts owing   as set forth on the Schedule   is $ 3,350,000 .  Section   9  .   This   bond   ordinance   shall   take   effect   20   days   after   the   first   publication   thereof  after   final   adoption,   as   provided   by   the   Local   Bond   Law,   provided   that   the   consent   of   the   Local  Finance Board has been endorsed upon a certified copy of this ordinance as finally adopted.

2394-13  AN ORDINANCE AMENDING AND SUPPLEMENTING  CHAPTER III, SECTION 3-3, OF THE REVISED  GENERAL ORDINANCES OF THE TOWNSHIP OF WEST  ORANGE ENTITLED   “ RULES OF COUNCIL ” ,  “ MEETINGS ” , AND SPECIFICALLY SECTION 3-3.1,  “ REGULAR MEETINGS ”  BE IT ORDAINED BY THE TOWNSHIP COUNCIL OF THE TOWNSHIP OF  WEST ORANGE , in the County of Essex, State of New Jersey, that Chapter III, Section 3-3, of  the Revised General Ordinances of the Township of West Orange, entitled   “ Rules of Council ” ,  “ Meetings ” , and specifically Section 3-3.1,   “ Regular Meetings ” , shall be amended and  supplemented as follows:  Section 1.   The Revised General Ordinances of the Township of West Orange are amended  and supplemented to read as follows:  CHAPTER   III RULES OF COUNCIL  3-3   MEETINGS.  3-3.1   REGULAR MEETINGS.  The Township Council shall hold regular meetings commencing at 7:00 p.m., on  two Tuesdays of each month of the year, each meeting to be separated from the  immediately preceding and succeeding meeting by at least two weeks, except that  during the months of July and August there shall only be one (1) regular meeting  a month, also on a Tuesday and commencing at 7:00 p.m., and separated by at  least two weeks from the preceding and following meetings.   The Council shall  determine by majority vote of its membership, and by no later than the first  meeting in November of the preceding year, the following year ’ s schedule of  regular Council meetings, after reviewing the following year ’ s calendar for  conflicts with holidays, special events and anticipated vacation schedules.  Whenever the day fixed for any regular meeting falls upon a legal or national  holiday the meeting shall be held at the same hour on the next succeeding day  which is not a legal or national holiday. The Council may alter its meeting  schedule, including cancellation of, or rescheduling of, any regular Council  meeting, by majority vote of its membership and providing sufficient time to  provide lawful notice to the public of the change of schedule. In an emergency,  including, but not limited to, a power outage at Town Hall, a severe storm, or  other occurrence implicating substantial public safety concerns, the Mayor,  Business Administrator, or Council President, may summarily cancel a scheduled  Council meeting, notifying all Council members and the public by any available  and appropriate means and with reasonable promptness under all of the  circumstances. (1972 Code § 3-3; Ord. No. 1895-03 § II).

Section 2.   The remainder of Chapter III remains unchanged.  Section 3.   If any part of this Ordinance is determined to be invalid, such part shall be  severed and its invalidity shall not affect the remaining parts of this Chapter.  Section 4.   Any   and   all   parts   of   ordinances   which   are   inconsistent   with   any   of   the   terms   and  provisions   of   this   Ordinance   shall   be   and   the   same   are   hereby   repealed   as   to   and   to   the   extent   of  such inconsistency.  Section 5.   This Ordinance shall take effect upon final passage and publication as required by  law.  Susan McCartney, Council President  Robert D. Parisi, Mayor  Karen Carnevale, Municipal Clerk  Approved as to form and legality on the basis of the facts provided.  Introduction :   November 12, 2013  Adopted : December 3, 2013  2394-13

LEGISLATIVE HISTORY  This Ordinance amends the previous version of the Rules of the Council regarding its  Regular meeting schedule, setting forth the anticipated minimum schedule, the factors to be  considered in creating the schedule, and providing the timing of and manner in which the  following year ’ s meeting schedule is to be determined. It also sets forth procedures for  cancellation of any meeting after the schedule is in place.

2396-13  LEGISLATIVE HISTORY  This Ordinance   adds bus stops along Pleasant Valley Way from Eagle Rock Avenue to the  Verona line. This section of Pleasant Valley Way is served by NJ Transit Bus No. 29 Express and  DeCamp Bus Line No. 66 Express.

L EGISLATIVE HISTORY  This Ordinance is in response to a request from a handicapped resident who resides at  22 Dean Street.   By adopting this Ordinance, the Township will establish a handicapped parking  stall adjacent to this address. Such parking stalls are permissible under the laws of New Jersey,  and they are designated as Restricted Parking Zones.

pages.text: 209-13  November 12, 2013  RESOLUTION  WHEREAS, for many years the Rock Spring Club ( “ the Club ” ), located at 90 Rock  Spring Road, West Orange, NJ 07052, has arranged for a small helicopter to land on the grounds  of the Club to deliver Santa Claus to Club members and their guests in attendance for a holiday  celebration; and  WHEREAS, this year ’ s annual Santa Claus event is scheduled for Sunday, December 15,  2013; and  WHEREAS, Santa ’ s reindeer, Rudolph, et als., are not available for the trip at this time of  year as they must be well-rested in preparation for their big Christmas Eve Gala event; and  WHEREAS, Santa Claus has again graciously agreed to make arrangements to travel by  small helicopter to attend the Club ’ s annual holiday celebration; and  WHEREAS, the New Jersey Department of Transportation, Division of Aeronautics,  Inspection & Aircraft Operation, not wanting to be Scrooge-like and instilled with the spirit of  Christmas, does permit and has in the past permitted helicopter landings such as these on country  club property when approved pursuant to its application process; and  WHEREAS, the Club, or someone on its behalf, will prepare the required Application for  Temporary Aeronautical Facility License for submission to the New Jersey Department of  Transportation, and which must be accompanied by, among other documents and things which  the Club will provide, a   “ letter, statement, or certificate from the appropriate governing body  which states there is no objection to the issuance of a temporary license (mandatory for all  temps) ” ; and  WHEREAS, for this event, the appropriate governing body is the Township Council of  the Township of West Orange; and  WHEREAS, in order to accommodate the Club, and relying upon the Club ’ s experience  with this tradition and its assurance that all appropriate security and safety precautions will be  taken, the Council President and Members wish to approve and adopt this Resolution in order  that Rock Spring Club can include it as an attachment to its application as required and continue  this holiday tradition;  NOW, THEREFOR, BE IT RESOLVED, that the West Orange Township Council, the  governing body for the Township of West Orange, New Jersey, does and hereby states that it has  no objection to the issuance of a Temporary Aeronautical Facility License to the Rock Spring  Club, located at 90 Rock Spring Road, West Orange, NJ 07052, for the purpose of permitting  transportation and delivery of Santa Claus to the Club at approximately 2:30 p.m., on Sunday,  December 15, 2013, for participation in the Club ’ s traditional Christmas celebration.  Sus an McCartney, Council President   Karen   J.   Carnevale, Municipal Clerk  Adopted: November 12, 2013

#2010959 v1  100181-55389  211-13  November 12, 2013  RESOLUTION OF THE   TOWNSHIP OF   WEST ORANGE   AUTHORIZING THE  APPLICATION   FOR REFUNDING BOND ORDINANCE TO BE FILED  WITH THE LOCAL FINANCE BOARD   PURSUANT TO N.J.S.A. 40A:2-51  WHEREAS,   The   Township   of   West   Orange   desires   to   provide   funds   in   the   amount   not   to  exceed   $ 3,500,000.00   to   finance   the   cost   of   tax   appeal   refunding   notes   (hereinafter   called  “ purpose ” ), a purpose for which obligations are permitted by the Local Bond Law, and  WHEREAS,   pursuant   to   N.J.S. A.   40A:2-51,   obligations   to   finance   said   purpose   may   be  issued,   with   the   consent   of   the   Local   Finance   Board   in   the   Division   of   Local   Government  Services ,   in   the   Department   of   Community   Affairs,   which   consent   is   to   be   endorsed   upon   a  certified   copy   as   passed   on   first   reading   of   the   ordinance   authorizing   such   obligations,   if   said  Board is satisfied, and makes the findings required, and  WHEREAS,   The   Township   Council   of   the   Township   of   West   Orange   believing   that   said  proposed   ordinance   authorizes   obligations   for   a   purpose   for   which   obligations   are   permitted   by  the   Local   Bond   Law   and   that   the   Township ’ s   application   to   the   Local   Finance   Board   for   its  consent shows:  (a)   it is in the public interest to accomplish such purposes,  (b)   said   purposes   or   improvements   are   for   the   health,   welfare,   convenience   or  betterment of the inhabitants of the Township,  (c)   the   amounts   to   be   expended   for   said   purposes   or   improvements   are   not  unreasonable or exorbitant,  (d)   the   proposal   is   an   efficient   and   feasible   means   of   providing   services   for   the   needs  of   the   inhabitants   of   the   Township   and   such   purposes   or   improvements   will   not   cause   any   undue  financial burden to be placed upon the Township,  NOW,   THEREFORE,   BE   IT   RESOLVED   by   the   Township   Council   of   the   Township   of  West Orange   as follows:  Section   1.   Application   to   the   Local   Finance   Board   for   consideration   by   said   Board   of   the  Towns hip's   proposed   tax   appeal   refunding   is   hereby   approved   and   Bond   Counsel,   along   with   the  other   representatives   of   the   Township,   are   hereby   authorized   to   prepare   such   application   and   to  represent the Township in the matters pertaining thereto.  Section   2.   The   Clerk   of   the   Township   Council   of   the   Township   of   West   Orange   be   and  she   is   hereby   directed   to   prepare   and   file   a   certified   copy   of   said   proposed   ordinance   after  passage on first reading with the Local Finance Board as a part of such application.

214 -13  November 12 , 2013  RESOLUTION TO HOLD A CLOSED SESSION  WHEREAS , the Open Public Meetings Act (N.J.S.A. 10:4. et seq.) provides for the exclusion of  the public meetings of the governing body during the discussion of certain matters; and  WHEREAS , prior to the exclusion of the public from a meeting of the Township Council it is  required that the Council adopt a Resolution stating the general nature of the subject to be discussed and  stating as precisely as possible the time when the minutes of the discussion conducted in closed session   can  be disclosed to the public; and  WHERE AS , this body is about to consider a matter which falls within the purview of N.J.S.A. 10  -4-12, and can properly exclude the public from such discussions; now, therefore, be it  BE IT   RESOLVED , that the Township Council of the Township of West Orange, now assembled  in public session   at   its regular meeting of   November 12, 2013   shall enter into a closed session to consider  the following matter(s).  ( )   1.   Discussion of any material, the disclosure of which constitutes an  invasion of individual privacy..  (   )   2 .   A collective bargaining agreement or the terms of provisions of same.  ( )   3.   Techniques or tactic utilized to protect the safety and property of the public provided that  their disclosure could impair such protection.  ( )   4.   An investigation of violations o r   possible violations of the laws of the State of New  Jersey   and/ or   the Township of West Orange Administrative Code.  ( X )   5.   Pending or anticipated litigation or contract negotiation in which this body is   or maybe a  party.  ( )   6.   A matter falling within the attorney-client privilege to the extent that confidentiality is  r equired in order for the attorney to   exercise his ethical duties to this body.  ()   7.   Personnel matters.  (   )   8.   Discussion of any matter which by express provision of Federal law or State statute or  rule of Court shall be rendered confidential.  ( )   9.   Discussion   of any matter in which the release of information would impair   a right to  receive funds from the Government of the United Sates.  ( )   10 .   Discussion of any matter involving the purchase, lease or acquisition   of   real property  with public funds, the setting of   banking rates or investment of public funds, where it  could adversely affect the public interest if discussion of such matters were disclosed.  ( )   11.   Deliberations occurring after a public hearing that may result in imposition of a civil  penalty or suspension or loss of a license or permit.

More specifically, the Council   may   discuss in closed session the following topic ( s ) :  Potential Matter of Litigation  Township of West Orange v. Link Communications, Ltd.  (the general subject matter ( s ) ) of discussion shall be indicated by a check mark in the appropriate box and  shall be more specifically referred to thereafter to the extent suc h   additional disclosure can be made without  compromising the need for confidentiality giving rise to the closed session.  IT IS FURTH E R RESOLVED , that the minutes of said discussion shall be made public as  soon as the matter under discussion is no longer of a confidential or sensitive nature such that the public  interest will no longer be served by such confidentiality.  The foregoing Resolution was duly adopted by the Township Council of the Township of West Orange at a  public meeting held on   November 12, 2013  Motion:   Victor Cirilo  Second:   Jerry Guarino  Vote  Aye:   5  Opposed:   0  Karen J. Carnevale   Susan McCartney  M unicipal Clerk   Council President  Adopted:   November 12, 2013

215-13  November 12, 2013  RESOLUTION  WHEREAS,   the   Township   of   West   Orange   ( “ Township ” )   is   currently   a   plaintiff   to   certain  litigation   captioned   Township   of   West   Orange   v.   Link   Communications,   Ltd. ,   Docket   No.   L-9667-  10   (the   “ Litigation ” ),   which   is   pending   before   the   Superior   Court   of   New   Jersey,   Law   Division,  Essex County; and  WHEREAS ,   the   Litigation   involves   the   Township ’ s   claims   against   Defendant   Link  Communications,   Ltd.   ( “ Defendant ” )   arising   out   of   the   Township ’ s   purchase   of   certain   mobile  computer   and   video   equipment   for   the   Township ’ s   police   vehicles,   and   Defendant ’ s   installation  thereof; and  WHEREAS ,   the   Township   asserts   in   the   Litigation   that   Defendant ’ s   installation   and  maintenance   of   certain   mobile   computer   and   video   equipment   in   Township   police   vehicles   was  faulty; and  WHEREAS ,   the   Township   Attorney ’ s   office   represents   the   Township   in   connection   with  the Litigation; and  WHEREAS,   pursuant   to   Resolution   No.   121-12,   the   Township   Council   authorized   the  retention   of   Computer   Design   and   Services,   Inc.   ( “ CDS ” )   for   thirty   (30)   hours   at   a   cost   of   $3,000  in order to prepare an expert report in connection with the Litigation; and  WHEREAS,   pursuant   to   the   Township ’ s   retention   of   CDS ,   Thomas   J.   Summers   ( “ Mr.  Summers ” ),   employed   by   CDS   as   a   Senior   Systems   Administrator   and   Technology   Director   at  the   time ,   examined   Defendant ’ s   equipment   and   the   Township ’ s   police   vehicles   and   prepared   an  expert report in connection with the Litigation; and  WHEREAS,   pursuant   to   Resolution   No.   174-13,   the   Township   Council   authorized   the  retention   of   CDS   for   fifteen   (15)   hours   at   a   cost   of   $1,500   for   CDS   to   prepare   for   and   testify   at  trial; and

WHEREAS,   on   September   24,   2013,   Mr.   Summers   notified   the   Township   Attorney ’ s  office   for   the   first   time   that   he   left   the   employ   of   CDS   and   would   not   serve   as   the   Township ’ s  expert in connection with the Litigation ; and  WHEREAS,   annexed   hereto   as   Attachment   “ A ”   is   a   copy   of   a   letter   from   CDS ’ s  President   to   the   Township   Attorney ’ s   office   confirming   Mr.   Summers ’ s   refus al   to   participate   as  an expert at trial; and  WHEREAS,   the   Township   Attorney ’ s   office   requires   retention   of   a   new   expert   with  technical   computer   experience   to   assess   the   installation   and   operation   of   Defendant ’ s  equipment   and prepare an expert report in connection with the Litigation ; and  WHEREAS ,   CDS,   with   its   new   Senior   Systems   Administrator   and   Technology   Director  Vincent   Abrusia,   possesses   the   technical   computer   expertise   required   by   the   Township   to   assist  in evaluating the   claims in the Litigation; and  WHEREAS,   CDS   has   offered   a   discounted   rate   of   $100   per   hour   in   connection   with   its  services preparing an expert report; and  WHEREAS ,   CDS ’ s   services   in   connection   with   preparation   of   this   expert   report   will   not  exceed $1, 5 00 ;   and  NOW,   BE   IT   HEREBY   RESOLVED ,   by   the   Township   Council   of   the   Township   of   West  Orange   that   the   Township   Attorney   is   hereby   authorized   to   engage   CDS   in   connection   with   the  Litigation   on the terms and conditions set forth herein ;   and be it further  RESOLVED , that   $ 1 , 5 00   shall be allocated for CDS ’ s engagement .  ________________________   _________________________  Karen J. 2391 -13  AN   ORDINANCE   AMENDING   CHAPTER   4,   SECTION   31   OF   THE   REVISED   GENERAL  ORDINANCES   OF   THE   TOWNSHIP   OF   WEST   ORANGE,   ENTITLED   “ REGISTRATION   OF  STREET ADDRESSES OF NONRESIDENT PROPERTY OWNERS ”  BE   IT   ORDAINED   BY   THE   TOWNSHIP   COUNCIL   OF   THE   TOWNSHIP   OF   WEST  ORANGE ,   in   the   County   of   Essex   and   State   of   New   Jersey,   that   Chapter   4,   Section   31   of   the  Revised   General   Ordinances   of   the   Township   of   West   Orange   (the   “ Revised   General  Ordinances ” ) is hereby amended as follows:  I.   PURPOSE  The   purpose   of   this   Ordinance   is   to   require   that   non-resident   owners   of   one-   and   two-  dwelling   unit   rental   properties   file   a   statutorily- prescribed   registration   form   with   the   Township  Clerk   simultaneously   with   the   filing   of   the   registration   form   prescribed   by   the   Township   for   non-  resident owners of real property within the Township .  II.   THE   TITLE   OF   CHAPTER   4,   SECTION   31   SHALL   BE   AND   HEREBY   IS   AMENDED  AND SUPERERSEDED TO READ AS FOLLOWS :  “ Registration of Non-Resident Property Owners. ”  I I I.   CHAPTER   4,   SECTION   31.1   SHALL   BE   AND   HEREBY   IS   AMENDED   AND  SUPERSEDED TO READ AS FOLLOWS :  The   purpose   of   this   section   is   to   obtain   information   and   maintain   a   register   of   the   actual  residential   addresses   for   non-owner   occupied   residential   dwellings   and   commercial  properties.   This   section   is   enacted   pursuant   to   N.J.S.A.   40:48-2.53   and   N.J.S.A. 46:8-  28.  I V .   CHAPTER   4,   SECTION   31.3   SHALL   BE   AND   HEREBY   IS   AMENDED   AND  SUPERSEDED TO READ AS FOLLOWS :  a.   As   provided   herein,   any   Non-Occupant   Owner   of   Real   Property   located   within   the  Township   of   West   Orange   shall   register   said   owner ’ s   street   address   with   the  Township Clerk   by completing the form provided by the Township Clerk .  b.   In   addition   to   filing   the   form   required   by   subsection   a.,   any   Non-Occupant   Owner   of   a  one-   or   two-dwelling   unit   rental   property   shall   also   simultaneously   file   with   the  Township   Clerk   a   certificate   of   registration   on   the   form   prescribed   by   the  Commissioner of Community Affairs pursuant to   N.J.S.A.   46:8-28.  V.   REPEAL OF CONFLICTING ORDINANCES  Any   Ordinances   of   the   Township   in   conflict   with   this   Ordinance   are   herby   repealed   to   the  extent of such conflict.

2  V I .   SEVERABILITY  If   any   part   of   this   Ordinance   shall   be   deemed   invalid,   such   parts   shall   be   severed   and  the invalidity thereof shall not affect the remaining parts of this Ordinance.  V I I.   EFFECTIVE DATE  This   Ordinance   shall   take   effect   upon   final   passage   and   publication   in   accordance   with  the law.  __________________________________  Honorable Susan McCartney  Council President  ___________________________________  Honorable Robert D. Parisi  Mayor  ___________________________________  Karen J. Carnevale  Municipal Clerk  Introduced:   November   12,   2013  Adopted: December 3, 2013

3  2391-13  LEGISLATIVE HISTORY  Section   31   of   Chapter   4   of   the   Revised   General   Ordinances   was   created   by   adoption   of  Ordinance   #1519-98   on   April   7,   1998.   Ordinance   #1519-98   was   adopted   pursuant   to   N.J.S.A.  40:48-2.53,   which   authorizes   a   municipality   to   enact   an   ordinance   requiring   an   owner   of   real  property   situated   within   the   municipality   to   register   with   the   clerk   of   the   municipality   the   street  address   of   the   owner ’ s   residence   when   the   owner   does   not   reside   at   the   real   property.   The  registration   form   prescribed   by   the   Township   in   connection   with   Section   4-31   of   the   Revised  General   Ordinances   (the   “ Township   Form ” )   calls   for   three   (3)   general   categories   of   information:  (1)   the   name,   address,   and   contact   information   of   the   non-resident   owner;   (2)   the   address   of   the  West   Orange   property   owned   by   the   non-resident   owner;   and   (3)   the   current   use   of   the   West  Orange   property   owned   by   the   non-resident   owner   –   e.g.,   industrial,   single   family   home,   or  commercial .  In   the   more   specific   context   of   non-resident   owners   of   one-   and   two-dwelling   unit   rental  properties,   N.J.S.A.   46:8-28   requires   such   non-resident   owners   to   file   with   the   municipal   clerk   a  certificate   of   registration   on   a   form   prescribed   by   the   Commissioner   of   Community   Affairs   (the  “ State   Form ” ).   The   State   Form   call s   for   information   above   and   beyond   the   three   (3)   general  categories   of   information   called   for   by   the   Township   Form   –   e.g.,   name   and   address   of   the  managing   agent   of   the   property,   name   and   address   of   every   holder   of   a   recorded   mortgage   on  the property, and name and address of any fuel oil dealer servicing the property.  This   Ordinance   amends   Section   4-31   of   the   Revised   General   Ordinances   in   order   to  comply   with   the   requirements   of   N.J.S.A.   46:8-28   as   well   as   N.J.S.A.   40:48-2.53.   All   non-  resident   owners   of   real   property   within   the   Township   will   continue   to   be   required   to   file   the  Township   Form   with   the   Township   Clerk,   and   non-resident   owners   of   one-   and   two-dwelling   unit  rental   properties   will   be   required   to   file   the   State   Form   simultaneously   with   the   filing   of   the  Township   Form.   In   addition   to   the   benefit   of   compliance   with   State   regulations,   the   Ordinance

4  will   further   promote   the   purpose   underlying   the   adoption   of   Ordinance   #1519-98,   which   is   to  enhance   the   Township ’ s   ability   to   respond   to   and   address   emergent   issues   relating   to   non-  owner-occupied rental properties within the Township.

2392-13  AN ORDINANCE ESTABLISHING   THE   NOVEMBER GENERAL ELECTION DAY  AS THE DATE FOR THE TOWNSHIP OF WEST ORANGE ’ S  NONPARTISAN MUNICIPAL ELECTIONS  WHEREAS ,   nonpartisan   municipal   elections   in   the   Township   of   West   Orange   ( “ Township ” )   are  currently held on the second Tuesday during the month of May; and  WHEREAS ,   pursuant   to   N.J.S.A.   40:45-7.1,   the   New   Jersey   Legislature   has   amended   the  applicable   statutes   to   permit   municipalities   to   hold   nonpartisan   municipal   elections   on   the   same   day  as the general elections in the month of November; and  WHEREAS ,   it   will   be   more   efficient,   cost-effective,   improve   voter   turnout,   and   be   in   the   best  interests   of   the   Township   and   its   citizens   to   hold   municipal   elections   in   the   month   of   November   at   the  same time as the General Election ;  NOW,   THEREFORE,   BE   IT   ORDAINED   BY   THE   TOWNSHIP   COUNCIL   OF   THE   TOWNSHIP  OF WEST ORANGE , in the County of Essex and State of New Jersey, as follows:  SECTION   ONE :   In   accordance   with   the   Uniform   Nonpartisan   Elections   Law,   N.J.S.A.   40:45-5,  et   seq. ,   regular   municipal   elections   in   the   Township   of   West   Orange   shall   be   conducted   on   the   day   of  the   G eneral   E lection, specifically the Tuesday after the first Monday in November.  SECTION   TWO :   Pursuant   to   the   Uniform   Nonpartisan   Elections   Law,   the   term   of   any   person  in   office   on   the   date   of   adoption   of   this   Ordinance   shall   be   extended   until   the   beginning   of   the   term   of  the person elected to that office on the day of the   G eneral   E lection.  SECTION   THREE :   The   term   of   any   officer   elected   in   the   November   G eneral   E lection   shall  begin on the first day of January subsequent to the   G eneral   E lection.  SECTION   FOUR :   Any   Ordinances   of   the   Township   which   conflict   with   this   Ordinance   are  hereby repealed to the extent of such conflict.  SECTION   FIVE :   If   any   part   of   this   Ordinance   shall   be   deemed   invalid,   such   parts   shall   be  severed and the invalidity thereof shall not affect the remaining parts of this Ordinance.

2  SECTION   SIX :   The   Ordinance   shall   take   effect   upon   final   passage   and   publication   in  accordance with the law.  __________________________________  Honorable Susan McCartney  Council President  ___________________________________  Honorable Robert D. Parisi  Mayor  _________________________________________  Karen J. Carnevale, R.M.C.  Municipal Clerk  Introduced: November 12, 2013  Adopted: December 3, 2013  809546_1

3  2392-13  LEGISLATIVE HISTORY  This   Ordinance   is   intended   to   employ   a   mechanism   authorized   by   the   State   L egislature   for   streamlining   the  holding   of   elections   in   municipalities   in   the   State   of   New   Jersey.   Specifically,   i n   January   2010,   the   New   Jersey   Legislature  amended   the   Uniform   Nonpartisan   Elections   Law,   N.J.S.A.   40:45-5,   et   seq.   (the   “ UNEL ” ).   Among   other   things,   the  amendments   to   the   UNEL   authorized   municipalities   which   hold   nonpartisan   municipal   elections   in   May   to   choose   to   hold  regular   municipal   elections   on   the   day   of   the   G eneral   E lection,   the   Tuesday   after   the   first   Monday   in   November.  Specifically,   N.J.S.A.   40:45-7.1(a)   explicitly   authorizes   municipalities   to   adopt   this   format   for   its   regular   municipal elections.  The adoption of this   Ordinance   formally   adopts   the mechanism created by the Legislature.  To   that   end,   t his   legislation   is   intended   to   promote   economy,   efficiency   and   cost   savings   in   the   administration   of  municipal   elections.   T he   Township   budgets   approximately   $80,000   for   each   regular   municipal   election   held   in   May.   In   the  past,   the   Township   has   spent   close   to   all   of   that   amount   on,   among   other   things,   poll-workers,   mailing   and   printing   of  sample   ballots,   polling   place   fees,   required   advertisements,   and   delivery   of   voting   machines.   These   costs   often   increase  from   election   to   election.   Consolidating   the   regular   municipal   election   with   the   General   Election   will   defray   much   of   these  costs.  Furthermore,   adoption   of   this   Ordinance   is   intended   to   promote   and   enhance   public   safety.   Because   municipal  elections   are   currently   held   separate   and   apart   from   the   General   Election,   the   Township ’ s   public   schools   and   other   such  polling   places   remain   open   to   all   visitors   and   the   public   at   large   for   one   (1)   extra   day   each   year .   Consolidation   of   the  elections   will   mean   that   all   polling   places,   and   more   specifically   schools,   and   the   children,   teachers   and   administrators   in  them,   will   be   accessible   to   the   public   at   large   for   one   (1)   fewer   day   per   year.   Also,   in   presidential   election   years,   public  schools are closed and there will be no dates on which the public at large is authorized to access the schools.  T his   legislation   is   also   intended   to   assist   in   improving   voter   turnout,   which   has   historically   been   lower   for   regular  municipal   elections   than   General   Elections .   Finally,   i t   must   be   noted   that,   pursuant   to   N.J.S.A.   40:45-7.1(b),   adopting   this  Ordinance   means   the   Township   cannot   change   the   timing   of   the   regular   municipal   elections   back   to   the   month   of   May  until:   (i)   at   least   ten   (10)   years   have   passed   since   the   adoption   of   this   Ordinance;   and   (ii)   a   new   ordinance   providing   for  elections to occur on the second Tuesday in May has been adopted by the governing body.

#2014164 v1 100181-33129  REFUNDING   BOND   ORDINANCE   PROVIDING   FOR PAYMENT   OF   AMOUNTS   OWING   TO   OTHERS   FOR TAXES LEVIED IN AND BY THE TOWNSHIP OF WEST ORANGE, IN THE COUNTY OF ESSEX, NEW JERSEY, APPROPRIATING   $3,350,000   THEREFOR   AND AUTHORIZING THE ISSUANCE OF $3,350,000 BONDS OR NOTES OF THE TOWNSHIP FOR FINANCING THE COST THEREOF.  BE IT ORDAINED BY THE TOWNSHIP COUNCIL OF THE TOWNSHIP OF WEST ORANGE, IN THE COUNTY OF ESSEX, NEW JERSEY (not less than two-thirds of all members thereof affirmatively concurring) AS FOLLOWS:  Section 1.   The Township of West Orange, New Jersey (the “Township”) is hereby authorized to pay an aggregate amount not exceeding $3,102,362.17 for amounts owed by the Township to the owners of various properties for taxes levied in the Township, as set forth in a schedule on file with the Tax Collector (the “Schedule”), pursuant to the Local Bond Law of the State of New Jersey, constituting Chapter 2 of Title 40A of the New Jersey Statutes, as amended and supplemented (the “Local Bond Law”).  Section 2.   An aggregate amount not exceeding $247,637.83 for items of expense listed in and permitted under N.J.S.A. 40A:2-51(b) has been included in the aggregate principal amount of refunding bonds authorized herein.  Section 3.   In order to finance the cost of the project described in Section 1 hereof, negotiable refunding bonds are hereby authorized to be issued in the principal amount not exceeding $3,350,000 pursuant to the Local Bond Law.  Section 4.   In anticipation of the issuance of the refunding bonds, negotiable bond anticipation notes are hereby authorized to be issued pursuant to and within the limitations prescribed by the Local Bond Law. All refunding bond anticipation notes issued hereunder shall mature at such times as may be determined by the Chief Financial Officer, provided that no note shall mature later than one year from its date.   The notes shall bear interest at such rate or rates and be in such form as may be determined by the Chief Financial Officer.   The Chief Financial Officer shall determine all matters in connection with notes issued pursuant to this ordinance, and the Chief Financial Officer’s signature upon the notes shall be conclusive evidence as to all such determinations.  All notes issued hereunder may be renewed from time to time, but all such notes including renewals shall mature and be paid no later than the seventh anniversary of the date of the original notes; provided, however, that no notes shall be renewed beyond the first or any succeeding anniversary date of the original notes unless an amount of such notes, at least equal to the first legally payable installment of the bonds in anticipation of which the notes are issued, determined in accordance with the maturity schedule for the bonds approved by the Local Finance Board, is paid and retired on or before such anniversary date; and provided, further, that

#2014164 v1 100181-33129  the period during which the bond anticipation notes and any renewals thereof and any permanent bonds ore outstanding, shall not exceed the period set for the maturity of the bonds by the Local Finance Board.  The Chief Financial Officer is hereby authorized to sell part or all of the notes from time to time at public or private sale and to deliver them to the purchasers thereof upon receipt of payment of the purchase price plus accrued interest from their dates to the date of delivery thereof. The Chief Financial Officer is directed to report in writing to the governing body at the meeting next succeeding the date when any sale or delivery of the notes pursuant to this ordinance is made.   Such report must include the amount, the description, the interest rate and the maturity schedule of the notes sold, the price obtained and the name of the purchaser.  Section 5. The Supplemental Debt Statement required by the Local Bond Law has been duly prepared and filed in the office of the Clerk of the Township and a complete executed duplicate thereof has been filed in the office of the Director of the Division of Local Government Services.   Such statement shows that the gross debt of the Township as defined in the Local Bond Law is increased by the authorization of the debt provided in this bond ordinance by $3,350,000.   The obligations authorized herein will be within all debt limitations prescribed by the Local Bond Law.  Section 6. A certified copy of this refunding bond ordinance as adopted on first reading has been filed with the Director of the Division of Local Government Services in the Department of Community Affairs of the State of New Jersey prior to final adoption, together with a complete statement in the form prescribed by the Director of the Division of Local Government Services and signed by the Chief Financial Officer of the Township as to the indebtedness to be financed by the issuance of the refunding bonds authorized herein.  Section 7.   The full faith and credit of the Township are hereby pledged to the punctual payment of the principal of and interest on the obligations authorized by this bond ordinance. The obligations shall be direct, unlimited obligations of the Township, and the Township shall be obligated to levy ad valorem taxes upon all the taxable property within the Township for the payment of the obligations and the interest thereon without limitation of rate or amount.  Section 8. This bond ordinance constitutes a declaration of official intent under Treasury Regulation Section 1.150-2. The Township reasonably expects to pay expenditures with respect to the tax amounts owing as set forth on the Schedule prior to the date that Township incurs debt obligations under this bond ordinance.   The Township reasonably expects to reimburse such expenditures with the proceeds of debt to be incurred by the Township under this Bond Ordinance.   The maximum principal amount of debt expected to be issued for payment of the costs of tax amounts owing as set forth on the Schedule is $3,350,000.  Section 9. This bond ordinance shall take effect 20 days after the first publication thereof after final adoption, as provided by the Local Bond Law, provided that the consent of the Local Finance Board has been endorsed upon a certified copy of this ordinance as finally adopted.

#2014164 v1 100181-33129  STATEMENT  The Bond Ordinance, the summary terms of which are included herein, has been finally adopted by the Township of West Orange, State of New Jersey on December 3, 2013 and the 20- day period of limitation within which a suit, action or proceeding questioning the validity of such Ordinance can be commenced, as provided in the Local Bond Law, has begun to run from the date of the first publication of this statement. 2394-13  AN ORDINANCE AMENDING AND SUPPLEMENTING  CHAPTER III, SECTION 3-3, OF THE REVISED  GENERAL ORDINANCES OF THE TOWNSHIP OF WEST  ORANGE ENTITLED   “ RULES OF COUNCIL ” ,  “ MEETINGS ” , AND SPECIFICALLY SECTION 3-3.1,  “ REGULAR MEETINGS ”  BE IT ORDAINED BY THE TOWNSHIP COUNCIL OF THE TOWNSHIP OF  WEST ORANGE , in the County of Essex, State of New Jersey, that Chapter III, Section 3-3, of  the Revised General Ordinances of the Township of West Orange, entitled   “ Rules of Council ” ,  “ Meetings ” , and specifically Section 3-3.1,   “ Regular Meetings ” , shall be amended and  supplemented as follows:  Section 1.   The Revised General Ordinances of the Township of West Orange are amended  and supplemented to read as follows:  CHAPTER   III RULES OF COUNCIL  3-3   MEETINGS.  3-3.1   REGULAR MEETINGS.  The Township Council shall hold regular meetings commencing at 7:00 p.m., on  two Tuesdays of each month of the year, each meeting to be separated from the  immediately preceding and succeeding meeting by at least two weeks, except that  during the months of July and August there shall only be one (1) regular meeting  a month, also on a Tuesday and commencing at 7:00 p.m., and separated by at  least two weeks from the preceding and following meetings.   The Council shall  determine by majority vote of its membership, and by no later than the first  meeting in November of the preceding year, the following year ’ s schedule of  regular Council meetings, after reviewing the following year ’ s calendar for  conflicts with holidays, special events and anticipated vacation schedules.  Whenever the day fixed for any regular meeting falls upon a legal or national  holiday the meeting shall be held at the same hour on the next succeeding day  which is not a legal or national holiday. The Council may alter its meeting  schedule, including cancellation of, or rescheduling of, any regular Council  meeting, by majority vote of its membership and providing sufficient time to  provide lawful notice to the public of the change of schedule. In an emergency,  including, but not limited to, a power outage at Town Hall, a severe storm, or  other occurrence implicating substantial public safety concerns, the Mayor,  Business Administrator, or Council President, may summarily cancel a scheduled  Council meeting, notifying all Council members and the public by any available  and appropriate means and with reasonable promptness under all of the  circumstances. (1972 Code § 3-3; Ord. No. 1895-03 § II).

Section 2.   The remainder of Chapter III remains unchanged.  Section 3.   If any part of this Ordinance is determined to be invalid, such part shall be  severed and its invalidity shall not affect the remaining parts of this Chapter.  Section 4.   Any   and   all   parts   of   ordinances   which   are   inconsistent   with   any   of   the   terms   and  provisions   of   this   Ordinance   shall   be   and   the   same   are   hereby   repealed   as   to   and   to   the   extent   of  such inconsistency.  Section 5.   This Ordinance shall take effect upon final passage and publication as required by  law.  Susan McCartney, Council President  Robert D. Parisi, Mayor  Karen Carnevale, Municipal Clerk  Approved as to form and legality on the basis of the facts provided.  Introduction :   November 12, 2013  Adopted : December 3, 2013  2394-13

LEGISLATIVE HISTORY  This Ordinance amends the previous version of the Rules of the Council regarding its  Regular meeting schedule, setting forth the anticipated minimum schedule, the factors to be  considered in creating the schedule, and providing the timing of and manner in which the  following year ’ s meeting schedule is to be determined. It also sets forth procedures for  cancellation of any meeting after the schedule is in place.

pages.text: 198-13  October 29, 2013  RESOLUTION  WHEREAS ,   the   division   of   Alcoholic   Beverages   Control   (ABC)   is   responsible   for  administering   the   Office   of   Juvenile   Justice   Delinquency   Prevention   (OJJDP)   State  Block Grant Program Enforcing the Underage Drinking Laws; and  WHEREAS,   The   West   Orange   Police   Department   applied   to   ABC   for   funds   in  connection   with   a   project   entitled   Cops-in-Shop   Grant   for   the   fiscal   20 1 3   –   20 1 4   year ;  and  WHEREAS ,   the   Township   of   West   Orange   Township   Council   had   already   reviewed   the  application and had deemed it to be in the best interests of the Township; and  WHEREAS ,   the   project   is   a   joint   project   between   the   State   of   New   Jersey   (ABC)   and  the West Orange Department for the purpose of combating underage drinking; and  WHEREAS ,   the   State   of   New   Jersey   (ABC)   has   allocated   a   federal   sub   grant   award   in  the amount of $   to the Township; and  WHEREAS,   there is no matching requirement by the Township.  NOW,   BE   IT   HEREBY   RESOLVED ,   by   the   Township   Council   that   the   Township   of  West   Orange   wishes   to   participate   with   the   State   of   New   Jersey,   Division   of   Alcoholic  Beverage   Control   (ABC)   to   the   greatest   extent   possible;   that   the   Township   hereby  approves   and   authorizes   the   execution   of   the   sub   grant   award   documentation;   and   that  the   Chief   Financial   Officer   of   the   Township   shall   accept   the   funds   in   connection   with   the  project from the ABC and make disbursements in accordance with said application.  IT   IS   FURTHER   RESOLVED ,   that   the   Mayor   and   Chief   of   Police   be   and   are   hereby  authorized   to   execute   any   and   all   documentation   necessary   to   obtain   the   grant   and  effectuate the purposes set forth therein.  A   copy   of   the   grant   shall   be   available   to   the   public   upon   request   and   in   accordance   with  NJSA 47:1A-1 et seq.  Susan McCartney, Council President   Karen J. Carnevale, Municipal Clerk  Adopted: October 29, 2013
