186-13  September 17, 2013  R E S O L U T I O N  WHEREAS,   pursuant to N.J.S.A. 40:67-23.2 et. seq, commonly referred to as the  Municipal Services Act, the Township of West Orange entered into an Agreement with Llewellyn  Park entitled   “ Municipal Services Agreement ’   and dated December 23, 2010; and  WHEREAS,   the Municipal Services Agreement specified the services the Township shall  provide Llewellyn Park or the cost of services the Township shall reimburse to Llewellyn Park;  and  WHEREAS,   the Agreement included Leaf Collection from the Park roads as a service  and specifies the method for calculating the reimbursement, and it also includes the removal of  obstructions such as fallen tree limbs as a service; and  WHEREAS,   there is attached hereto a report of the Township Engineer recommending  reimbursement for 2012 Leaf Collection costs to Llewellyn Park in accordance with the formula  for reimbursement set forth in the Agreement between Llewellyn Park and the Township as well  as the reimbursement for costs associated with the removal of tree debris associated with  Hurricane Sandy that resulted in the simultaneous collection of leaves and tree debris in  October, November and December 2012; and  WHEREAS,   the Township of West Orange wishes to reimburse Llewellyn Park as set  forth in the aforementioned report of the Township Engineer.  NOW, THEREFORE BE IT RESOLVED   by the Township Council of the Township of  West Orange that Llewellyn Park be reimbursed as set forth therein, on the attached report of  the Township Engineer, Leonard R. Lepore, for 2012 leaf collection costs and obstruction  removal.  BE IT FURTHER RESOLVED   that the Township Business Administrator and/or  Comptroller issue the appropriate checks to comply with this Resolution as soon as feasible.  KAREN J. CARNEVALE, MUNICIPAL CLERK   SUSAN McCARTNEY, COUNCIL  PRESIDENT  Adopted: September 17, 2013  I hereby certify funds are available from:  JOHN GROSS, CHIEF FINANCIAL OFFICER

2382-13  AN ORDINANCE AMENDING AND SUPPLEMENTING  CHAPTER III, SECTION 3-5, OF THE REVISED  GENERAL ORDINANCES OF THE TOWNSHIP OF WEST  ORANGE ENTITLED   “ RULES OF COUNCIL ” ,  “ AGENDA ” , AND SECTION 3-8,   “ ORDER OF BUSINESS ”  BE IT ORDAINED BY THE TOWNSHIP COUNCIL OF THE TOWNSHIP OF  WEST ORANGE , in the County of Essex, State of New Jersey, that Chapter III, Section 3-5, of  the Revised General Ordinances of the Township of West Orange, entitled   “ Rules of Council ” ,  “ Agenda ” , shall be amended and supplemented to memorialize certain rules and procedures  regarding the creation and distribution of the Council Agenda, and publication of supporting  materials, and Section 3-8, entitled   “ Order of Business ” , shall be amended and supplemented to  make minor changes to the order of business at regular Council meetings, all as follows:  Section 1.   The Revised General Ordinances of the Township of West Orange are amended  and supplemented to read as follows:  CHAPTER   III RULES OF COUNCIL  3-5   AGENDA.  An agenda for each regular meeting of the Township Council shall be prepared  by the Clerk under the supervision of the President of the Council. All  Ordinances, Resolutions and a ny supporting written material   for consideration at  the next Council Meeting shall be delivered to and received at the Clerk's Office  by 3:00 p.m., on the Wednesday   next   preceding the meeting, earlier if possible  and, if later, shall require permission of the Council President before placement  on the agenda. The agenda headings shall be written in plain English and each  Resolution and Ordinance, unless clearly self-explanatory, shall include a brief  description of its purpose also written in plain English by the department  submitting it. The agenda shall be completed   not   later than   3 p.m.   on the Thursday  preceding each regular meeting of the Council, except for emergency matters  which may be added to the agenda at any time before the meeting with permission  of the President of the Council. The Clerk shall expeditiously deliver a copy of  the Agenda, all Ordinances and Resolutions and all submitted supporting  materials, to each Council member, to the Mayor, Business Administrator, and to  the Law Department. The Clerk shall expeditiously cause the agenda, Ordinances,  and   Resolutions   to be posted on the Township's website no later than the  following Friday   at noon   in order that such materials are readily available to the  general public. In the event of an intervening holiday, the deadlines set forth here  shall be adjusted to provide the same amount of time for preparation and notice of  the agenda. In the event of additions, deletions, or changes of any kind to the  agenda, or any item on it, such changes shall be expeditiously posted to the  Township's website with a notation indicating the change by 4:30 p.m., on the

Monday preceding the Council meeting. The agenda and any changes to it shall  also be physically posted on the Clerk's Bulletin Board in the public hallway at  Town Hall. A conforming agenda shall be published to the Township's website on  the day after each Council meeting to indicate those items which were not acted  upon due to removal or postponement by the Council at its meeting.   (1972 Code  § 3-5; Ord. No. 2349-12).  3-8   Order of Business.  The order of business at a regular meeting of the Council shall be as follows:  Conference Agenda:  Council Liaison Announcements - an opportunity for council members to  announce upcoming events and/or to express appreciation to local  clubs /organizations/volunteers, etc.  Public Agenda:  Pledge of Allegiance;  Roll Call;  Public Comment - Each member of the public wishing to speak shall do so  through the Council President after stating their name and address for the record  and shall be limited to 5 minutes. Each Council Member, including the Council  President, may respond .  Consent Agenda   1- Approval of Minutes of Previous Meeting  2- Report of Township Officers  3- Reading of Petitions and Communications and Bids  4- Bills  5- Resolutions;  Ordinances on Second and Final Reading;  Ordinances on First Reading;  ABC Hearing, if any;  Pending Matters/New Matters/Council Discussion - Each Council  Member, including the Council President, shall have the opportunity to  raise and discuss any pending matter, new matter, or issue for Council  discussion .  Adjournment.

Section 2.   The remainder of Chapter III remains unchanged.  Section 3.   If any part of this Ordinance is determined to be invalid, such part shall be  severed and its invalidity shall not affect the remaining parts of this Chapter.  Section 4.   Any   and   all   parts   of   ordinances   which   are   inconsistent   with   any   of   the   terms   and  provisions   of   this   Ordinance   shall   be   and   the   same   are   hereby   repealed   as   to   and   to   the   extent   of  such inconsistency.  Section 5.   This Ordinance shall take effect upon final passage and publication as required by  law.  __________________________________  Susan McCartney, Council President  Approved:  ____________________________________  Robert D. Parisi, Mayor  Approved as to form and legality on the  basis of the facts provided.  ____________________________________  Karen   J.   Carnevale, Municipal Clerk  Introduction :   August 20, 2013  Adopted : September   17 , 2013  2382-13  LEGISLATIVE HISTORY  This Ordinance amends the previous version of the Rules of the Council regarding its  Agenda and the Order of Business at regular Council Meetings to make them consistent with the

actual recent practice of the Council and Clerk and the intended practice going forward with  respect to the preparation and publication of its meeting agendas and the conduct of its meetings,  including any meetings of the Council sitting as the Township ’ s ABC Board. On the whole, the  intent is to focus on the business of the Council, its ordinances and resolutions, to discuss and  vote upon them in expeditious fashion as a courtesy to those interested members of the public  who have seen them posted on the Agenda and have come to the meeting out of particular  interest in them, or any of them, and wish to get back to their homes and private matters without  undue delay. Such focus and order of business also serve as a courtesy and cost savings to those  Township Department Directors and professionals, and private professionals, whether working  for the Township or on behalf of a citizen/resident/business owner, who must attend a Council  meeting, or ABC Board meeting, to assist the Township ’ s elected and appointed officials, or a  citizen/resident/business owner who is paying for their presence and professional services, often  on the basis of time spent. The legislative purpose is to provide information to the public which  is likely to be useful to their understanding of the Council business matters to be discussed and  acted upon at the Council meeting, while at the same time respecting their time and that of those  who must attend a Council meeting as part of their employment responsibilities, whether public  or private.

2383-13  AN   ORDINANCE   AMENDING   CHAPTER   2,   SECTION   66 .1a   OF   THE   REVISED   GENERAL  ORDINANCES   OF   THE   TOWNSHIP   OF   WEST   ORANGE,   ENTITLED   “FEES   FOR  SERVICES ”  BE   IT   ORDAINED   BY   THE   TOWNSHIP   COUNCIL   OF   THE   TOWNSHIP   OF   WEST  ORANGE ,   in   the   County   of   Essex   and   State   of   New   Jersey,   that   Chapter   2,   Section   66 .1a   of   the  Revised General Ordinances of the Township of West Orange is hereby amended as follows:  I.   PURPOSE  The   purpose   of   this   Ordinance   is   to   reduce   the   fees   charged   for   public   records   in   order  to ensure   compliance with the Open Public Records Act,   N.J.S.A.   47:1A-1 to -13   (OPRA) .  II.   CHAPTER   2,   SECTION   66.1a   SHALL   BE   AND   HEREBY   IS   AMENDED   AND  SUPERSEDED TO READ AS FOLLOWS:  a.   For   the   issuance   of   photocopies   of   ordinances,   minutes,   resolutions,   and   other  government   records   embodied   in   the   form   of   printed   matter,   except   those   listed  below, the fees shall be :  1.   $0.05 per letter size page or smaller ;   and  2.   $0.07 per legal size page or larger.  However,   i f   a   copy   of   a   police   accident   report   is   requested   other   than   in   person,   an  additional   fee   of   $5.00   shall   be   charged   to   cover   the   administrative   costs   of   the  report.  Further,   if   the   public   records   are   delivered   or   mailed,   there   shall   be   an   additional  charge assessed depending upon the postage or delivery method utilized.  III.   REPEAL OF CONFLICTING ORDINANCES  Any   Ordinances   of   the   Township   in   conflict   with   this   Ordinance   are   hereby   repealed   to  the extent of such conflict.  IV.   SEVERABILITY  If   any   part   of   this   Ordinance   shall   be   deemed   invalid,   such   parts   shall   be   severed   and  the invalidity thereof shall not affect the remaining parts of this Ordinance.

2  V.   EFFECTIVE DATE  The   Ordinance   shall   take   effect   upon   final   passage   and   publication   in   accordance   with  the law.  __________________________________  Honorable   Susan McCartney  Council President  ___________________________________  Honorable Robert D. Parisi  Mayor  Approved as to form on the basis of the facts provided:  _________________________________________  Karen J. Carnevale, Municipal Clerk  Introduced: August 20, 2013  Adopted: September 17, 2013

3  LEGISLATIVE HISTORY  Currently,   Section   66.1a   of   Chapter   2   of   the   Revised   General   Ordinances   of   the  Township   of   West   Orange   provides   for   the   following   fees   for   public   records:   (1)   $0.75   per   page  for   the   first   page   to   the   tenth   page;   (2)   $0.50   per   page   for   the   eleventh   page   to   the   twentieth  page;   and   (3)   $0.25   per   page   for   all   pages   over   twenty.   These   fees   are   derived   from   N.J.S.A.  47:1A-2,   which   is   a   since-repealed   provision   of   the   former   Right   to   Know   Law   (“RKL”),   which  was   part   of   the   Public   Records   Act   of   1963.   The   RKL   was   displaced   by   the   Open   Public  Records   Act,   N.J.S.A.   47:1A-1   to   -13   (OPRA),   in   January   2000.   See   generally   Smith   v.   Hudson  County   Register ,   411   N.J.   Super.   538,   558   (App.   Div.   2010).   The   provision   of   OPRA   applicable  to   fees   for   public   records   is   N.J.S.A.   47:1A-5 b ,   and   it   provides   for   the   following   fees:   (1)   $0.05  per   letter   size   page   or   smaller,   and   (2)   $0.07   per   legal   size   page   or   larger.   This   O rdinance  brings Section 66.1a of Chapter 2 into compliance with OPRA.  In   addition,   the   second   paragraph   of   Section   2-66.1b   currently   provides   that   “for   a   police  accident   report   there   is   an   additional   fee   when   the   request   is   not   made   in   person   of   $5.00   for  the   first   three   (3)   pages   and   $1.00   for   each   additional   page   as   provided   by   N.J.S.A.   39:4-131.”  The   referenced   provision   of   the   State   Motor   Vehicle   Regulations   was   amended   in   September  2010,   and   the   provision   permitting   an   additional   $1.00/page   charge   after   three   (3)   pages   was  removed.   The   additional   charge   for   requests   not   made   in   person   is   now   capped   at   $5.00.  N.J.S.A.   39:4-131.   This   Ordinance   therefore   brings   Section   66.1a   of   Chapter   2   into   compliance  with the State Motor Vehicle Regulations as well.

TERMS AND CONDITIONS OF LICENSE AND LICENSE APPLICATION  DEFINITIONS  “Township”   shall   mean   the   Township   of   West   Orange,   a   municipal   corporation   of   the  State   of   New   Jersey   with   its   principal   location   at   60   Main   Street,   West   Orange,   New   Jersey  07052  “Shop   Local   Program”   shall   mean   any   program   whereby   Taxpayer   Consumers   make  purchases   at   Participating   Merchants   and   earn   property   tax   credits   to   satisfy   a   Taxpayer  Consumer ’ s   property   tax   obligations   to   the   Township ,   or   earn   credits   for   check   rebates   for  purchases made at Participating Merchants .  “Applicant”   shall   mean   the   entity   seeking   a   license   to   design,   operate   and   administer   a  “Shop   Local”   program   to   stimulate   local   commerce   by   providing   property   tax   credits   or   other  such   rewards   in   connection   with   purchases   by   Taxpayer   Consumers   made   at   Participating  Merchants, as defined herein.  “ Participating   Merchant(s)”   shall   mean   business(es)   with   a   location   in   the   Township ,   or  located   outside   the   Township   but   not   competing   with   any   local   business   within   the   Township,  which   voluntarily   register   and   enter   into   an   agreement   with   Applicant   to   participate   in   the   Shop  Local Program.  “ Taxpayer   Consumer ( s ) ”   shall   mean   Township   property   owners ,   residents ,   residents   of  other   municipalities   who   work   in   the   Township,   or   any   individual   who   voluntarily   registers   and  enters   into   an   agreement   with   the   Applicant   to   participate   in   the   Shop   Local   Program   and  qualifies for receipt of tax credits or rebates .  GENERAL TERMS AND CONDITIONS  This   License   Application   is   authorized   and   governed   by   Chapter   5,   Section   33   of   the  Revised   General   Ordinances   of   the   Township   of   West   Orange   (the   “Ordinance”).   The   terms  and   conditions   set   forth   therein   apply   to   and   shall   be   binding   on   the   Applicant.   Any   additional  terms   and   conditions   stated   herein,   in   addition   to   those   set   forth   in   the   Ordinance,   shall   also  apply to and be binding on the Applicant.  Annual Report  On   or   about   June   1   of   any   calendar   year,   the   Applicant   shall   provide   to   the   Township’s  Chief   Financial   Officer   a   balance   sheet   setting   forth   the   financial   status   of   the   Township   Shop  Local   Program   including,   but   not   limited   to:   (i)   gross   monies   derived   from   the   Township   Shop  Local   Program;   (ii)   revenue   earned   by   the   Applicant   in   connection   with   the   Shop   Local   Program;  (iii)   total   credits   earned   by   Taxpayer   Consumers;   (iv)   a   listing   of   Taxpayer   Consumers,  organized   by   name,   address   and   Block/Lot   number,   who   have   earned   credits   for   patronizing  local   businesses,   and   the   amounts   earned   by   such   Taxpayer   Consumers;   and   (v)   the   total  monies   due   and   owing   to   the   Township   in   connection   with   credits   earned   by   the   Taxpayer  Consumers.

Weekly   Payments to Township by Applicant  The   Applicant   agrees   to   maintain   a   dedicated   bank   account   for   funds   received   in   connection  with   the   Shop   Local   Program,   unless   the   Taxpayer   Consumer’s   property   tax   credit   funds   will   be  immediately   directed   to   and   held   in   the   Township’s   own   FDIC   insured   dedicated   bank   account,  in   which   case   the   Administrator   need   not   establish   such   a   bank   account.   The   Applicant   agrees  to   hold   in   trust   and/or   turn   over   all   funds   collected   from   the   Shop   Local   Program   on   behalf   of  Taxpayer   Consumers,   via   electronic   transfer,   to   the   Township’s   dedicated   bank   account,   on   a  weekly basis.  Payments to   T enants and   N on- R esidents  It   will   be   the   Licensee’s   sole   responsibility   to   hold   in   trust   and   remit   any   and   all   monies   to  be   paid   as   rebates   to   Taxpayer   Consumers   who   do   not   pay   property   taxes   to   the   Township.  This   includes,   but   is   not   necessarily   limited   to,   tenants,   renters,   non-resident   employees,  visitors,   or   other   such   individuals   authorized   to   participate   and   register   in   the   Shop   Local  Program.  Insurance Requirements, and Indemnification and Hold Harmless  As   more   fully   set   forth   in   the   Ordinance,   Applicant   shall   be   required   to   obtain   and   provide  proof   of   commercial/general   liability   insurance   in   the   amounts   stated   in   the   Ordinance,   and  workers’   compensation   coverage   insurance   as   well.   The   Applicant   also   agrees   to   indemnify,  defend,   and   hold   harmless   the   Township,   its   officers,   agents   and   employees,   from   any   and   all  claims,   actions,   liability   or   costs,   including   reasonable   attorneys’   fees   and   other   costs   of  defense,   in   connection   with,   arising   out   of,   or   in   any   way   related   to   the   negligence   of   the  Applicant, or its agents or employees, in the administration of the   Shop Local Program .  Fiduciary   Requirement  As   more   fully   set   forth   in   Section   5-33.10   of   the   Ordinance,   t he   Applicant   shall   provide   to  the   Township,   in   lieu   of   a   cash   or   other   bond,   proof   of   a   valid   fiduciary   policy   naming   the  Township   as   a   loss   payee   in   the   amount   of   $50,000,   which   amount   may   be   amended   from   time  to time by Resolution of the Township Council.  Termination of Program  Applicant   shall   provide   at   least   ninety   (90)   days’   written   notice   to   Participating  Merchants,   Taxpayer   Consumers   and   the   Township   in   the   event   the   Shop   Local   Program   shall  be   terminated   for   any   reason.   In   the   event   of   termination,   Applicant   will   be   responsible   for  transmitting   a   final   data   file   and   bank   wire   transfer   accounting   for   all   outstanding   credits   or  rebates   applicable   to   Taxpayer   Consumers.   The   Bond   Guarantee   and/or   Fiduciary   Policy   may  be   released   following   a   final   accounting   of   all   gross   revenues   and   outstanding   tax   credits,   which  may   be   independently   audited   at   the   request   of   the   Township   and   at   the   sole   cost   and   expense  of the Applicant.

No Subcontractors  The   Applicant   shall   not   be   permitted   to   utilize   any   subcontractors   in   the   performance   of  services   under   any   license   granted   pursuant   to   this   Application ,   unless   the   subcontractor   is  disclosed   on   this   application.   If   the   subcontractor   will   possess   or   hold   in   trust   any   monies   to   be  credited   to   the   Township   on   behalf   of   a   Taxpayer   Consumer,   such   subcontractor   shall   have   a  fiduciary   policy   naming   the   Township   as   a   loss   payee   in   the   amount   stated   in   Section   5-33.10   of  the Ordinance.  Terms of Use of   “ Shop West Orange ”   Phrase  The   Applicant   shall   not   use   the   name   “Shop   West   Orange”   nor   the   graphic   “Shop   West  Orange”   logo   or   any   part   thereof   without   the   written   consent   of   the   Township   Business  Administrator.   If   Applicant   breaches   this   requirement,   said   license   shall   automatically   terminate.  All   rights   to   the   name   “Shop   West   Orange,”   and   the   graphic   “Shop   West   Orange”   logo,   are  reserved to the Township.  Amount of Credits to   Taxpayer   Consumer s  As   more   fully   set   forth   in   Section   5-33.9   of   the   Ordinance,   it   shall   be   the   Applicant’s   sole  responsibility   to   ensure   that   Taxpayer   Consumers   have   access   to   a   written   description   of   the  gross   percentage   of   credits   that   will   be   paid   to   the   Township   towards   Taxpayer   Consumers’   real  property taxes upon a purchase made at all Participating Merchants.  The   Applicant   shall   be   responsible   for   providing   merchants   with   window   clings   stating  the   merchant’s   participation   in   the   program,   the   form   of   which   may   be   approved   by   the  Township’s   Business   Administrator.   Furthermore,   the   Applicant   shall   make   its   best   effort   to  ensure   that   all   Participating   Merchant s   post   the   window   cling   in   its   front   window   or   in   another  conspicuous   location   indicating   that   the   Participating   Merchant   is   a   “ Participating   Member   of  Shop West Orange Property Tax Reward Program . ”  Each   Participating   Merchant   shall   be   responsible   for   making   available   to   Taxpayer  Consumers   patronizing   its   business   a   written   description   of   the   percentage   of   each   transaction  which shall be paid to the Township and/or credited to the Taxpayer Consumer.  Advertising  Applicant   shall   have   the   responsibility   to   ensure   that   the   Shop   Local   Program   is   properly  advertised   to   all   Township   residents.   This   shall   include   at   a   minimum,   but   is   in   no   way   limited  to:   (i)   working   with   Participating   Merchants   to   launch   and/or   maintain   Shop   West   Orange  page(s)   on   the   Applicant’s   web   portal   and   keeping   same   updated;   (ii)   regularly   maintaining   the  Applicant’s   Web   site   to   post   a   list,   with   phone   numbers   and   addresses,   of   all   Participating  Merchants   participating   in   the   Shop   Local   Program;   (iii)   (iii)   providing   Participating   Merchants  with   e-mail   addresses   of   Taxpayer   Consumers   who   have   registered   for   the   Shop   Local   Program  and   have   shopped   at   their   location,   individually;   and   (iv)   making   itself   available   to   the   Township,  its   Chamber   of   Commerce   and   the   Downtown   West   Orange   Alliance   to   discuss   strategies   for  advertising.

Review of Applications by Merchants  The   Applicant   shall   coordinate,   receive   and   review   all   applications   by   local   businesses   to  become   a   Participating   Merchant.   In   the   event   that   the   Applicant   receives   an   application   to  become   a   Participating   Merchant   from   a   business   located   outside   the   Township,   the   Applicant  shall   review   same   and   provide   a   copy   to   the   Township’s   Business   Administrator.   Any   approvals  to   businesses   located   outside   the   Township   shall   be   subject   to   the   review   and   approval   of   the  Township ’ s Mayor, Chief Financial Officer and/or Business Administrator.  Breach of Licensing Terms  In   the   event   of   a   breach   of   any   of   the   terms   and   conditions   of   the   Ordinance   or   the   terms  and   conditions   stated   in   this   Application,   the   Township   reserves   the   right   to   revoke   and/or  suspend   the   Applicant’s   license.   In   the   event   of   termination,   the   Applicant   shall   satisfy   the  requirements set forth in the subsection titled   “ Termination of Program. ”  Business Registration Certification  Along   with   this   Application,   Applicant   shall   provide   to   the   Township   a   copy   of   its   New  Jersey Business Registration Certificate. Township   of West Orange   Page   1   of   6   Reward Programs / MOU  2384-13  MEMORANDUM OF UNDERSTANDING  BETWEEN  TOWNSHIP OF WEST ORANGE AND FINCREDIT, INC.  FOR THE  SHOP WEST ORANGE PROPERTY TAX REWARD PROGRAM  DEFINITIONS  “Township”   shall   mean   the   Township   of   West   Orange,   with   principal   offices   located   at   6 6   Main  Street, West Orange, New Jersey 07052  “Administrator”   shall   mean   Fincredit,   Inc.,   with   a   mailing   address   of   P.O.   Box   43,   Marlboro,   New  Jersey   07746,   and   a   physical   address   at   2271   Rt.   33,   Suite   106,   Hamilton,   New   Jersey   08690.  Fincredit   is   charged   with   implementation   of   the   Shop   West   Orange   Program,   including   entering  into agreements with   Participating   Merchants and   Taxpayer   Consumers.  “ Participating   Merchant(s)”   shall   mean   business(es)   with   a   location   in   the   Township ,   or   located  outside   the   Township   but   not   competing   with   any   local   business,   which   voluntarily   register   and  enter into an agreement with Applicant to participate in the Shop   West Orange   Program.  “ Taxpayer   Consumer ( s ) ”   shall   mean   Township   property   owners,   renters ,   residents   of   other  municipalities   who   work   in   the   Township ,   or   any   individual   who   voluntarily   register s   with   and  enter s   into   an   agreement   with   the   Administrator   and   qualifies   for   receipt   of   tax   credits   or  rebates .  “Property   Tax   Reward   Cards”   shall   mean   Shop   West   Orange   Program   swipe   cards   issued   to  Taxpayer C onsumers for use at   P articipating Merchants.  DESCRIPTION  The   Shop   West   Orange   Property   Tax   Reward   Program   the   ( “Shop   West   Orange  Program” ),   will   utilize   technology   similar   to   that   utilized   in   connection   with   traditional   “gift   and  loyalty   card”   programs   in   addition   to   Administrator   proprietary   software.   The   goal   of   the  program   is   to   provide   Township   taxpayers   with   a   financial   incentive   to   patronize   participating  Township   businesses   in   the   form   of   a   Property   Tax   Reward   or   other   such   rebate ,   and   at   the  same   time   provide   Township-based   businesses   with   a   financial   incentive,   in   the   form   of  increased patronage and revenues, to enroll in the program.  In   summary,   a   Taxpayer   Consumer   who   patronizes   a   P articipating   Merchant   will   receive  a   corresponding   percentage-of-sale   credit   on   his   or   her   property   taxes.   Where   a   non-resident  Taxpayer   Consumer   is   an   employee   in   a   Township   business,   or   where   a   Consumer   is   a  resident who is a tenant in the Township, these   Taxpayer   Consumers may receive a rebate.  The key aspects of the program involve:

Township   of West Orange   Page   2   of   6   Reward Programs / MOU  1.   Program promotion   and marketing;  2.   Participating   Merchant registration ;  3.   Taxpayer   Consumer   registration   via   a   secure   web   portal   through   the  Administrator’s   Web   site,   and   through   issuance   of   Property   Tax   Reward  Cards;  4.   Taxpayer   Consumers patronizing   P articipating   M erchants ;  5.   Taxpayer   Consumers   receiving   a   Property   Tax   Reward   credit   based   upon   a  percent age   of   a   purchase   made   at   a   Participating   Merchant,   as   a   reward   for  patronizing   the   P articipating   M erchant ,   and   where   the   percentage   credit   to   be  awarded   to   the   Taxpayer   Consumer   will   be   chosen   by   the   Participating  Merchant;  6.   Administrator   receiving   a   fee   for   the   cost   of   administering   the   program ,   which  shall   equal   to   twenty-five   ( 25% )   percent   of   the   Property   Tax   Reward   for   each  transaction between a   Taxpayer C onsumer and   P articipating   M erchant ; and  7.   The   Township   receiving   weekly   money   transfers   from   Administrator   in   the  amount of property tax reward credits earned by Taxpayer Consumers.  Administrator Responsibilities  1.   Strict   adherence   to   the   terms   of   the   Township   Reward   Program   ordinance,   and   any   other  applicable Township ordinances.  2.   Program Promotion, including but not limited to:  a.   Assisting   with   the   design   of   the   Property   Tax   Reward   Cards   for   the   Shop   West  Orange P rogram  b.   Assisting   in   the   production   of   “infomercials”   to   be   aired   on   Local   Cable   TV  Channel(s)  c.   Attending   meetings   with   the   Township   or   designated   association(s)   including  program kick offs with local businesses  3.   Entering   into   agreements   with   Participating   Merchants   and   establishing   all   terms   including,  but not limited to:     Responsibility for hardware     Software and training in use of software     Reward card processing     Percentage of transaction credit to   Taxpayer   Consumer     Percentage of transaction credit to Administrator     Incorporating   language   indicating   voluntary   nature   of   program   and   legal  indemnification language

Township   of West Orange   Page   3   of   6   Reward Programs / MOU  4.   Hosting   a   Web   portal/Web   site   whereby   Administrator   may   register   and   enter   into  agreements with   Taxpayer   Consumers establishing all terms including but not limited to:     A   statement   of   the   percentage   of   a   transaction   credit   which   will   go   to   Taxpayer  Consumer     A statement of the percentage of a transaction credit which will go to Administrator     The timing of posting of credits earned (annually)     Policy regarding data privacy and sharing of information for marketing purposes     Specific limits and restrictions relative to program participation     A   statement   that   a   taxpayer   in   the   Township   must   be   current   on   its   property   taxes   to  participate     No refunds will be issued     Credits   on   accounts   that   are   current   will   result   in   prepayments   of   property   taxes   for  future periods     A   statement   that   the   Administrator   is   not   responsible   for   impact   of   program  participation on mortgage escrow calculations.     A   statement   that   neither   the   Administrator   nor   the   Township   is   responsible   for   credits  with respect to changes in property ownership     Posting   language   indicating   the   voluntary   nature   of   program   and   legal  indemnification language satisfactory to the Association  5. Providing   Taxpayer   Consumers   with   24-hour   online   access   to   the   dollar   amount   of   property  tax credits earned by the   Taxpayer   Consumer s   to date.  8.   Establishing   FDIC   insured   dedicated   bank   account(s)   for   the   Township   Shop   West   Orange  Program ,   unless   the   Taxpayer   Consumer’s   property   tax   credit   funds   will   be   immediately  directed   to   and   held   in   the   Township’s   own   FDIC   insured   dedicated   bank   account,   in   which  case   the   Administrator   need   not   establish   such   a   bank   account . Township   of West Orange   Page   5   of   6   Reward Programs / MOU  IDENTITY OF PARTICIPATING MERCHANTS  The   Applicant   shall   coordinate,   receive   and   review   all   applications   by   local   businesses   to  become   a   Participating   Merchant.   In   the   event   that   the   Applicant   receives   an   application   to  become   a   Participating   Merchant   from   a   business   located   outside   the   Township,   the   Applicant  shall   review   same,   and   any   approvals   shall   be   subject   to   the   review   and   approval   of   the  Township’s   Chief   Financial   Officer   and/or   Business   Administrator,   which   approval   shall   not   be  unreasonably withheld.  DISCLOSURE OF PROGRAM FEES  It is understood that certain fees will be incurred in connection with administration of the  Shop   West Orange   Program, and a listing of such fees shall be submitted with the  Administrator ’ s application for a license to operate the Shop   West Orange   Program. Such fees  are currently as follows:  1.   Costs to   Participating   Merchant:     Hardware   Costs   are   a   $160,   one-time   cost   for   the   dial   up   processing   machine   ($230  dual   comm.   IP).   They   may   also   opt   for   processing   via   the   Administrator’s   Web   site   in  which   case   the   cost   is   $0,   or   $50   if   the   Participating   Merchant   wishes   to   purchase   a  card reader. The cost of equipment is subject to change.     $10 month ly   processing fee.     No   additional   costs   will   apply.   Administrator   agrees   to   waive   its   customary   $0.15  cents per swipe fee.  2.   Other costs :     For   providing   ongoing   services   as   A dministrator   of   the   Shop   West   Orange   Program,  Administrator   will   retain   twenty-five   (25%)   percent   of   the   Property   Tax   Reward   credit  provided by   Participating   Merchants to   Taxpayer   Consumers.     Example:   A   Participating   Merchant   chooses   a   ten   percent   (10%)   property   tax   credit  on   sales.   If   a   homeowner   makes   a   $100   purchase,   creating   a   $10   property   tax   credit,  the   homeowner   will   receive   $7.50   and   the   Administrator   will   retain   $2.50   for   program  expenses and administration fees.

2384-13  AN   ORDINANCE   AMENDING   CHAPTER   5   OF   THE   GENERAL   ORDINANCES   OF   THE  TOWNSHIP   OF   WEST   ORANGE,   AND   CREATING   SUBCHAPTER   33,   ENTITLED   “  LICENSING FOR   SHOP LOCAL REWARD PROGRAMS ”  WHEREAS ,   pursuant   to   Resolution   No.   87-13,   the   Township   Council   for   the   Township   of  West   Orange   formalized   its   support   of   a   “Shop   Local”   campaign   to   stimulate   the   patronage   of  local businesses located in the Township;  NOW,   THEREFORE,   BE   IT   ORDAINED   BY   THE   TOWNSHIP   COUNCIL   OF   THE  TOWNSHIP   OF   WEST   ORANGE ,   in   the   County   of   Essex   and   State   of   New   Jersey,   that   there  should   be   created   a   Chapter   5,   Section   33,   of   the   Revised   General   Ordinances   of   the   Township  of West Orange, which shall provide as follows:  5-33 LICENSING FOR   “ SHOP LOCAL ”   REWARD PROGRAMS  5-33.1   Purpose  The   purpose   of   this   Ordinance   is   to   provide   a   uniform   set   of   procedures   for   applications  by   and   licensing   of   an   Applicant   seeking   to   operate   and   administer   a   “Shop   Local”   program  within the Township.  5-33.2   Definitions  As   referred   to   in   this   Chapter   and   all   applicable   subsections,   the   following   terms   shall  have the following meanings:  “Shop   Local   Program”   shall   mean   any   program   whereby   Taxpayer   Consumers   make  purchases   at   Participating   Merchants   and   earn   property   tax   credits   to   satisfy   a   Taxpayer  Consumer’s   property   tax   obligations   to   the   Township ,   or   earn   credits   for   check   rebates   for  purchases made at Participating Merchants .  “Applicant”   shall   mean   the   entity   seeking   a   license   to   design,   operate   and   administer   a  “Shop   Local   P rogram ”   to   stimulate   local   commerce   by   providing   property   tax   credits   or   other  such   rewards   in   connection   with   purchases   by   Taxpayer   Consumers   made   at   Participating  Merchants,   as   defined   herein.   The   term   “Applicant”   may   also   refer   to   a   “Licensee”   after   a  license is awarded to an Applicant pursuant to the terms of this Chapter.  “ Participating   Merchant(s)”   shall   mean   business(es)   with   a   location   in   the   Township ,   or  located   outside   the   Township   but   not   competing   with   any   local   business,   which   voluntarily  register and enter into an agreement with Applicant to participate in the Shop Local Program.  “ Taxpayer   Consumer ( s ) ”   shall   mean   Township   property   owners ,   residents ,   residents   of  other   municipalities   who   work   in   the   Township,   or   any   individual   who   voluntarily   registers   and  enters   into   an   agreement   with   the   Applicant   to   participate   in   the   Shop   Local   Program   and  qualifies for receipt of tax credits or rebates .

2  5-33.3   License Required  No   person   or   entity   shall   operate   a   Shop   Local   Program   within   the   Township   unless   the  entity seeking to operate same is licensed pursuant to the terms of this Ordinance.  5-33.4   Licensing of Applicants  a.   Application   Information .   Applications   for   a   license   to   operate   a   Shop   Local   Program   shall  be   made   to   the   Township   Clerk   on   forms   provided   by   the   Township   Clerk.   The   form(s)   to  be   used   for   the   application   shall   be   approved   by   the   Township   Council   and   m a y   set   forth  additional   requirements,   terms   and   conditions,   other   than   those   enumerated   in   this  Ordinance,   which   the   Township   Council   deems   necessary   and   proper   to   the   efficient   and  effective administration of the Shop Local Program.  Applications   shall   be   verified   by   oath   or   affirmation.   Applications   by   a   corporation   shall  be   verified   by   all   officers   and   directors   and   all   persons   holding   more   than   ten-percent  (10%) of the corporation ’ s common stock, as well as the corporation itself.  b.   Review   of   License .   Upon   receipt   of   the   license   application,   the   Township   Clerk   shall  review   the   application   to   ensure   that   it   has   been   properly   completed.   The   Township  Clerk   shall   thereafter   submit   all   properly   completed   applications   to   the   Township   Council  for determination as to whether to grant or deny the license.  c.   Factors   considered .   In   determining   whether   to   grant   or   deny   the   license   to   the   Applicant,  the   Township   Council   shall   take   into   consideration,   among   any   other   factors   it   considers  relevant, the following:  1.   The Applicant ’ s ability to properly conduct the licensed activity .  2.   The   moral   character   of   the   Applicant,   whether   a   corporate   entity   or   other   such  partnership.   If   a   corporation,   the   Township   Council   shall   consider   the   moral  character   of   each   officer,   director,   manager,   supervisor   and/or   shareholder.   If   a  partnership,   the   Township   Council   shall   consider   the   moral   character   of   each  partner.  3.   The   business   responsibility   of   the   Applicant,   including   but   not   limited   to   community  service work or other such charitable endeavors.  4.   The   criminal   history   of   the   Applicant,   whether   an   entity   or   individual   owner,   director,  manager,   supervisor,   officer   or   shareholder .   A   copy   of   the   Applicant’s   criminal  history   record,   obtained   from   the   New   Jersey   State   Police,   shall   be   submitted   with  the   application.   If   the   Applicant   is   a   corporation,   a   criminal   history   record   from   the  New   Jersey   State   Police   shall   be   submitted   for   all   officers   and   directors   of   the  corporation,   all   shareholders   owning   more   than   ten   (10)   percent   of   common   stock ,  and   any   other   employee   who   will   be   an   authorized   signatory   on   any   bank   account  where   Taxpayer   Consumers’   monies   will   be   deposited .   If   the   Applicant   is   a  partnership,   a   criminal   history   record   from   the   New   Jersey   State   Police   shall   be  submitted for all partners.  5.   Prior   experience   in   the   financial   industry,   although   a   lack   of   prior   experience   shall   not  necessarily be a factor militating against issuance of a license.

3  d.   Issuance   of   License .   If   the   Applicant’s   request   for   a   license   is   approved   by   the   Township  Council,   the   Township   Clerk   shall   issue   a   license   to   the   Applicant   within   ten   (10)   business  days   of   the   approval.   The   contents   of   an   Applicant’s   license   to   operate   a   Shop   Local  Program shall contain the information required pursuant to Section 1.4 of this Chapter.  5-33.5   Term of License  The   first   license   granted   to   an   Applicant   shall   be   for   a   term   of   two   ( 2 )   year s   from   the   date  of   issuance   of   the   license   by   the   Township   Clerk   (the   “Initial   Term”),   after   Township   Council  approval .   After   the   expiration   of   the   Initial   Term ,   the   license   may   be   renewed   for   subsequent  term s   of   three   ( 3 )   years ,   provided   that   Applicant   submits   a   request   for   renewal   to   the   Township  Clerk   in   accordance   with   Section   5-33.7,   and   same   is   approved   by   the   Township   Council.   The  three   (3)   year   term   shall   commence   from   the   date   of   issuance   of   the   license   renewal   by   the  Township Clerk .  �-.6   Application and License Fees  a.   Application   Fee .   In   order   to   submit   an   application   for   a   license   pursuant   to   this  Ordinance,   the   Applicant   shall   remit   a   non-refundable   application   fee   of   $ 50   to   the  Township Clerk. This application fee shall not apply to applications for license renewals.  b.   License   Fee .   The   Applicant   shall   remit   a   $ 100   fee   for   the   initial   two   ( 2 )   year   term   of   its  license.   If   the   license   is   renewed   for   subsequent   three   ( 3 )   year   term(s)   in   accordance  with   Section   5-33.5 ,   the   Applicant   shall   pay   to   the   Township   a   $ 200   non-refundable  license fee.  �-.7   Renewals  All   license   renewals   shall   be   put   to   the   Township   Council   for   consideration.   Failure   to  request   a   license   renewal,   in   writing,   within   thirty   (30)   days   prior   to   expiration   of   a   license   term  shall render the Applicant ’ s license void at the conclusion of the term.  An   Applicant’s   license   may   be   renewed   contingent   upon   payment   of   the   applicable   non-  refundable license fee and the filing of:  1.   A   sworn   statement   filed   with   the   Township   Clerk   that   there   have   been   no   changes  in   the   information   contained   in   the   initial   application,   which   shall   be   filed   with   the  Township Clerk no later than thirty (30) days prior to the expiration of a term; or  2.   In   the   event   of   any   change   in   information   submitted   with   the   Applicant’s   initial  application,   submission   of   a   re new al   application   to   the   Township   Clerk   for  consideration   by   the   Township   Council   no   later   than   thirty   (30)   days   prior   to   the  expiration of a term.  5-33.8   Revocation and Suspension  An   Applicant’s   license   may   be   revoked   or   suspended   pursuant   to   the   terms   of   Section  1.9   of   this   Chapter.   In   addition   to   the   causes   for   revocation   or   suspension   specified   therein,   the  Applicant’s   license   may   be   revoked   or   suspended   by   the   Council   after   notice   and   a   hearing   for  any of the following reasons :

4  a.   Applicant   fails   to   timely   remit   payments   to   the   Township   to   satisfy   obligations   due   to   the  Township from Taxpayer Consumers .  b.   Applicant   does   not   satisfy   its   obligations   to   the   Township,   as   set   forth   in   the   terms   of   this  Ordinance   or   in   the   terms   and   conditions   set   forth   in   the   license   application   as   approved  by the Township Council.  c.   The   Township   learns   of   t he   existence   of   entry   of   a   judgment,   or   an   unsatisfied   judgment  of   record,   entered   against   the   licensee   or   Applicant   in   any   suit   arising   from   or   in   relation  to the operation of any Shop Local Program;  d.   Applicant   fails   to   comply   with   any   and   all   applicable   laws   of   the   State   of   New   Jersey,   the  ordinances   of   the   Township,   or   the   rules   and   regulations   adopted   in   accordance   with   this  Ordinance;  e.   The   Township   learns   of   the   intentional   misrepresentation   of   information   on   an  application for a license or a request for a renewal.  5-33.9   Shop Local   Program   As   Sole Responsibility of Applicant   and Applicant ’ s  Responsibility for Informing Taxpayer Consumers  a.   Sole   Responsibility   of   Applicant .   The   Shop   Local   Program   shall   be   administered   and  managed   solely   by   the   Applicant.   Upon   licensing ,   it   shall   be   the   Licensee’s   independent  responsibility   to   oversee   the   Shop   Local   Program,   register   Taxpayer   Consumers,   and/or  contract   with   interested   Participating   Merchants.   The   Applicant   shall   not   represent   in  any manner that it is an agent or representative of the Township.  b.   Written   descriptions   of   program .   It   shall   be   the   Applicant’s   sole   responsibility   to   ensure  that   Taxpayer   Consumers   have   access   to   a   written   description   of   the   gross   percentage  of   credits   that   will   be   paid   to   the   Township   towards   the   Taxpayer   Consumer’s   real  property   taxes   upon   a   purchase   made   at   all   Participating   Merchants.   Licensee   shall  make   its   best   effort   to   ensure   that   all   Participating   Merchant s   post   a   sign   in   its   front  window   or   in   another   conspicuous   location,   indicating   that   the   Participating   Merchant   is   a  “ Participating   Member   of   Shop   West   Orange   Property   Tax   Reward   Program .”   Every  Participating   Merchant   shall   be   responsible   for   making   available   to   Taxpayer   Consumers  patronizing   its   business   a   written   description   of   the   percentage   of   each   transaction   which  shall be paid to the Township and/or credited to the Taxpayer Consumer.  c.   Review   of   Participating   Merchant’s   Applications   To   Participate   In   Program .   The  Applicant   shall   coordinate,   receive   and   review   all   applications   by   local   businesses   to  become   a   Participating   Merchant.   In   the   event   that   the   Applicant   receives   an   application  to   become   a   Participating   Merchant   from   a   business   located   outside   the   Township,   the  Licensee   shall   review   same,   and   any   approvals   shall   be   subject   to   the   review   and  approval   of   the   Township’s   Chief   Financial   Officer   and/or   Business   Administrator ,   which  approval shall not be unreasonably withheld .  Insurance and Indemnification  a.   Proof   of   Insurance .   In   conjunction   with   the   submission   of   an   Applicant’s   application   or  renewal   notice   to   operate   a   Shop   Local   Program,   an   Applicant   must   provide   proof   of   a  valid   and   current   general   commercial   liability   insurance   policy   with   a   company   duly  licensed   to   transact   business   under   the   insurance   laws   of   this   State.   The   policy   or

5  policies   shall   provide   coverage   for   a   sum   of   not   less   than   $1,000,000   against   loss   by  reason   of   general   commercial   liability,   not   less   than   $250,000   to   satisfy   any   claim   for  damages   to   property   negligently   caused   by   the   A pplicant,   and   not   less   than   $2,000,000   in  the   aggregate.   Applicant   shall   also   be   required   to   maintain   in   force,   for   the   duration   of  the   licensing   period,   insurance   policies   for   workers   compensation   coverage   for   its  employees,   officers,   agents,   and/or   partners,   as   required   by   applicable   workers  compensation   laws,   and   shall   provide   a   Certificate   of   Insurance   to   the   Township   to  evidence   such   coverage.   The   Applicant   must   provide   a   current   Certificate   of   Insurance  listing   the   Township   as   an   additional   insured.   All   insurance   must   cover   any   acts   of  defalcation of Township funds.  b.   Indemnification .   The   Applicant   shall   indemnify,   defend,   and   hold   harmless   the   Township,  its   officers,   agents   and   employees,   from   any   and   all   claims,   actions,   liability   or   costs,  including   reasonable   attorneys’   fees   and   other   costs   of   defense,   in   connection   with,  arising   out   of,   or   in   any   way   related   to   the   negligence   of   the   Applicant,   or   its   agents   or  employees, in the administration of the   Shop Local Program.  5-33.1 0   Fiduciary Policy   Requirement  a.   Fiduciary   Policy   R equire d .   The   Applicant   shall   provide   to   the   Township,   in   lieu   of   a   cash  or   other   bond,   proof   of   a   valid   fiduciary   policy   naming   the   Township   as   a   loss   payee   in  the   amount   of   $50,000.   The   amount   required   for   the   Fiduciary   Policy   may   be   amended  from time to time by Resolution of the Township Council.  b.   Amendments   to   Fiduciary   Policy .   The   Township   specifically   reserves   the   right   to   require  changes   to   the   fiduciary   policy   amount   during   a   license   term ,   based   upon   the   volume   and  amounts   of   sales   transactions.   The   type   of   guarantee   may   be   substituted   with   another  form of security acceptable to the Township, at the discretion of the Township Council.  5-33.11   Timing and Reporting  a.   Weekly   Deposits   Required .   A   Licensee   under   this   Chapter   must   maintain   a   dedicated  bank   account   for   funds   received   in   connection   with   the   Shop   Local   Program,   unless   the  Taxpayer   Consumer’s   property   tax   credit   funds   will   be   immediately   directed   to   and   held   in  the   Township’s   own   FDIC   insured   dedicated   bank   account,   in   which   case   the  Administrator   need   not   establish   such   a   bank   account.   A   Licensee   shall   hold   in   trust   and  every   week   shall   turn   over   all   funds   collected   from   the   Shop   Local   Program   on   behalf   of  Taxpayer   Consumers,   via   electronic   transfer,   to   the   Township’s   dedicated   bank   account.  The cost of the wire transfer, if any, shall be the responsibility of the Applicant.  b.   Quarterly   Reporting   Required .   Along   with   the   weekly   deposits   required   under   this  subsection,   within   ten   (10)   business   days   of   the   expiration   of   each   calendar   quarter,   a  Licensee   shall   provide   a   report   to   the   Township’s   Chief   Financial   Officer,   which   must   be  certified   and   provide   the   following   information:   (i)   name   of   Taxpayer   Consumer;   (ii)  amount   of   funds   collected;   and   (iii)   the   address   and/or   Block   and   Lot   number   of   the  Township property which should be credited, if any.  c.   Payments   to   tenants   and   non-residents .   It   will   be   the   Licensee’s   sole   responsibility   to  hold   in   trust   and   remit   any   and   all   monies   to   be   paid   as   rebates   to   Taxpayer   Consumers  who   do   not   pay   property   taxes   to   the   Township,   including   but   not   limited   to   tenants,  renters,   non-resident   employees,   visitors,   or   other   such   individuals   authorized   to

6  participate   and   register   in   the   Shop   Local   Program.   A   Licensee’s   sign-up   form(s)   or  agreement(s)   with   a   Taxpayer   Consumer   shall   conspicuously   state   that   the   Licensee   will  be   solely   responsible   for   handling   and   distributing   monies   to   tenants,   renters,   non-  resident employees, visitors or other such individuals who do not pay property taxes.  d.   Reports   to   Taxpayer   Consumers .   The   Licensee   shall   make   available   to   all   Taxpayer  Consumers   a   written   statement   of   credits   or   rebates   earned   by   the   Taxpayer   Consumer.  The   Licensee   may   provide   this   statement   via   e-mail,   or   make   same   accessible   via   its  Web site.  5-33.1 2   Property Tax Credits Provided Only Upon Receipt of Funds by the  Township  The   Township   will   only   provide   credits   to   a   Taxpayer   Consumer’s   property   tax   bill   after  payment   is   received   from   a   Licensee   with   respect   to   the   specific   property   tax   account.   If   the  Township   does   not   receive   payment   relating   to   a   property   tax   credit,   whether   or   not   such  rebates   were   collected   by   the   Licensee,   the   Taxpayer   Consumer   will   be   responsible   for   the   full  amount   of   the   property   tax   liability   as   if   the   credits   were   never   provided   by   the   Participating  Merchant.  5-33.1 3   Violations and Penalties  In   addition   to   suspension   and/or   revocation   of   any   license   issued   hereunder,   and   in  addition   to   any   applicable   State   penalties,   any   violation   of   the   provisions   of   this   Ordinance,   or  any   violation   of   the   terms   and   conditions   of   the   Applicant’s   application,   shall   be   punishable   as  provided   in   Chapter   I,   Section   1-5   of   the   Revised   General   Ordinances   of   the   Township   of   West  Orange.  5-33.1 4   Additional Conditions.  The   Township   shall   establish   the   necessary   reporting   requirement   for   any   payment   of  taxes   made   to   the   Township   by   the   vendor.   The   Township   shall   not   be   responsible   for   any  impact   of   program   participation   on   mortgage   escrow   calculations.   The   Township   shall   not   be  responsible for any changes in property ownership.  The   Applicant   shall   have   the   right   to   surrender   its   license   to   the   Township   at   any   time,   so  long   as   it   provides   ninety   (90)   days’   notice   to   the   Township   Clerk   and   Business   Administrator,   in  writing.   In   such   an   event,   the   Applicant   agrees   to   the   orderly   phasing   out   of   its   involvement   in  the   Shop   Local   Program,   including   but   not   limited   to   remitting   the   balance   of   any   and   all   funds  due   to   the   Township   in   connection   with   property   tax   credits   which   may   have   been   retained   by  the Applicant on behalf of Taxpayer Consumers. 8  LEGISLATIVE HISTORY  This   Ordinance   is   intended   to   provide   a   legislative   scheme   which   requires   that   any   entity  seeking   to   operate   any   type   of   “Shop   Local”   incentive   program   must   first   obtain   a   license   to   do  so.  On   May   7,   2013,   the   Township   Council   adopted   Resolution   No.   87-13,   formally  expressing   its   support   for   a   “Shop   Local”   property   tax   credit   program   (the   “Shop   Local  Program”).   The   Shop   Local   Program   proposes   to   authorize   licensed   third   party   administrators  (“TPA”)   to   organize   and   administer   a   local   economic   stimulation   plan   whereby   a   Township  resident   is   rewarded   with   property   tax   credits   when   it   makes   purchases   at   P articipating  M erchants.  As   currently   contemplated,   the   Shop   Local   Program   will   require   TPAs   to   register   and  contract   with   certain   interested   merchants,   which   would   offer   tax   credits,   processed   through   the  TPA,   to   residents   who   make   purchases   at   their   businesses.   The   TPA   will   receive   a   fee   from   the  Participating   M erchant   for   a   designated   portion   of   the   transaction   (in   an   exact   percentage  selected   by   the   Participating   M erchant),   and   a   portion   of   that   fee   would   then   be   provided   to   the  Township to satisfy the resident ’ s financial or tax obligations to the Township.  Other   municipalities   have   adopted   or   are   in   the   process   of   adopting   and   authorizing   such  programs.   These   municipalities   include   Marlboro,   Pequannock,   Voorhees,   Bayonne,   Caldwell  and   Manalapan.   The   New   Jersey   Department   of   Consumer   Affairs,   Division   of   Local  Government   Services,   has   advised   the   Township   that   there   is   no   prohibition   on   this   manner   of  satisfying a resident ’ s tax obligations.  This   proposed   Ordinance   serves   several   important   functions.   First,   it   creates   a   clear  procedure   to   govern   the   application   process   for   those   entities   seeking   to   operate   any   Shop Local  Program.   Once   a   conforming   application   has   been   submitted   to   the   Township   Clerk   and  reviewed   for   completeness,   the   Township   Council   has   the   authority   to   grant   or   deny   the   license  by resolution based on its review of several enumerated factors .

9  This   legislation   also   promotes   the   public   safety   and   welfare,   requiring   that   all   applicants  for   a   license   to   operate   a   Shop   Local   Program   meet   a   review   by   the   Township   Council   about   the  applicant’s   business   aptitude   and   criminal   history.   This   Ordinance   also   protects   the   public   by  requiring   the   licensee   to   post   a   bond,   essentially   guaranteeing   that   monies   credited   to   a  Taxpayer   C onsumer   for   making   purchases   at   local   businesses   actually   be   made   available   to   the  Township   as   a   credit   to   the   Taxpayer   C onsumer.   The   Ordinance   also   requires   the   applicant   to  file   proof   of   liability   insurance   which   names   the   Township   as   an   Additional   Insured,   and   further  requires   an   indemnification   in   favor   of   the   Township   to   ensure   that   there   is   limited   liability   in  connection   with   the   Shop   Local   Program.   Also,   given   the   innovative   nature   of   the   Shop   Local  Program,   this   legislation   authorizes   the   Township   Council   to   establish,   by   resolution,   a   form   of  application   to   be   submitted   by   the   prospective   licensee;   this   application   form   may   also   set   forth  additional   terms   and   conditions   which   the   Council   deems   necessary   and   proper   to   the   efficient  and effective administration of the Shop Local Program.  The   Authority   for   this   proposed   Ordinance   is   derived   from   N.J.S.A.   40:48-1,   which  authorizes   the   governing   body   of   every   municipality   in   the   State   of   New   Jersey   to   “make,  amend,   repeal   and   enforce   .   .   .   ordinances,   regulations,   rules   and   by-laws   not   contrary   to   the  laws   of   this   state   or   of   the   United   States,   as   it   may   deem   necessary   and   proper   for   the   good  government,   order   and   protection   of   persons   and   property,   and   for   the   preservation   of   the   public  health, safety and welfare of the municipality and its inhabitants. ”  747869 _ 2

2385-13  AN ORDINANCE AMENDING AND SUPPLEMENTING  CHAPTER II, SECTION 2-9, OF THE REVISED GENERAL  ORDINANCES OF THE TOWNSHIP OF WEST ORANGE  ENTITLED   “ DEPARTMENT OF LAW ” .  BE IT ORDAINED BY THE TOWNSHIP COUNCIL OF THE TOWNSHIP OF  WEST ORANGE,   in the County of Essex, State of New Jersey, that Chapter II, Section 2-9, of  the Revised General Ordinances of the Township of West Orange, entitled   “ Law Department ” ,  shall be amended and supplemented to modify Sections 2-9.1, Township Attorney, 2-9.2, Duties  of the Township Attorney, 2-9.3, Assistant Township Attorney, and 2-9.4, Special Counsel, to  make them consistent with recent practice and best practices going forward with respect to the  operation of the Law Department.  Section 1.   The Revised General Ordinances of the Township of West Orange are amended  and supplemented to read as follows:  CHAPTER II  2-9   DEPARTMENT OF LAW .  2-9.1   Township Attorney  There shall be a Department of Law, the director of which shall be the Township Attorney. The  Township Attorney shall be appointed by the Mayor with the advice and consent of the Council,  need not serve full time, and shall serve during the term of office of the mayor appointing him,  and until the appointment and qualification of his successor, with the manner and amount of  compensation to be approved by the Governing Body. The Department of Law shall consist of  the Township Attorney and such other employees as are hired by the Township Attorney with  approval of the Governing Body, including, but not limited to, an Assistant Township Attorney.  (1972 Code § 2-6.1)  2-9.2   Duties of the Township Attorney.  The Township Attorney shall be the attorney for the Township as a municipal corporation of the  State of New Jersey and he or she shall provide that entity and its officers in their official  capacity with all appropriate legal advice, opinions and services which they may require, at all  times being subject to the laws, regulations, ordinances and rules of the State of New Jersey,  County of Essex, Township of West Orange, and as the same may be interpreted by the courts  having proper jurisdiction.  The duties of the Township Attorney shall include at least the following and may be performed  by him or her personally, or as delegated to one or more subordinates:

a.   Provide legal advice and opinions, whether orally and informally, or in formal written  form, to those Township Officials, elected or appointed, including, but not limited to, the Mayor,  Council, Business Administrator, Chief Financial Officer, or any Department Director,  requesting same in the course of performing their official duties and to ensure that such  performance and the operation of the municipal corporation in the administration of municipal  affairs is lawful and proper;  b.   Attend all meetings of the Township Council and provide legal advice to the Council as  necessary to ensure that the proceedings are conducted in a lawful and proper manner, and to  provide legal advice on all matters of Township business;  c.   Represent the Township in any and all judicial and administrative proceedings, whether  before a Judge, Arbitrator, or Mediator, in which the Township, or any of its officers in their  official capacity, may have an interest, or before the Council sitting as an administrative  authority, including, but not limited to, ABC matters;  d.   Represent the Township in any transactional matters, including, but not limited to, the  purchase and/or sale, or lease of property, real or personal, in which the Township has an  interest;  e.   Draft, or review and approve as to form and sufficiency, all legal documents, contracts,  deeds, ordinances and resolutions made, executed or adopted by or on behalf of the Township;  f.   Enter into any agreement, compromise, or settlement of any litigation in which the  Township is involved, subject to the approval of the Governing Body;  g.   Supervise and direct the work of such additional attorneys, technical and professional  assistants as may be authorized by the Governing Body for regular or special employment by or  for the Township.  (1972 Code § 2-6.2; Ord. No. 288-73 §§ 1-4; New)  2-9.3   Assistant Township Attorney.  Subject to Governing Body approval, there shall be an Assistant Township Attorney appointed  by the Township Attorney, who shall supervise same and delegate and assign those of his duties,  or portions of them, which in the Township Attorney ’ s judgment will contribute to and facilitate  the efficient and effective operation of the Law Department. The Assistant Township Attorney  shall be authorized to perform all of the duties of the Township Attorney in the event of his or  her absence or unavailability, and subject to the Township Attorney ’ s delegation of authority to  avoid any duplication of services. The Assistant Township Attorney need not serve full time and  shall be subject to annual reappointment with the manner and amount of compensation to be  approved by the Governing Body. (1972 Code § 2-2-6.4)

2-9.4   Special Counsel.  Subject to Governing Body approval, the Township Attorney may appoint such Special Counsel  as may be required or advisable to handle matters in which a conflict of interest precludes  representation by the Township Attorney or Assistant Township Attorney, or which due to its  complexity, special nature, time demands or constraints, or for any good reason, would benefit  from the special experience and/or training of the Special Counsel to be hired. Such Special  Counsel need not serve full time and would be expected to serve for the duration of the matter  for which he or she was appointed, subject to annual reappointment with the manner and amount  of compensation to be approved by the Governing Body. Special Counsel would be subject to  the supervision of the Township Attorney who would monitor the work, review and approve the  bills of all Special Counsel. (1972 Code § 2-6.4)  Section 2.   The remainder of Chapter II and Section 2-9, remain unchanged.  Section 3.   If any part of this Ordinance is determined to be invalid, such part shall be  severed and its invalidity shall not affect the remaining parts of this Chapter.  Section 4.   Any and all parts of ordinances which are inconsistent with any of the terms and  provisions of this Ordinance shall be and the same are hereby repealed as to and to the extent of  such inconsistency.  Section 5.   This Ordinance shall take effect upon final passage and publication as required by  law.  _____________________________________  Susan McCartney, Council President  ______________________________________  Robert D. Parisi, Mayor  Approved as to form and legality on the  basis of the facts provided.  ______________________________________  Karen   J.   Carnevale, Municipal Clerk  First Reading:   September 17, 2013  Second Reading:   October 1, 2013  2385-13

LEGISLATIVE HISTORY  The Law Department section of the Administrative Code is being modified to more  accurately set forth the manner in which it has been operated for the past decade or more  and to promote and provide for best practices as recommended by the Office of the NJ  State Comptroller.
