154-13  August 20, 2013  RESOLUTION   SUPPORTING THE DELAYING OF DRAINING THREE (3)  RESERVOIRS ON GARRET MOUNTAIN AND REPLACING THEM WITH  LARGE CONCRETE TANKS, UNTIL UPDATED RULES ARE RELEASED BY  THE ENVIRONMENTAL PROTECTION AGENCY  WHEREAS,   in   response   to   a   federal   Environmental   Protection   Agency   mandate   to   cover  all   treated   drinking   water   supplies,   the   Passaic   Valley   Water   Commission   is   planning   on  draining   the   three   (3)   reservoirs   on   Garret   Mountain   and   replacing   them   with   large  concrete tanks; and  WHEREAS,   the   reservoirs   are   the   Levine,   New   Street   and   the   Great   Notch,   which  happens to form about half of the western border of Rifle Camp County Park; and  WHEREAS,   construction   of   the   tanks   would   mar   the   landscape   of   the   immediate   area,  which   straddles   Paterson   and   Woodland   Park   and   would   cost   upwards   of   $100   million;  and  WHEREAS,   the cost would be passed on to consumers; and  WHEREAS,   being that local residents have been drinking this water for decades; and  WHEREAS,   lawsuits   filed   against   the   mandate   by   New   York   City   and   Portland,   Oregon,  have   spurred   the   Environmental   Protection   Agency   (EPA)   to   revisit   this   requirement,  known as LT2 and the EPA is taking until 2016 to release an updated version; and  WHEREAS,   the   Environmental   Protection   Agency   has   also   granted   New   York   City   an  extension for one particularly expensive reservoir replacement project; and  WHEREAS,   based   on   this   information,   the   Passaic   County   Planning   Department   would  like   to   see   the   Passaic   Valley   Water   Commission   delay   movement   on   the   construction  until the updated rules are released and less harmful solutions are explored; and  WHEREAS,   these   three   sites   about   residential   neighborhoods   and   the   introduction   of  tanks   would   render   them   industrial   in   look   while   also   raising   the   residents’   water   rates;  and  WHEREAS,   the   Township   Council   of   the   Township   of   West   Orange   ar e   desirous   of  demonstrating   their   support   for   the   delay   on   the   project,   until   the   updated   rules   are  released; and  WHEREAS,   this   matter   was   discussed   at   the   Regular   Meeting   of   the   Governing   Body   of  the   Township of West Orange ;

NOW,   THEREFORE,   BE   IT   RESOLVED   that   the   Township   C ouncil   of   the   Township  of   West   Orange ,   County   of   Essex ,   State   of   New   Jersey   hereby   urges   the   delay   in  movement   on   the   tank   construction   project,   until   the   updated   rules   are   released   by   the  Environmental Protection Agency; and  BE   IT   FURTHER   RESOLVED   that   a   copy   of   this   resolution   shall   be   forwarded   to   all  New Jersey representatives.  Susan McCartney, Council President   Karen J. Carnevale, Municipal Clerk  Adopted: August 20, 2013

155-13  August 20, 2013  RESOLUTION  WHEREAS,   the   Township   of   West   Orange   owns   and   operates   a   recycling   center   located   at  570 Mt. Pleasant Avenue, Township of West Orange (the   “ Site ” ); and  WHEREAS,   the   Site   operates   pursuant   to   an   exemption   issued   by   the   State   of   New   Jersey  Department of Environmental Protection ( “ DEP ” ); and  WHEREAS,   pursuant   to   Request   for   Proposal,   the   Township   has   entered   into   a   License  Agreement   (the   “Agreement”)   with   Reliable   Wood   Products,   LLC   of   One   Caven   Point   Avenue,  Jersey City, New Jersey (the   “ Reliable ” ); and  WHEREAS,   the   agreement   requires   Reliable   to   operate   the   Site   in   compliance   with   all  applicable   federal,   state,   county   and   Township   regulations,   including   the   New   Jersey   Department   of  Environmental Protection ( “ NJDEP ” ) Regulations; and  WHEREAS,   Reliable   agreed   to   a   liquidated   damages   provision   entitling   the   Township   to  assess   against   Reliable   damages   of   $5,000   for   each   incident   that   the   Township’s   Environmental  Compliance   Officer   found   that   Reliable   received   vegetative   material   in   excess   of   the   amount  permitted   under   the   limited   NJDEP   approval   of   the   Recycling   Center,   which   is   7,500   cubic   yards   of  unprocessed   material   and   7,500   cubic   yards   of   processed   material   ( See   N.J.A.C.   7:26A-1.4(a)(3));  and  WHEREAS,   in   August   2011,   the   Township   and   surrounding   areas   were   subject   to   Hurricane  Irene; and  WHEREAS,   in   October   2011,   the   Township   and   surrounding   areas   were   subject   to   a  massive ice storm which created various quantities of debris and tree items; and  WHEREAS,   on   October   29,   2012,   the   Township   and   surrounding   areas   were   subject   to  Hurricane Sandy; and  WHEREAS,   these   three   (3)   major   weather   events   caused   the   Site   to   receive   a   significant  amount of vegetative material in excess of what is allowed by the NJDEP; and  WHEREAS,   due   to   the   severe   weather   events,   the   NJDEP   allowed   the   Site   to   temporarily  take on vegetative material in excess of what is allowed under   N.J.A.C.   7:26A-1.4(a)(3); and

WHEREAS ,   The   NJDEP   required   the   Site   to   have   all   of   the   material   ground   into   a   first   grind  state by March 1, 2012 and have all storm related materials removed no later than July 1, 2012; and  WHEREAS ,   notwithstanding   various   reminders   by   Wayne   DeFeo,   the   Township  Environmental Compliance Officer ,   and attempts by Reliable, the deadlines were not achieved; and  WHEREAS ,   Reliable   has   since   spent   approximately   $5,592,000   to   purchase   equipment   to  enable   it   to   process   large   quantities   of   vegetative   material   faster   in   an   attempt   to   avoid   having  excess material on site, and all such equipment is available to be used at the   Site ; and  WHEREAS ,   Reliable   is   now   in   compliance   with   the   amount   of   material   permitted   under   the  limited NJDEP exemption for the   Site ; and  WHEREAS ,   in   order   to   amicably   resolve   the   issues   above   without   the   need   for   litigation,  both   the   Township   and   Reliable   intend   to   memorialize   the   release   and   resolution   of   these   issues   by  execution   of   a   Stipulation   of   Settlement   in   the   form   annexed   hereto   as   Attachment   “A”   (the  “ Stipulation ” ); and  WHEREAS ,   pursuant   to   the   Stipulation,   Reliable   has   agreed   to   pay   to   the   Township  $5,000.00   in consideration of the   non-compliance   described herein ;  NOW,   THEREFORE   BE   IT   HEREBY   RESOLVED ,   by   the   Township   Council   of   the   Township  of   West   Orange   that   the   Township   approves   the   settlement   of   Reliable’s   non-compliance   upon  payment   of   $ 5 ,0 00   plus   any   additional   fees   and   expenses   due   to   the   Environmental   Compliance  Officer;   and it   is   further  RESOLVED   that   notice   of   Resolution   shall   be   published   and   available   in   the   Clerk’s   Office  for reasonable inspection in accordance with applicable law.  ________________________________  K aren   J.   Carnevale   Susan McCartney  Township Clerk   Council President  Adopted: August 20, 2013

156-13  August 20, 2013  RESOLUTION  WHEREAS,   the   Township’s   Refuse   and   Recycling   Collection   Service   Contract   is  scheduled   to expire on September 8, 2013; and  WHEREAS,   on   April   25,   2013   public   notice   was   given   of   bid   specifications   for   a  new contract ;   and  WHEREAS,   t he   Solid   Waste   Advisory   Committee   reviewed   the   bid   specifications  and   has   supervised   the   bidding   and   bid   award   procedure s   in   cooperation   with   the  Purchasing Agent;   and  WHEREAS ,   on   the   due   date   and   time   for   submission   of   bids   for   this   Contract,  July   23,   2013 ,   two   bids   were   received   and   opened   in   the   Township   Municipal   Building;  and  WHEREAS ,   Suburban   Disposal,   Inc.   submitted   the   lowest   financial   bid   with  regard to Option #1 for Refuse and Recycling Collection Services;   and  WHEREAS ,   Joseph   Smentowski,   Inc./Galaxy   submitted   the   2 nd   lowest   financial  bid for Option #1 for Refuse and Recycling Collection Services;   and  WHEREAS ,   the   Township   has   reviewed   the   two   bids   submitted   and   has  determined Suburban Disposal, Inc. to be the lowest responsible bidder;   and  WHEREAS ,   the   Township   has   further   determined   that   the   bid   submitted   by  Joseph   Smentowski,   Inc./Galaxy,   was   not   only   considerably   higher   than   the   bid  submitted   by   Suburban   Disposal,   Inc.,   but   that   it   was   also   not   a   responsive   bid   in   that   a  required form titled,   “ Disclosure of Investment Activities in Iran ”   was not submitted; and  WHEREAS ,   the   Solid   Waste   Advisory   Committee   recommends   awarding   a  contract for the following:  OPTION #1  Item   #1   –   Refuse   Collection   Service   for   residential   sources   two   times   per   week   in   three  zones.  Item   #2   –   Additional   Refuse   Collection   Service;   municipal   sources,   limited   commercial  collection   two   times   per   week   in   three   zones,   bulky   trash   collection   for   residential  sources   one   time   per   week   in   three   zones,   litter   baskets   two   times   per   week   in   three  zones   and   four   times   per   week   in   designated   zones,   refrigerants   and   electronic   waste  one time per week   o n a call in basis to the contractor.  Item   #4   –   Recycling   Collection   Service   to   residents,   municipal   sources   and   limited  commercial   collection   one   time   per   week   in   three   zones   utilizing   the   single   stream  method. No commercial cardboard collection.  OPTION #4   –   Rental of Compactor Truck  OPTION #   5   –   Refuse   Container Mechanical Pick Up

NOW   THEREFORE,   BE   IT,   AND   HEREBY   IT   IS,   RESOLVED by   the   Township  Council   of   the   Township   of   West   Orange   that   the   Refuse   and   Recycling   Collection  Contract   effective   September   9,   2013   for   Option   #1   Items   #1,   #2   and   #4   and   Option   #4  and   Option   #5,   all   as   set   forth   at   length   and   with   specificity   in   the   bid   specifications   and  the   response   thereto   submitted   by   Suburban   Disposal,   Inc.,   54   Montesano   Road,  Fairfield,   NJ   07004   is   awarded   to   Suburban   Disposal   Inc.   for   a   period   of   five   (5)   years;  and it is further  RESOLVED,   that   the   Mayor   and   Municipal   Clerk   are   hereby   authorized   to  execute   and   attest   to   the   execution   of   all   appropriate   documents   to   effectuate   the   award  of this bid as   referred to   herein; and it is further  RESOLVED,   that   the   Municipal   Clerk   is   hereby   directed   to   cause   notice   of   this  award   to   be   published   in   accordance   with   applicable   law   and   shall   maintain   a   copy   of  the Contract on file for public inspection.  Karen J. Carnevale, Municipal Clerk   Susan McCartney, Council President  Adopted: August 20, 2013  I hereby certify funds available from   01-2010-00-8902-140  John O. Gross, Chief Financial Officer

156-13  THIS AGREEMENT , entered into this   day of   , 20 13  between   SUBURBAN DISPOSAL, INC.  (State whether a corporation, partnership, joint venture)  hereinafter   referred   to   as   the   CONTRACTOR ,   and   the   Township   of   West   Orange,   a  municipal corporation of New Jersey, hereinafter referred to as the Township.  WITNESSETH:  For   and   in   consideration   of   the   amount   expressed   hereinafter,   the   parties   agree   as  follows:  1. It is understood that the Township has awarded the Bid in question and the within  Contract pursuant to   Option#1 Items #1, #2 and #4 and Option #4 and Option #5.  2.   The   Contractor   will   furnish   refuse and   recycling   collection   and   disposal   services  to   the   Township   of   West   Orange,   New   Jersey,   for   a   period   of   Five   (5)   years  commencing   September   9,   2013   in   accordance   with   the   Specifications   entitled   "Refuse  And   Recycling   Collection   Service,   Township   of   West   Orange",   dated   April   2013,   and  Proposal   which   are   attached   hereto   and   made   a   part   hereof   as   if   same   were   recited   at  length;   and   the   Contractor   agrees   faithfully   and   promptly   to   comply   with   the   terms   of  said   Specifications   and   to   meet   and   perform   the   obligations   therein   imposed   upon   the  contractor.  3.   The   Township   agrees   to   pay   to   the   Contractor   for   the   aforesaid   period   of Five   (5)  years   the   sum   stated   in   schedule   attached   forming   part   of   said   specifications   attached  hereto   and   made   a   part   hereof,   same   to   be   paid   monthly   as   more   particularly   set   forth   in  the   Specifications   above   referred   to   and   the   proposal   attached   hereto.   In   addition   to  said   compensation,   the   Township   agrees   to   pay   the   Contractor   certain   compensation  based   on   certificates   of   occupancy   issued   subsequent   to   the   commencement   of   work  under   this   Contract   at   the   rate   set   forth   in   said   Specifications   per   month   for   each   new  unit.  4.   The   parties   to   this   Contract   do   hereby   agree   that   the   provisions   of   N.J.S.A.   10:2-1  through   10:2-4,   dealing   with   discrimination   in   employment   on   public   contracts,   and   the  Rules   and   Regulations   promulgated   pursuant   hereunto,   are   hereby   made   a   part   of   this

Contract and are binding upon them.  IN   WITNESS   HEREOF ,   the   parties   have   hereunto   set   their   hands   and   seals,   or   have  caused   these   presents   to   be   signed   by   their   proper   corporate   office   and   their   corporate  seals affixed hereto, the day and year first above written.  .  Attest:   BY:  John Roselle, President  Suburban Disposal, Inc.  Attest:   BY:  Township of West Orange, a   M unicipal  Corporation in the County of Essex and  State of New Jersey.  Karen J. 157-13  August 20, 2013  R E S O L U T I O N  WHEREAS,   on November 22, 2012 the Township Council by Resolution No. 207-12  awarded a contract for the project 2012 Street Improvements, Phase I, Various Streets to the  lowest responsive bidder, A & J Contractors Group, 105 Federal Road, Monroe Township, New  Jersey in the amount of $422,145.00 of which $341,352.00 was funded by Community  Development Block Grants; and  WHEREAS,   the bid and contract were based on unit prices; and  WHEREAS,   the streets in this project included Erwin Place, Kling Street, Liberty Street  and one block of Ridge Avenue between Franklin Avenue and Whittlesey Avenue; and  WHEREAS,   A & J Contractor ’ s Group, Inc. proceeded with and completed the  improvements associated with this project; and  WHEREAS,   based on the unit prices, the final contract amount is $421,317.30 or a  reduction of $827.70 in the original contract amount; and  WHEREAS,   the Township Engineer by letter dated August 13, 2013 recommends  Change Order No. 1 and Final for this project be approved which represents a 0.20 percent  decrease over the award amount of $422,145.00; and  WHEREAS,   the Township Council concurs in the foregoing  NOW THEREFORE BE IT RESOLVED   by the Township Council of the Township of  West Orange that Change Order No. 1 and Final in the amount of - $827.70 for the project 2012  Street Improvements-Phase I, Various Streets with A & J Contractor ’ s Group, Monroe Township,  New Jersey be approved bringing the final contract amount to $421,317.30 or a 0.20 percent  decrease in the award amount.  Karen Carnevale, Municipal Clerk   Susan McCartney,   Council President  I hereby certify funds are available from:  ACCOUNT NO:  John Gross, Chief Financial Officer

#1975041 v3  109824-80036  158-13  August 20, 2013  R ESOLUTION TO AGREE TO JOIN OTHER LOCAL UNITS OF  GOVERNMENT TO FORM A JOINT HEALTH INSURANCE FUND  WHEREAS,   N.J.S.A.   40A:10-36   et seq.,   and the regulations promulgated thereunder   at  N.J.A.C.   11:15-2.1,   et seq.,   provide for the establishment and operation of Joint Insurance Funds  for local government units, including Joint Insurance Funds for Group Health Benefits, all of  which must be reviewed and approved by the State of New Jersey and which must comply with  the Local Fiscal Affairs Law and the Local Public Contracts Law, as well as the statutes and  regulations specifically governing them; and  WHEREAS,   the   Mayor and his Administration, cognizant of the ever increasing costs of  providing health care to the Township   of West Orange ’ s   employees   and its impact on the  Township ’ s budget, desired   to explore the potential benefits of establishing such a Joint  Insurance   Fund   for Group Health   Benefits, also known as a Joint Health Insurance Fund,   with  other local government units   to take advantage of the economic and service benefits they may  provide; and  WHEREAS,   to that end,   a preliminary investigation indicates that the re   may be  advantages, economic and otherwise, to the Township participating in the formation of a Joint  Health Insurance Fund ; and  WHEREAS ,   having concluded from the aforesaid   investigation   that the establishment of  a Joint Health Insurance Fund   may yield benefits to the Township ,   the Mayor seeks the support  and involvement of the Township Council   in establishing   a Joint Health Insurance Fund ;  NOW, THEREFORE, BE IT, AND HEREBY IT IS, RESOLVED BY THE TOWNSHIP  COUNCIL OF THE TOWNSHIP OF WEST ORANGE, THAT:  1.   The   Township of West Orange   may   join together with other   local units of government to  file an application to   establish a Joint Health Insurance Fund;  2.   T h e   obligation of the Township of West Orange to join in the establishment of said Joint  Health Insurance Fund   is conditioned upon the subsequent receipt, review and express  approval by the   Mayor and Township C oun cil   in accordance with   N.J.A.C . 11:15-3.3 and  N.J.A.C.   11:15-3.6,   of   the   Member Agreement   and By-laws   of the   Joint Health Insurance  Fund   and, that if said Member Agreement and By-laws are not received, reviewed and  expressly approved by the Mayor and Township Council, the Township of West Orange  shall be under no obligations to take any further action whatsoever in connection with the  establishment of a Joint Health Insurance Fund ;

#1975041 v3  109824-80036  3.   T he obligation of the   Township of West Orange   to actually join in the establishment of  said Joint Health Insurance Fund is   further   expressly conditioned on the approval by the  New Jersey Department of Banking and Insurance of   said   application for the  establishment of such a Joint Health Insurance Fund and, that, if the New Jersey  Department of Banking and Insurance does not approve said application, the   Township of  West Orange   shall be under   no   obligation to take any further action whatsoever in  connection with the establishment of   a   Joint Health Insurance Fund;  4.   The Member Agreement and By-laws to be approved by the   Mayor and Council of the  Township of West Orange   shall be   executed by a duly authorized official of   the  Township of West Orange , and   such agreement shall   include   (but not be limited to)   the  following terms:  a.   The extent of participation by   the Township of West Orange   in the contemplated  Joint Health Insurance Fund is limited to the self funding of health benefits, and;  b.   The extent of membership in the Joint Health Insurance Fund shall be for an  initial term of no more than three years , in compliance with N.J.S.A. 40A:11-  15(6) and N.J.A.C. 11:15-3.10, and;  c.   That as a condition of participation in the contemplated Joint Health Insurance  Fund, that each Fund member   attest that it   has never defaulted on claims if self-  insured, and if not self-insured, ha s   not been cancelled for non-payment of  insurance premiums for a period of at least two years prior to application.  _______________________________  Susan McCartney, Council President  _______________________________  Karen Carnevale, Municipal Clerk  Adopted: August 20, 2013

pages.text: 160-13  August 20, 2013  RESOLUTION  Third Party Billing for Emergency Transport  WHEREAS , the Township of West Orange   through the West Orange Fire  Department   provide emergency ambulance transport service to the residents, visitors and  travelers in West Orange; and  WHEREAS,   the providing of such services by the Fire Department generates a  substantial annual cost   which can be off set by cha rging the medical insurance companies  a user fee for ambulance transport services; and  WHEREAS ,   this bid was advertised as a competitive contract pursuant   New  Jersey Local Contract Law N.J.S.A. 40A:11-4.1 and at the said   date, time and place   the  Township did receive three (3)   bids ;   and  WHEREAS ,   a committee was formed to evaluate and rank said bids to select the  most advantageous company   to collect these charges   for the Township; and  WHEREAS ,   Revenue Guard ranked the most advantageous company to collect  said billable services and Township committee   verified   that the successful bidder   is both  responsive and responsible   with the bid specifications ;   and  WHEREAS , the commission rate for Revenue Guard is as follows:  5%   $ 0   up to   $101,000.00  10%   $101,001.00   up to   $201,000.00  12.5%   $201,001.00   up to   $301,000.00  15%   $301,001   up to   $1,000,000.00 plus  NOW, THEREFORE BE IT RESOLVED   by the Municipal Council of the  Township   of West Orange   that   a contract be awarded to   Revenue Guard, 50   Route 9 North,  Morganville , New   Jersey 07751 .  BE IT FURTHER RESOLVED   that a copy of this resolution be published in the  West Orange Chronicle within ten (10) days of adoption.  Karen   J.   Carnevale, Municipal Clerk   Susan McCartney, Council President  Adopted: August 20, 2013

{00771845.DOC}  164-13  August 20, 2013  RESOLUTION OF NEED OF THE TOWNSHIP OF WEST ORANGE  REGARDING THE RESIDENTIAL DEVELOPMENT OF BLOCK 9,  LOTS 1, 7, 44, 50 AND 56  WHEREAS,   Valley   Road   Residential,   LLC   (hereinafter   referred   to   as   the   “Sponsor”)  proposes   to   construct   the   Valley   Road   Residential   housing   project,   a   100   family   rental   unit  project   for   moderate   income   households   (hereinafter   referred   to   as   the   “Project”)   pursuant   to   the  provisions   of   the   New   Jersey   Housing   and   Mortgage   Finance   Agency   Law   of   1983,   as   amended  ( N.J.S.A.   55:14K-1   et   seq.),   the   rules   promulgated   thereunder   at   N.J.A.C.   5:80-1.1   et   seq.,   and  all   applicable   guidelines   promulgated   thereunder   (the   foregoing   hereinafter   collectively   referred  to   as   the   “HMFA   Requirements”)   within   the   Township   of   West   Orange   (hereinafter   referred   to  as   the   “Municipality”)   on   a   site   described   as   Lots   1,   7,   44,   50   and   56   in   Block   9   as   shown   on   the  Official   Tax   Map   of   the   Township   of   West   Orange,   Essex   County,   and   commonly   known   as   the  Harvard   Press   site,   located   at   9,   15   Mitchell   Street   and   22-26   Central   Avenue,   West   Orange,  New Jersey; and  WHEREAS,   the   Project   will   be   subject   to   the   HMFA   Requirements   and   the   mortgage  and   other   loan   documents   executed   between   the   Sponsor   and   the   New   Jersey   Housing   and  Mortgage Finance Agency (hereinafter referred to as the   “ Agency ” ); and  WHEREAS,   pursuant   to   the   HMFA   Requirements,   the   governing   body   of   the  Municipality hereby determines that there is a need for this housing project in the Municipality.  NOW   THEREFORE,   BE   IT   RESOLVED   by   the   Township   Council   of   the   Township  of West Orange (the   “ Council ” ) that:  1.   The   Council   finds   and   determines   that   the   Valley   Road   Residential   housing  project proposed by the Sponsor meets or will meet an existing housing need; and  2.   The   Council   does   hereby   adopt   the   within   Resolution   and   makes   the  determination   and   findings   herein   contained   by   virtue   of,   pursuant   to,   and   in  conformity   with   the   provisions   of   the   HMFA   Law   to   enable   the   Agency   to  process the Sponsor ’ s application for Agency funding to finance the Project.  RESOLVED,   that   a   copy   of   this   resolution   shall   be   ma intained   in   the   Municipal   Clerk’s  Office.  Karen J. Carnevale, Municipal Clerk   Susan McCartney, Council President  Adopted: August 20, 2013

165-13  August 20, 2013  R E S O L U T I O N  WHEREAS,   by Resolution No. 126-12, dated June 26, 2013, the Township Council awarded a  contract to Sanitary Construction Company, 415 Franklin Avenue, Rockaway, New Jersey 07866 in the  amount of $3,483,873 for Llewellyn Park Roadway and Sanitary Sewer Improvements; and  WHEREAS,   these improvements are being made pursuant to Ordinance No. 2323-11 which  authorized the Township to make the improvements and assess the benefitting property owners for their  cost; and  WHEREAS,   the Township by Ordinance No. 2358-12 appropriated $4,184,305.00 for this  project of which $500,000 consisted of a cash deposit by the Llewellyn Park Committee of Managers; and  WHEREAS,   by letter dated August 8, 2013 the Llewellyn Park Committee of Managers  requested additional funds in the amount of $348,387.00 be authorized to allow additional roadway and  sanitary sewer improvements in Llewellyn Park; and  WHEREAS,   by letter dated August 14, 2013 the Municipal Engineer recommends a Change  Order be issued in the amount of $348,387.00 for this additional work; and  WHEREAS,   sufficient funds exist for this Change Order; and  WHEREAS,   the Township Council concurs in the foregoing.  NOW THEREFORE BE IT RESOLVED   by the Township Council of the Township of West  Orange that Change Order No. 1 for the contract Llewellyn Park Roadway and Sanitary Sewer  Improvements between Sanitary Construction Company, 415 Franklin Avenue, Rockaway, New Jersey  07866 and the Township of West Orange which authorizes additional funding in the amount of  $348,387.00 for additional roadway and sanitary sewer improvements in Llewellyn Park is approved.  BE IT FURTHER RESOLVED   that the Municipal Engineer is authorized to execute the  Change Order.  ________________________________   _____________________________________  Karen J. Carnevale, Municipal Clerk   Susan McCartney, Council President  Adopted: August 20, 2013  I hereby Certify funds are available from:   Account No. 03-2358-12-0010-010  ________________________________________  John O. Gross, Chief Financial Officer

166-13  August 20, 2013  R E S O L U T I O N  WHEREAS,   by Resolution No. 201-12 dated November 27, 2012 the Township awarded  a contract to Reivax Contracting Corporation, 46 Washington Street, Harrison, New Jersey  07029 for the Improvement of Belle Terre Road and Roosevelt Avenue in the amount of  $482,855.60; and  WHEREAS,   construction commenced in Spring 2013; and  WHEREAS,   due to the construction of Belle Terre Road an adjoining street, Garfield  Avenue, was being used as a detour around the construction area; and  WHEREAS,   due to the increased traffic, the section of Garfield Avenue between Hoover  Avenue and Belle Terre Road, which was in fair to poor condition prior to construction, was now  in poor condition; and  WHEREAS,   the Township Engineer recommended that sections of Garfield Avenue be  improved with new curb, driveway aprons, handicapped ramps and pavement; and  WHEREAS,   the Township Engineer stated the most efficient manner to improve Garfield  Avenue would be approve a Change Order for this work with Reivax Contracting Corporation;  and  WHEREAS,   the Township Engineer estimated the cost of the improvement at $81,400;  and  WHEREAS,   the Local Public Contracts Law allows increases of twenty percent above  the original award amount which totals $96,571.12; and  WHEREAS,   due to contingencies the Township Engineer requested a Change Order be  approved in the amount of $96,571.12 with Reivax Contracting, to perform additional street  improvements on Garfield Avenue from Hoover Avenue south; and  WHEREAS,   the contractor was willing to perform this work at the unit prices bid for the  improvement of Belle Terre Road and Roosevelt Avenue; and  WHEREAS,   sufficient funds existed for this additional work; and

WHEREAS,   the Township Council by Resolution No. 98-13 authorized Change Order  No. 1 in the amount of $96,571.12 bringing the adjusted contract amount to $579,426.72; and  WHEREAS,   during the improvement of Garfield Avenue field conditions were  encountered   which resulted in unanticipated costs for installing concrete sidewalks and  driveway aprons to meet Americans with Disabilities Act (ADA) requirements and unanticipated  costs to make improvements to the base pavement in Garfield Avenue which brought the total  cost to improve Garfield Avenue to nearly $116,000 or about $19,500 more than anticipated with  contingencies; and  WHEREAS,   unanticipated conditions on Belle Terre Road required additional drainage  improvements to prevent runoff from running down driveways as well as additional base  pavement to stabilize the roadway which added just over $10,000.00 to the cost of the project;  and  WHEREAS,   the Township Engineer estimates the total cost of the completed project will  be $609,641.36 or $30,224.64 more than currently authorized through Change Order No. 1; and  WHEREAS,   to date the monies expended for this project totaled $537,851.36; and  WHEREAS,   the Municipal Engineer requests approval of Change Order No. 2 in the  amount of $30,224.64 to complete the final surface course and traffic striping on Belle Terre  Road and Roosevelt Avenue; and  WHEREAS,   with Change Order No. 2 the adjusted contract amount will be $609,641.36  or a 26.26 percent increase over the original contract amount; and  WHEREAS,   the Municipal Engineer recommended these Change Orders as the most  efficient and cost effective manner to improve Garfield Avenue from Belle Terre Road and  Hoover Avenue that would result in a timely improvement that would avoid further deterioration  of Garfield Avenue and that would not conflict with the improvement of Belle Terre Road and  Roosevelt Avenue; and  WHEREAS,   the Township Council concurs in the foregoing; and

NOW THEREFORE BE IT RESOLVED,   by the Township Council of the Township of  West Orange that Change Order No. 2 with Reivax Contracting Corporation, 40 Washington  Street, Harrison, New Jersey 07029 for the Improvement of Belle Terre Road and Roosevelt  Avenue in the amount of $30,224.64 be approved for the additional street improvements on  Garfield Avenue, which adjoins Belle Terre Road, and whose condition had deteriorated as a  result of additional traffic being detoured onto it, to address the additional costs resulting from  field conditions encountered there as well as on Belle Terre Road during the construction.  BE IT FURTHER RESOLVED,   that a notice be placed in the West Orange Chronicle in  accordance with N.J.A.C. 5:30-11.9 since the adjusted contract amount through Change Order  No. 2 of $609,641.36 exceeds the awarded contract amount of $482,855.60 by 26.26 percent.  KAREN J. CARNEVALE, MUNICIPAL CLERK   SUSAN McCARTNEY   , COUNCIL  PRESIDENT  I hereby Certify funds are available from:   Account No. 03-2137-22-5040-010  2013 STREET RESURFACING  $30,224.64  JOHN GROSS, CHIEF FINANCIAL OFFICER

167-13  August 20, 2013  R ESOLUTION AUTHORIZING  COMPETITIVE CONTRACTING PROCESS  FOR   BASIC LIFE SUPPORT SERVICE COMPANY  This resolution will allow the Township to prepare a   r equest for   p roposal in   which the  competitive contracting methodology will be used according to State Statute.   The   proposal  will   be prepared with the ranking of criteria so an award can be made based   on   multiple   factors .  WHEREAS,   the Township   is seeking to secure a contract with a company that can  supply Basic Life Support   Ambulance   Services in response to the Township ’ s 911  Emergency Police Dispatch Communication Center; and  WHEREAS , the cost of these services will exceed the Township bid threshold; and  WHEREAS,   under the Local Public Contracts Law   N.J.S.A.   40A:11-4.1 competitive  contracting may   be used by   local contracting units in lieu of   the standard   public bidding  process   for procurement of specialized goods and services   where   the price exceeds the  bid threshold; and  WHEREAS,   the   T ownship meets the criteria of Local Public Contracts Law  N.J.S.A. 40a:11-4.1 .3   which   allows   the   use of a methodology to rank and evaluate  proposals received to not only secure the best price but the best service to suit the Townships   needs.  NOW   HEREBY BE IT RESOLVED , by the Council of the Township of West Orange ,   th at  Competitive Contract Process shall be i nitiated   for the purpose   of receiving proposal s   for our payroll  processing services   and implemented by the Purchasing   Agent,   Business Administrator ,   and the Chief  Financial Officer   according to the local public contracts law.  Karen   J.   Carnevale   Susan McCartney  Municipal Clerk   Council President  Adopted: August 20, 2013

170 -13  August 20, 2013  RESOLUTION TO HOLD A CLOSED SESSION  WHEREAS , the Open Public Meetings Act (N.J.S.A. 10:4. et seq.) provides for the exclusion of  the public meetings of the governing body during the discussion of certain matters; and  WHEREAS , prior to the exclusion of the public from a meeting of the Township Council it is  required that the Council adopt a Resolution stating the general nature of the subject to be discussed and  stating as precisely as possible the time when the minutes of the discussion conducted in closed session   can  be disclosed to the public; and  WHERE AS , this body is about to consider a matter which falls within the purview of N.J.S.A. 10  -4-12, and can properly exclude the public from such discussions; now, therefore, be it  BE IT   RESOLVED , that the Township Council of the Township of West Orange, now assembled  in public session   at   its regular meeting of   August 20, 2013   shall enter into a closed session to consider the  following matter(s).  ( )   1.   Discussion of any material, the disclosure of which constitutes an  invasion of individual privacy..  (   )   2 .   A collective bargaining agreement or the terms of provisions of same.  ( )   3.   Techniques or tactic utilized to protect the safety and property of the public provided that  their disclosure could impair such protection.  ( )   4.   An investigation of violations o r   possible violations of the laws of the State of New  Jersey   and/ or   the Township of West Orange Administrative Code.  ( X )   5.   Pending or anticipated litigation or contract negotiation in which this body is   or maybe a  party.  ( )   6.   A matter falling within the attorney-client privilege to the extent that confidentiality is  r equired in order for the attorney to   exercise his ethical duties to this body.  ()   7.   Personnel matters.  (   )   8.   Discussion of any matter which by express provision of Federal law or State statute or  rule of Court shall be rendered confidential.  ( )   9.   Discussion   of any matter in which the release of information would impair   a right to  receive funds from the Government of the United Sates.  ( )   10 .   Discussion of any matter involving the purchase, lease or acquisition   of   real property  with public funds, the setting of   banking rates or investment of public funds, where it  could adversely affect the public interest if discussion of such matters were disclosed.  ( )   11.   Deliberations occurring after a public hearing that may result in imposition of a civil  penalty or suspension or loss of a license or permit.

More specifically, the Council   may   discuss in closed session the following topic ( s ) :  Litigation  The Orange Reservoir Tax Case  (the general subject matters) of discussion shall be indicated by a check mark in the appropriate box and  shall be more specifically referred to thereafter to the extent suc h   additional disclosure can be made without  compromising the need for confidentiality giving rise to the closed session.  IT IS FURTH E R RESOLVED , that the minutes of said discussion shall be made public as  soon as the matter under discussion is no longer of a confidential or sensitive nature such that the public  interest will no longer be served by such confidentiality.  The foregoing Resolution was duly adopted by the Township Council of the Township of West Orange at a  public meeting held   on   August 20, 2013 .  Motion:  Second:  Vote  Aye:  Opposed:  Karen J. Carnevale   Susan McCartney  M unicipal Clerk   Council President  Adopted:   August 20, 2013

2380-13  ORDINANCE   OF   THE   TOWNSHIP   OF   WEST   ORANGE ,   IN   THE  COUNTY   OF   ESSEX ,   STATE   OF   NEW   JERSEY   ADOPTING   A N  AMENDMENT TO THE   HARVARD PRESS   REDEVELOPMENT   PLAN  WHEREAS,   the   Local   Redevelopment   and   Housing   Law,   N.J.S.A.   40A:12A-1,   et   seq .,  (the   “Act”)   authorizes   municipalities   to   determine   whether   certain   parcels   of   land   in   the  municipality constitute areas in need of redevelopment; and  WHEREAS,   after   referral   to   and   investigation   by   the   Planning   Board   of   the   Township   in  accordance   with   the   Act,   on   March   18 ,   2008,   the   West   Orange   Township   Council,   acting   by  resolution,   designated   the   following   area   as   an   area   in   need   of   redevelopment   in   accordance   with  the   Act:   Block   9,   Lot   1,   commonly   known   as   9   Mitchell   Street;   Block   9,   Lot   7,   commonly  known   as   15   Mitchell   Street;   Block   9,   Lot   44,   commonly   known   as   22-24   Central   Avenue;   Block  9,   Lot   50,   commonly   known   as   24-26   Central   Avenue;   and   Block   9,   Lot   56,   located   on   Central  Avenue   (collectively,   and   together   with   any   roads,   right-of-ways   or   other   public   lands  encompassed therein or appurtenant thereto, the   “ Redevelopment Area ” ); and  WHEREAS,   on   April   29,   2008,   after   review   of   a   proposed   redevelopment   plan   for   the  Redevelopment   Area   by   the   Planning   Board   of   the   Township   in   accordance   with   the   Act,   and  after   reviewing   the   recommendations   of   the   Planning   Board   with   respect   to   the   same,   the  Township   Council   finally   adopted   Ordinance   #   2168-08   implementing   the   proposed  redevelopment   plan   together   with   the   Planning   Board’s   recommendations,   as   reflected   in   the  Ordinance (the   “ Redevelopment Plan ” ); and  WHEREAS,   acting   by   Resolution   adopted   June   25,   2013,   the   Township   referred   certain  proposed   amendments   to   the   Redevelopment   Plan   as   set   forth   at   Exhibit   A   attached   hereto   (the  “Proposal”)   to   the   Planning   Board   for   its   review   and   comment   in   accordance   with   N.J.S.A.  40A:12A-7 (e) ; and  WHEREAS ,   on   July   9,   2013 ,   the   Planning   Board   conducted   a   public   meeting   regarding  the   Proposal,   recommended   the   adoption   of   the   Proposal   and   resolved   to   transmit   its  recommendation   to   the   Mayor   and   Council   (such   resolution   of   the   Planning   Board   attached  hereto as   Exhibit B ) ; and  WHEREAS ,   the   Township   desire s   to adopt the   Proposal,  NOW,   THEREFORE,   BE   IT   ORDAINED   BY   THE   TOWNSHIP   COUNCIL   OF  THE TOWNSHIP OF WEST ORANGE   AS FOLLOWS :  I.   GENERAL  The aforementioned recitals are incorporated herein as though fully set forth at length.

II.   ADOPTION OF PLAN   AMENDMENT  The   Propos al ,   as   filed   in   the   Office   of   the   Township   Clerk   and   attached   hereto   as   Exhibit  A , is hereby approved .  III.   AMENDMENT OF ZONING ORDINANCE  The   zoning   ordinance   and   map   of   the   Township   is   hereby   amended   to   incorporate   the  provisions of the Propos al .  IV.   SEVERABILITY  If   any   part   of   this   Ordinance   shall   be   deemed   invalid,   such   parts   shall   be   severed   and   the  invalidity thereby shall not affect the remaining parts of this Ordinance.  V.   AVAILABILITY OF THE ORDINANCE  A   copy   of   this   Ordinance   shall   be   available   for   public   inspection   at   the   offices   of   the  Township Clerk.  VI.   EFFECTIVE DATE  This Ordinance shall take effect as provided in law.  ____________________________________  Honorable   Susan McCartney  Council President  ____________________________________  Honorable Robert Parisi  Mayor  Introduced:   July 16, 2013  Adopted:   August 20, 2013  Approved as to form on the basis of the facts provided:  ____________________________________  Karen J. Carnevale, Municipal Clerk

This   use   provision   may  need   to   be   amended   upon   completing   the   research   of   the   remaining   properties   located   in   Block   9  and   along   Central   Avenue. And insert the following language in its place :  Permitted   uses   in   the   MUBR   zone   shall   be   limited   to   multi-family   residential   uses   and   those   uses  allowed in the B-1 zone. 2381-13  AN ORDINANCE OF THE TOWNSHIP OF WEST ORANGE, COUNTY OF ESSEX,  STATE OF NEW JERSEY,  AUTHORIZING THE ESTABLISHMENT OF A  GOVERNMENT ENERGY AGGREGATION PROGRAM  WHEREAS,   the   Government Energy Aggregation Act,   N.J.S.A.   48:3-93.1   et   seq.   governs the  establishment of a government energy aggregation program, which is a government-operated purchasing  cooperative through which multiple energy consumers purchase energy together under the auspices of a  government aggregator; and  WHEREAS,   the New Jersey Board of Public Utilities ( “ BPU ” ) has promulgated rules (N.J.A.C.  14:4-6.1,   et   seq. ) for the implementation of government energy aggregation programs; and  WHEREAS , pursuant to the Government Energy Aggregation Act,   N.J.S.A.   48:3-93.1   et   seq. ,  the Township seeks to establish a Government Energy Aggregation Program ( “ Program ” ) for the  provision of electricity within the Township, for the purpose of obtaining power supply cost savings for  residential and non-residential participants in the Township; and  WHEREAS,   the   West Orange Township Council intends to review and approve a Government  Energy Aggregation Program in the Township and authorize the Mayor and Township personnel under  the authority of the Mayor, consistent with any applicable rules, to solicit proposals and enter into a  contract, subject to review by the Township, for the provision of electric generation services on behalf of  residential customers who do not opt out and non-residential customers that opt in, within the boundaries  of the Township, to the extent that such a contract will produce power supply cost savings to them; and  WHEREAS,   the   Township may from time to time solicit proposals from electric power suppliers  for electric generation services through the Program in which the Township and its authorized personnel  will be lead agents in administering the West Orange Community Energy Aggregation ( “ WOCEA ” ); and  WHEREAS,   the   Township may from time to time issue one or more Request for Proposals for  electric generation services and energy aggregation services on behalf of the WOCEA pursuant to the  Local Public Contract Law, the Government Energy Aggregation Act and the Electric Discount and  Energy Competition Act; and  WHEREAS,   the Township will award contracts for said electric generation service and energy  aggregation services to electric power suppliers that submit qualified price bids lower than the prevailing  price for utility-provided basic generation service; and  WHEREAS , pursuant to applicable BPU rules, the Township needs to enter into an Electric  Distribution Company Aggregation Agreement with Public Service Electric and Gas Company; and  WHEREAS ,   N.J.S.A.   48:3-93.1   et seq.   requires the Program to be established by ordinance;  NOW,   THEREFORE,   BE   IT   ORDAINED   BY   THE   TOWNSHIP   COUNCIL   OF   THE  TOWNSHIP   OF   WEST   ORANGE ,   in   the   County   of   Essex   and   State   of   New   Jersey,   that   there   should  be   created   a   Chapter   2,   Section   93,   of   the   Revised   General   Ordinances   of   the   Township   of   West   Orange,  which shall provide as follows:

TOWNSHIP CODE § 2-93.1  2-93.1:   Program Established.   The Township hereby establishes a Government Energy  Aggregation Program, in accordance with the provisions of the Government Energy Aggregation Act,  N.J.S.A.   48:3-93.1   et   seq. , and the rules promulgated thereunder, N.J.A.C. 14:4-6.1,   et   seq.   The  Township ’ s program may be referred to as the West Orange Community Energy Aggregation, or  “ WOCEA. ”   The Township of West Orange shall be the lead agency and any agreements entered into in  connection with the Program shall be subject to review by the Township.  2-93.2:   Aggregation Agreement.   The Mayor shall be and hereby is authorized to execute and  the Clerk to attest to the execution of an Electric Distribution Aggregation Agreement, in a form  acceptable to the Township, with Public Service Electric and Gas Company ( “ PSE&G ” ).  2-93.3:   Applicability of Rules and Regulations; Solicitation of Proposals.   Pursuant to the  terms and conditions of the Government Energy Aggregation Act and the rules promulgated thereunder,  and specifically N.J.A.C. 14:4-6.2, the Township shall oversee the WOCEA as lead agency   and consistent  with applicable rules, shall solicit proposals for electric generation service and energy aggregation  services on behalf of Township residents who do not opt out and non-residential customers that opt in to  the WOCEA program. The Mayor may execute and enter into a contract for such services provided that  the lowest qualified bid price is below the prevailing price for utility-provided basic generation service,  consistent with any applicable law. Pursuant to the regulations of the Government Energy Aggregation  Act, a public notice of the WOCEA program will be issued whereby non-residential energy consumers  may opt into the WOCEA program, and residential customers may opt out.  2-93.4:   Master Performance Agreement.   The Mayor may also execute a master performance  agreement that obligates the participants in the WOCEA to purchase electricity at terms and conditions  stated therein with a third party supplier that has been awarded the contract by the Township on behalf of  participating members of the WOCEA, and provided that such contract shall be at prices reasonably  forecast and estimated by the Township to provide savings to participants relative to the price charged for  basic generation service by PSE&G.  2-93.5:   Repeal.   All ordinances or parts of ordinances inconsistent herewith are hereby repealed.  2-93.6:   Conflicts.   If any section, subsection, sentence, clause, phrase or portion of this ordinance  is for any reason held to be invalid or unconstitutional by a court of competent jurisdiction, such portion  shall be deemed a separate, distinct and independent provision, and such holding shall not affect the  validity of the remaining portions hereof.  Karen J. Carnevale, Municipal Clerk   Susan McCartney,   Council President  Honorable Robert D. Parisi  Mayor  Introduced: July 16, 2013  Adopted: August 20, 2013

2382-13  AN ORDINANCE AMENDING AND SUPPLEMENTING  CHAPTER III, SECTION 3-5, OF THE REVISED  GENERAL ORDINANCES OF THE TOWNSHIP OF WEST  ORANGE ENTITLED   “ RULES OF COUNCIL ” ,  “ AGENDA ” , AND SECTION 3-8,   “ ORDER OF BUSINESS ”  BE IT ORDAINED BY THE TOWNSHIP COUNCIL OF THE TOWNSHIP OF  WEST ORANGE , in the County of Essex, State of New Jersey, that Chapter III, Section 3-5, of  the Revised General Ordinances of the Township of West Orange, entitled   “ Rules of Council ” ,  “ Agenda ” , shall be amended and supplemented to memorialize certain rules and procedures  regarding the creation and distribution of the Council Agenda, and publication of supporting  materials, and Section 3-8, entitled   “ Order of Business ” , shall be amended and supplemented to  make minor changes to the order of business at regular Council meetings, all as follows:  Section 1.   The Revised General Ordinances of the Township of West Orange are amended  and supplemented to read as follows:  CHAPTER   III RULES OF COUNCIL  3-5   AGENDA.  An agenda for each regular meeting of the Township Council shall be prepared  by the Clerk under the supervision of the President of the Council. All  Ordinances, Resolutions and a ny supporting written material   for consideration at  the next Council Meeting shall be delivered to and received at the Clerk's Office  by 3:00 p.m., on the Wednesday   next   preceding the meeting, earlier if possible  and, if later, shall require permission of the Council President before placement  on the agenda. The agenda headings shall be written in plain English and each  Resolution and Ordinance, unless clearly self-explanatory, shall include a brief  description of its purpose also written in plain English by the department  submitting it. The agenda shall be completed   not   later than   3 p.m.   on the Thursday  preceding each regular meeting of the Council, except for emergency matters  which may be added to the agenda at any time before the meeting with permission  of the President of the Council. The Clerk shall expeditiously deliver a copy of  the Agenda, all Ordinances and Resolutions and all submitted supporting  materials, to each Council member, to the Mayor, Business Administrator, and to  the Law Department. The Clerk shall expeditiously cause the agenda, Ordinances,  and   Resolutions   to be posted on the Township's website no later than the  following Friday   at noon   in order that such materials are readily available to the  general public. In the event of an intervening holiday, the deadlines set forth here  shall be adjusted to provide the same amount of time for preparation and notice of  the agenda. In the event of additions, deletions, or changes of any kind to the  agenda, or any item on it, such changes shall be expeditiously posted to the  Township's website with a notation indicating the change by 4:30 p.m., on the

Monday preceding the Council meeting. The agenda and any changes to it shall  also be physically posted on the Clerk's Bulletin Board in the public hallway at  Town Hall. A conforming agenda shall be published to the Township's website on  the day after each Council meeting to indicate those items which were not acted  upon due to removal or postponement by the Council at its meeting.   (1972 Code  § 3-5; Ord. No. 2349-12).  3-8   Order of Business.  The order of business at a regular meeting of the Council shall be as follows:  Conference Agenda:  Council Liaison Announcements - an opportunity for council members to  announce upcoming events and/or to express appreciation to local  clubs /organizations/volunteers, etc.  Public Agenda:  Pledge of Allegiance;  Roll Call;  Public Comment - Each member of the public wishing to speak shall do so  through the Council President after stating their name and address for the record  and shall be limited to 5 minutes. Each Council Member, including the Council  President, may respond and shall be limited to 5 minutes;  Consent Agenda   1- Approval of Minutes of Previous Meeting  2- Report of Township Officers  3- Reading of Petitions and Communications and Bids  4- Bills  5- Resolutions;  Ordinances on Second and Final Reading;  Ordinances on First Reading;  ABC Hearing, if any;  Pending Matters/New Matters/Council Discussion - Each Council  Member, including the Council President, shall have the opportunity to  raise and discuss any pending matter, new matter, or issue for Council  discussion, each Council Member having 5 minutes to speak and a  representative of the Administration to be permitted 5 minutes to speak in  response, the total time for this section to be no longer than 30 minutes

unless a majority of the Council votes to expand that time;  Adjournment.  Section 2.   The remainder of Chapter III remains unchanged.  Section 3.   If any part of this Ordinance is determined to be invalid, such part shall be  severed and its invalidity shall not affect the remaining parts of this Chapter.  Section 4.   Any   and   all   parts   of   ordinances   which   are   inconsistent   with   any   of   the   terms   and  provisions   of   this   Ordinance   shall   be   and   the   same   are   hereby   repealed   as   to   and   to   the   extent   of  such inconsistency.  Section 5.   This Ordinance shall take effect upon final passage and publication as required by  law.  __________________________________  Susan McCartney, Council President  Approved:  ____________________________________  Robert D. Parisi, Mayor  Approved as to form and legality on the  basis of the facts provided.  ____________________________________  Karen Carnevale, Municipal Clerk  Introduction :   August 20, 2013  Adopted : September   17 , 2013  2382-13

LEGISLATIVE HISTORY  This Ordinance amends the previous version of the Rules of the Council regarding its  Agenda and the Order of Business at regular Council Meetings to make them consistent with the  actual recent practice of the Council and Clerk and the intended practice going forward with  respect to the preparation and publication of its meeting agendas and the conduct of its meetings,  including any meetings of the Council sitting as the Township ’ s ABC Board. On the whole, the  intent is to focus on the business of the Council, its ordinances and resolutions, to discuss and  vote upon them in expeditious fashion as a courtesy to those interested members of the public  who have seen them posted on the Agenda and have come to the meeting out of particular  interest in them, or any of them, and wish to get back to their homes and private matters without  undue delay. Such focus and order of business also serve as a courtesy and cost savings to those  Township Department Directors and professionals, and private professionals, whether working  for the Township or on behalf of a citizen/resident/business owner, who must attend a Council  meeting, or ABC Board meeting, to assist the Township ’ s elected and appointed officials, or a  citizen/resident/business owner who is paying for their presence and professional services, often  on the basis of time spent. The legislative purpose is to provide information to the public which  is likely to be useful to their understanding of the Council business matters to be discussed and  acted upon at the Council meeting, while at the same time respecting their time and that of those  who must attend a Council meeting as part of their employment responsibilities, whether public  or private.

2383-13  AN   ORDINANCE   AMENDING   CHAPTER   2,   SECTION   66 .1a   OF   THE   REVISED   GENERAL  ORDINANCES   OF   THE   TOWNSHIP   OF   WEST   ORANGE,   ENTITLED   “FEES   FOR  SERVICES ”  BE   IT   ORDAINED   BY   THE   TOWNSHIP   COUNCIL   OF   THE   TOWNSHIP   OF   WEST  ORANGE ,   in   the   County   of   Essex   and   State   of   New   Jersey,   that   Chapter   2,   Section   66 .1a   of   the  Revised General Ordinances of the Township of West Orange is hereby amended as follows:  I.   PURPOSE  The   purpose   of   this   Ordinance   is   to   reduce   the   fees   charged   for   public   records   in   order  to ensure   compliance with the Open Public Records Act,   N.J.S.A.   47:1A-1 to -13   (OPRA) .  II.   CHAPTER   2,   SECTION   66.1a   SHALL   BE   AND   HEREBY   IS   AMENDED   AND  SUPERSEDED TO READ AS FOLLOWS:  a.   For   the   issuance   of   photocopies   of   ordinances,   minutes,   resolutions,   and   other  government   records   embodied   in   the   form   of   printed   matter,   except   those   listed  below, the fees shall be :  1.   $0.05 per letter size page or smaller ;   and  2.   $0.07 per legal size page or larger.  However,   i f   a   copy   of   a   police   accident   report   is   requested   other   than   in   person,   an  additional   fee   of   $5.00   shall   be   charged   to   cover   the   administrative   costs   of   the  report.  Further,   if   the   public   records   are   delivered   or   mailed,   there   shall   be   an   additional  charge assessed depending upon the postage or delivery method utilized.  III.   REPEAL OF CONFLICTING ORDINANCES  Any   Ordinances   of   the   Township   in   conflict   with   this   Ordinance   are   hereby   repealed   to  the extent of such conflict.  IV.   SEVERABILITY  If   any   part   of   this   Ordinance   shall   be   deemed   invalid,   such   parts   shall   be   severed   and  the invalidity thereof shall not affect the remaining parts of this Ordinance.

2  V.   EFFECTIVE DATE  The   Ordinance   shall   take   effect   upon   final   passage   and   publication   in   accordance   with  the law.  __________________________________  Honorable   Susan McCartney  Council President  ___________________________________  Honorable Robert D. Parisi  Mayor  Approved as to form on the basis of the facts provided:  _________________________________________  Karen J. Carnevale, Municipal Clerk  Introduced: August 20, 2013  Adopted: September 17, 2013

3  LEGISLATIVE HISTORY  Currently,   Section   66.1a   of   Chapter   2   of   the   Revised   General   Ordinances   of   the  Township   of   West   Orange   provides   for   the   following   fees   for   public   records:   (1)   $0.75   per   page  for   the   first   page   to   the   tenth   page;   (2)   $0.50   per   page   for   the   eleventh   page   to   the   twentieth  page;   and   (3)   $0.25   per   page   for   all   pages   over   twenty.   These   fees   are   derived   from   N.J.S.A.  47:1A-2,   which   is   a   since-repealed   provision   of   the   former   Right   to   Know   Law   (“RKL”),   which  was   part   of   the   Public   Records   Act   of   1963.   The   RKL   was   displaced   by   the   Open   Public  Records   Act,   N.J.S.A.   47:1A-1   to   -13   (OPRA),   in   January   2000.   See   generally   Smith   v.   Hudson  County   Register ,   411   N.J.   Super.   538,   558   (App.   Div.   2010).   The   provision   of   OPRA   applicable  to   fees   for   public   records   is   N.J.S.A.   47:1A-5 b ,   and   it   provides   for   the   following   fees:   (1)   $0.05  per   letter   size   page   or   smaller,   and   (2)   $0.07   per   legal   size   page   or   larger.   This   O rdinance  brings Section 66.1a of Chapter 2 into compliance with OPRA.  In   addition,   the   second   paragraph   of   Section   2-66.1b   currently   provides   that   “for   a   police  accident   report   there   is   an   additional   fee   when   the   request   is   not   made   in   person   of   $5.00   for  the   first   three   (3)   pages   and   $1.00   for   each   additional   page   as   provided   by   N.J.S.A.   39:4-131.”  The   referenced   provision   of   the   State   Motor   Vehicle   Regulations   was   amended   in   September  2010,   and   the   provision   permitting   an   additional   $1.00/page   charge   after   three   (3)   pages   was  removed.   The   additional   charge   for   requests   not   made   in   person   is   now   capped   at   $5.00.  N.J.S.A.   39:4-131.   This   Ordinance   therefore   brings   Section   66.1a   of   Chapter   2   into   compliance  with the State Motor Vehicle Regulations as well.
