TERMS AND CONDITIONS OF LICENSE AND LICENSE APPLICATION  DEFINITIONS  “Township”   shall   mean   the   Township   of   West   Orange,   a   municipal   corporation   of   the  State   of   New   Jersey   with   its   principal   location   at   60   Main   Street,   West   Orange,   New   Jersey  07052  “Shop   Local   Program”   shall   mean   any   program   whereby   Taxpayer   Consumers   make  purchases   at   Participating   Merchants   and   earn   property   tax   credits   to   satisfy   a   Taxpayer  Consumer ’ s   property   tax   obligations   to   the   Township ,   or   earn   credits   for   check   rebates   for  purchases made at Participating Merchants .  “Applicant”   shall   mean   the   entity   seeking   a   license   to   design,   operate   and   administer   a  “Shop   Local”   program   to   stimulate   local   commerce   by   providing   property   tax   credits   or   other  such   rewards   in   connection   with   purchases   by   Taxpayer   Consumers   made   at   Participating  Merchants, as defined herein.  “ Participating   Merchant(s)”   shall   mean   business(es)   with   a   location   in   the   Township ,   or  located   outside   the   Township   but   not   competing   with   any   local   business   within   the   Township,  which   voluntarily   register   and   enter   into   an   agreement   with   Applicant   to   participate   in   the   Shop  Local Program.  “ Taxpayer   Consumer ( s ) ”   shall   mean   Township   property   owners ,   residents ,   residents   of  other   municipalities   who   work   in   the   Township,   or   any   individual   who   voluntarily   registers   and  enters   into   an   agreement   with   the   Applicant   to   participate   in   the   Shop   Local   Program   and  qualifies for receipt of tax credits or rebates .  GENERAL TERMS AND CONDITIONS  This   License   Application   is   authorized   and   governed   by   Chapter   5,   Section   33   of   the  Revised   General   Ordinances   of   the   Township   of   West   Orange   (the   “Ordinance”).   The   terms  and   conditions   set   forth   therein   apply   to   and   shall   be   binding   on   the   Applicant.   Any   additional  terms   and   conditions   stated   herein,   in   addition   to   those   set   forth   in   the   Ordinance,   shall   also  apply to and be binding on the Applicant.  Annual Report  On   or   about   June   1   of   any   calendar   year,   the   Applicant   shall   provide   to   the   Township’s  Chief   Financial   Officer   a   balance   sheet   setting   forth   the   financial   status   of   the   Township   Shop  Local   Program   including,   but   not   limited   to:   (i)   gross   monies   derived   from   the   Township   Shop  Local   Program;   (ii)   revenue   earned   by   the   Applicant   in   connection   with   the   Shop   Local   Program;  (iii)   total   credits   earned   by   Taxpayer   Consumers;   (iv)   a   listing   of   Taxpayer   Consumers,  organized   by   name,   address   and   Block/Lot   number,   who   have   earned   credits   for   patronizing  local   businesses,   and   the   amounts   earned   by   such   Taxpayer   Consumers;   and   (v)   the   total  monies   due   and   owing   to   the   Township   in   connection   with   credits   earned   by   the   Taxpayer  Consumers.

Weekly   Payments to Township by Applicant  The   Applicant   agrees   to   maintain   a   dedicated   bank   account   for   funds   received   in   connection  with   the   Shop   Local   Program,   unless   the   Taxpayer   Consumer’s   property   tax   credit   funds   will   be  immediately   directed   to   and   held   in   the   Township’s   own   FDIC   insured   dedicated   bank   account,  in   which   case   the   Administrator   need   not   establish   such   a   bank   account.   The   Applicant   agrees  to   hold   in   trust   and/or   turn   over   all   funds   collected   from   the   Shop   Local   Program   on   behalf   of  Taxpayer   Consumers,   via   electronic   transfer,   to   the   Township’s   dedicated   bank   account,   on   a  weekly basis.  Payments to   T enants and   N on- R esidents  It   will   be   the   Licensee’s   sole   responsibility   to   hold   in   trust   and   remit   any   and   all   monies   to  be   paid   as   rebates   to   Taxpayer   Consumers   who   do   not   pay   property   taxes   to   the   Township.  This   includes,   but   is   not   necessarily   limited   to,   tenants,   renters,   non-resident   employees,  visitors,   or   other   such   individuals   authorized   to   participate   and   register   in   the   Shop   Local  Program.  Insurance Requirements, and Indemnification and Hold Harmless  As   more   fully   set   forth   in   the   Ordinance,   Applicant   shall   be   required   to   obtain   and   provide  proof   of   commercial/general   liability   insurance   in   the   amounts   stated   in   the   Ordinance,   and  workers’   compensation   coverage   insurance   as   well.   The   Applicant   also   agrees   to   indemnify,  defend,   and   hold   harmless   the   Township,   its   officers,   agents   and   employees,   from   any   and   all  claims,   actions,   liability   or   costs,   including   reasonable   attorneys’   fees   and   other   costs   of  defense,   in   connection   with,   arising   out   of,   or   in   any   way   related   to   the   negligence   of   the  Applicant, or its agents or employees, in the administration of the   Shop Local Program .  Fiduciary   Requirement  As   more   fully   set   forth   in   Section   5-33.10   of   the   Ordinance,   t he   Applicant   shall   provide   to  the   Township,   in   lieu   of   a   cash   or   other   bond,   proof   of   a   valid   fiduciary   policy   naming   the  Township   as   a   loss   payee   in   the   amount   of   $50,000,   which   amount   may   be   amended   from   time  to time by Resolution of the Township Council.  Termination of Program  Applicant   shall   provide   at   least   ninety   (90)   days’   written   notice   to   Participating  Merchants,   Taxpayer   Consumers   and   the   Township   in   the   event   the   Shop   Local   Program   shall  be   terminated   for   any   reason.   In   the   event   of   termination,   Applicant   will   be   responsible   for  transmitting   a   final   data   file   and   bank   wire   transfer   accounting   for   all   outstanding   credits   or  rebates   applicable   to   Taxpayer   Consumers.   The   Bond   Guarantee   and/or   Fiduciary   Policy   may  be   released   following   a   final   accounting   of   all   gross   revenues   and   outstanding   tax   credits,   which  may   be   independently   audited   at   the   request   of   the   Township   and   at   the   sole   cost   and   expense  of the Applicant.

No Subcontractors  The   Applicant   shall   not   be   permitted   to   utilize   any   subcontractors   in   the   performance   of  services   under   any   license   granted   pursuant   to   this   Application ,   unless   the   subcontractor   is  disclosed   on   this   application.   If   the   subcontractor   will   possess   or   hold   in   trust   any   monies   to   be  credited   to   the   Township   on   behalf   of   a   Taxpayer   Consumer,   such   subcontractor   shall   have   a  fiduciary   policy   naming   the   Township   as   a   loss   payee   in   the   amount   stated   in   Section   5-33.10   of  the Ordinance.  Terms of Use of   “ Shop West Orange ”   Phrase  The   Applicant   shall   not   use   the   name   “Shop   West   Orange”   nor   the   graphic   “Shop   West  Orange”   logo   or   any   part   thereof   without   the   written   consent   of   the   Township   Business  Administrator.   If   Applicant   breaches   this   requirement,   said   license   shall   automatically   terminate.  All   rights   to   the   name   “Shop   West   Orange,”   and   the   graphic   “Shop   West   Orange”   logo,   are  reserved to the Township.  Amount of Credits to   Taxpayer   Consumer s  As   more   fully   set   forth   in   Section   5-33.9   of   the   Ordinance,   it   shall   be   the   Applicant’s   sole  responsibility   to   ensure   that   Taxpayer   Consumers   have   access   to   a   written   description   of   the  gross   percentage   of   credits   that   will   be   paid   to   the   Township   towards   Taxpayer   Consumers’   real  property taxes upon a purchase made at all Participating Merchants.  The   Applicant   shall   be   responsible   for   providing   merchants   with   window   clings   stating  the   merchant’s   participation   in   the   program,   the   form   of   which   may   be   approved   by   the  Township’s   Business   Administrator.   Furthermore,   the   Applicant   shall   make   its   best   effort   to  ensure   that   all   Participating   Merchant s   post   the   window   cling   in   its   front   window   or   in   another  conspicuous   location   indicating   that   the   Participating   Merchant   is   a   “ Participating   Member   of  Shop West Orange Property Tax Reward Program . ”  Each   Participating   Merchant   shall   be   responsible   for   making   available   to   Taxpayer  Consumers   patronizing   its   business   a   written   description   of   the   percentage   of   each   transaction  which shall be paid to the Township and/or credited to the Taxpayer Consumer.  Advertising  Applicant   shall   have   the   responsibility   to   ensure   that   the   Shop   Local   Program   is   properly  advertised   to   all   Township   residents.   This   shall   include   at   a   minimum,   but   is   in   no   way   limited  to:   (i)   working   with   Participating   Merchants   to   launch   and/or   maintain   Shop   West   Orange  page(s)   on   the   Applicant’s   web   portal   and   keeping   same   updated;   (ii)   regularly   maintaining   the  Applicant’s   Web   site   to   post   a   list,   with   phone   numbers   and   addresses,   of   all   Participating  Merchants   participating   in   the   Shop   Local   Program;   (iii)   (iii)   providing   Participating   Merchants  with   e-mail   addresses   of   Taxpayer   Consumers   who   have   registered   for   the   Shop   Local   Program  and   have   shopped   at   their   location,   individually;   and   (iv)   making   itself   available   to   the   Township,  its   Chamber   of   Commerce   and   the   Downtown   West   Orange   Alliance   to   discuss   strategies   for  advertising.

Review of Applications by Merchants  The   Applicant   shall   coordinate,   receive   and   review   all   applications   by   local   businesses   to  become   a   Participating   Merchant.   In   the   event   that   the   Applicant   receives   an   application   to  become   a   Participating   Merchant   from   a   business   located   outside   the   Township,   the   Applicant  shall   review   same   and   provide   a   copy   to   the   Township’s   Business   Administrator.   Any   approvals  to   businesses   located   outside   the   Township   shall   be   subject   to   the   review   and   approval   of   the  Township ’ s Mayor, Chief Financial Officer and/or Business Administrator.  Breach of Licensing Terms  In   the   event   of   a   breach   of   any   of   the   terms   and   conditions   of   the   Ordinance   or   the   terms  and   conditions   stated   in   this   Application,   the   Township   reserves   the   right   to   revoke   and/or  suspend   the   Applicant’s   license.   In   the   event   of   termination,   the   Applicant   shall   satisfy   the  requirements set forth in the subsection titled   “ Termination of Program. ”  Business Registration Certification  Along   with   this   Application,   Applicant   shall   provide   to   the   Township   a   copy   of   its   New  Jersey Business Registration Certificate.  QUESTIONS FOR APPLICANT  (Applicant may submit a written response   to these questions   on separate sheets)  1.   Set   forth   the   State   of   incorporation   of   the   business   entity   applying   for   the   license   to   operate  the   Shop   Local   Program,   the   nature   of   the   registration,   (e. g. ,   S-corporation,   partnership,  joint   venture,   etc.),   and   set   forth   the   names   of   any   subsidiaries,   affiliates,   or   parent  companies   of   the   entity.   Also   set   forth   the   principal   place   of   business,   and   any   locations  from which the Applicant operates in the State of New Jersey.  (a)   Provide   the   names   and   business   addresses   of   all   Principals,   managers,   subsidiaries  and   affiliates   of   the   entity   submitting   this   Application.   For   purposes   of   this   license  application,   “Principals”   shall   mean   individuals   possessing   an   equitable   or   legal  ownership   interest   in   the   Applicant,   or   in   any   subsidiary   or   affiliate   of   Applicant.   If   the  Applicant   is   a   corporation,   “Principals"   shall   also   include   each   investor   who   would  have   any   amount   of   operational   control   over   the   Applicant   and   every   stockholder  having an ownership interest of ten percent (10%) or more in the firm.

Name & Title   Address, Phone and Email  (b)   If   the   entity   is   a   partially   owned   or   a   fully-owned   subsidiary   of   another   entity,   identify  the   parent   company   and   describe   the   nature   and   extent   of   the   affiliate’s   approval  rights   over   the   activities   of   the   entity   submitting   this   license   application.   If   applicable,  set   forth   and   describe   the   approval   process.   If   the   Applicant   is   a   partnership   or   a  joint   venture   or   other   such   organization,   provide   the   requested   information   with  respect to each member of the partnership, joint venture or other such organization.  (c)   Provide   proof   or   a   verified   statement   that   the   Applicant   has   complied   with   all  applicable   affirmative   action   (or   similar)   requirements   with   respect   to   its   business  activities.

2.   How many years has the business organization been operating under its present name?  3.   How   many   years   has   the   business   organization   been   under   its   current   management,  including   but   not   limited   to   identifying   the   names   and   service   terms   of   its   Chief   Executive  Officer, Chief Operating Officer, Treasurer/Controller, Managing Partner, etc.  4.   (a) How many employees are employed by the Applicant?  Full Time  Part Time  Contract/Temporary  (b)   If   the   Applicant   is   a   closely   held   corporation   or   partnership   of   fewer   than   twenty-five   (25)  employees,   of   the   above-referenced   employees   what   are   each   employee’s   job   titles   and   job  duties?

5.   Are there any civil judgments entered against, or lawsuits filed against, the Applicant  within   the   last   three   (3)   years?   If   yes,   set   forth   the   nature   of   each   action,   and   the   status  or result of same.  6.   Is   the   Applicant   now   involved   in   any   bankruptcy   or   re-organization   proceedings,   or   has   it  been   involved   in   any   bankruptcy   or   re-organization   proceedings   in   the   last   ten   (10)  years?   If   yes,   set   forth   the   jurisdiction,   the   current   status   of   the   action,   and/or   the   result  of the action.  7.   Provide   three   (3)   references   who   can   attest   to   the   Applicant’s   past   conduct   with   respect   to   a  municipal   Shop   Local   Program   or   similar   municipal   loyalty   or   rewards   programs.   If   the  Applicant   has   no   prior   experience   operating   a   municipal   loyalty   program,   state   that   and  provide   three   (3)   references   who   can   attest   to   the   Applicant’s   prior   business   activities  and conduct.

8.   Set   forth   the   manner   and   means   in   which   the   Applicant   intends   to   administer   the   Shop  Local   Program,   including   but   not   limited   to:   (a)   the   procedure(s)   for   registering   local   m  erchants   and   advertising   the   program   to   same;   (b)   the   manner   in   which   Applicant   will  handle   day-to-day   operations,   such   as   communicating   with   Participating   Merchants   and  municipalities   about   questions   and   concerns,   fixing   breakdowns   of   equipment,   etc.;   and  (c)   generally,   the   extent   to   which   Applicant   is   able   to   operate   under   and   according   to   the  terms   and   conditions   of   the   Ordinance   and   this   Application   (e.g.,   roles   of   key   staff,   24-  hour availability, customer service, etc.).  9.   Describe   the   nature   of   the   goods   to   be   sold   and/or   supplied   to   Participating   Merchants   in  order   to   administer   the   Shop   Local   Program,   including   but   not   limited   to   swipe   card  terminals,   and   computer   access.   Also   describe   the   extent,   if   any,   to   which   the  Applicant’s   Web   site   will   provide   means   for   Participating   Merchants   and   Taxpayer  Consumers to monitor their participation in and access to the Shop Local Program.

10.   Append   to   this   application   a   copy   of   the   Applicant’s   A rticles   of   I ncorporation ,   B ylaws,   or  Operating Agreement, as applicable.  11.   If   there   are   no   Articles   of   Incorporation   or   Bylaws   of   the   Applicant,   set   forth   any   and   all  applicable   governance   procedures   of   the   Applicant.   If   there   are   no   governance  procedures, please state that there are no such procedures in place.

12.   At   its   sole   cost   and   expense,   Applicant   shall   complete   and   submit   to   a   criminal  background   check   through   MorphoTrak,   Inc.   ( see   http://www.bioapplicant.com/nj ),  formerly   known   as   Sagem   Morpho,   Inc.   Applicant   shall   complete   an   approved  MorphoTrak   form   available   from   the   Township   Clerk,   and   will   be   required   to   visit   a  MorphoTrak   office   for   fingerprinting   and   completion   of   the   background   check.   If   the  Applicant   is   a   corporation,   a   criminal   background   check   shall   be   submitted   for   all   officers  and   directors   of   the   corporation,   all   shareholders   owning   more   than   ten   (10)   percent   of  common   stock ,   and   any   other   employee   who   will   be   an   authorized   signatory   on   any  bank   account   where   Taxpayer   Consumers’   monies   will   be   deposited .   If   the   Applicant   is  a   partnership,   each   partner   shall   submit   to   a   criminal   background   check   by  MorphoTrack,   Inc.   No   license   shall   be   issued   to   an   Applicant   until   results   of   the  background check are submitted to the Township.  13.   Append   a   current   financial   statement   listing   all   assets   and   liabilities   for   the   business  entity for the past two (2) years.

14.   Does   the   Applicant   intend   to   use   any   subcontractor(s)   to   perform   any   duties   in  connection   with   administration   of   the   Shop   Local   Program?   If   so:   (i)   disclose   the  corporate   and/or   trade   name(s)   of   all   such   entities   or   individuals;   (ii)   the   location(s)   of   its  operation(s); and (iii) the anticipated scope of services of the subcontractor(s).  740141_2

Township   of West Orange   Page   1   of   6   Reward Programs / MOU  2384-13  MEMORANDUM OF UNDERSTANDING  BETWEEN  TOWNSHIP OF WEST ORANGE AND FINCREDIT, INC.  FOR THE  SHOP WEST ORANGE PROPERTY TAX REWARD PROGRAM  DEFINITIONS  “Township”   shall   mean   the   Township   of   West   Orange,   with   principal   offices   located   at   6 6   Main  Street, West Orange, New Jersey 07052  “Administrator”   shall   mean   Fincredit,   Inc.,   with   a   mailing   address   of   P.O.   Box   43,   Marlboro,   New  Jersey   07746,   and   a   physical   address   at   2271   Rt.   33,   Suite   106,   Hamilton,   New   Jersey   08690.  Fincredit   is   charged   with   implementation   of   the   Shop   West   Orange   Program,   including   entering  into agreements with   Participating   Merchants and   Taxpayer   Consumers.  “ Participating   Merchant(s)”   shall   mean   business(es)   with   a   location   in   the   Township ,   or   located  outside   the   Township   but   not   competing   with   any   local   business,   which   voluntarily   register   and  enter into an agreement with Applicant to participate in the Shop   West Orange   Program.  “ Taxpayer   Consumer ( s ) ”   shall   mean   Township   property   owners,   renters ,   residents   of   other  municipalities   who   work   in   the   Township ,   or   any   individual   who   voluntarily   register s   with   and  enter s   into   an   agreement   with   the   Administrator   and   qualifies   for   receipt   of   tax   credits   or  rebates .  “Property   Tax   Reward   Cards”   shall   mean   Shop   West   Orange   Program   swipe   cards   issued   to  Taxpayer C onsumers for use at   P articipating Merchants.  DESCRIPTION  The   Shop   West   Orange   Property   Tax   Reward   Program   the   ( “Shop   West   Orange  Program” ),   will   utilize   technology   similar   to   that   utilized   in   connection   with   traditional   “gift   and  loyalty   card”   programs   in   addition   to   Administrator   proprietary   software.   The   goal   of   the  program   is   to   provide   Township   taxpayers   with   a   financial   incentive   to   patronize   participating  Township   businesses   in   the   form   of   a   Property   Tax   Reward   or   other   such   rebate ,   and   at   the  same   time   provide   Township-based   businesses   with   a   financial   incentive,   in   the   form   of  increased patronage and revenues, to enroll in the program.  In   summary,   a   Taxpayer   Consumer   who   patronizes   a   P articipating   Merchant   will   receive  a   corresponding   percentage-of-sale   credit   on   his   or   her   property   taxes.   Where   a   non-resident  Taxpayer   Consumer   is   an   employee   in   a   Township   business,   or   where   a   Consumer   is   a  resident who is a tenant in the Township, these   Taxpayer   Consumers may receive a rebate.  The key aspects of the program involve:

Township   of West Orange   Page   2   of   6   Reward Programs / MOU  1.   Program promotion   and marketing;  2.   Participating   Merchant registration ;  3.   Taxpayer   Consumer   registration   via   a   secure   web   portal   through   the  Administrator’s   Web   site,   and   through   issuance   of   Property   Tax   Reward  Cards;  4.   Taxpayer   Consumers patronizing   P articipating   M erchants ;  5.   Taxpayer   Consumers   receiving   a   Property   Tax   Reward   credit   based   upon   a  percent age   of   a   purchase   made   at   a   Participating   Merchant,   as   a   reward   for  patronizing   the   P articipating   M erchant ,   and   where   the   percentage   credit   to   be  awarded   to   the   Taxpayer   Consumer   will   be   chosen   by   the   Participating  Merchant;  6.   Administrator   receiving   a   fee   for   the   cost   of   administering   the   program ,   which  shall   equal   to   twenty-five   ( 25% )   percent   of   the   Property   Tax   Reward   for   each  transaction between a   Taxpayer C onsumer and   P articipating   M erchant ; and  7.   The   Township   receiving   weekly   money   transfers   from   Administrator   in   the  amount of property tax reward credits earned by Taxpayer Consumers.  Administrator Responsibilities  1.   Strict   adherence   to   the   terms   of   the   Township   Reward   Program   ordinance,   and   any   other  applicable Township ordinances.  2.   Program Promotion, including but not limited to:  a.   Assisting   with   the   design   of   the   Property   Tax   Reward   Cards   for   the   Shop   West  Orange P rogram  b.   Assisting   in   the   production   of   “infomercials”   to   be   aired   on   Local   Cable   TV  Channel(s)  c.   Attending   meetings   with   the   Township   or   designated   association(s)   including  program kick offs with local businesses  3.   Entering   into   agreements   with   Participating   Merchants   and   establishing   all   terms   including,  but not limited to:     Responsibility for hardware     Software and training in use of software     Reward card processing     Percentage of transaction credit to   Taxpayer   Consumer     Percentage of transaction credit to Administrator     Incorporating   language   indicating   voluntary   nature   of   program   and   legal  indemnification language

Hosting   a   Web   portal/Web   site   whereby   Administrator   may   register   and   enter   into  agreements with   Taxpayer   Consumers establishing all terms including but not limited to:     A   statement   of   the   percentage   of   a   transaction   credit   which   will   go   to   Taxpayer  Consumer     A statement of the percentage of a transaction credit which will go to Administrator     The timing of posting of credits earned (annually)     Policy regarding data privacy and sharing of information for marketing purposes     Specific limits and restrictions relative to program participation     A   statement   that   a   taxpayer   in   the   Township   must   be   current   on   its   property   taxes   to  participate     No refunds will be issued     Credits   on   accounts   that   are   current   will   result   in   prepayments   of   property   taxes   for  future periods     A   statement   that   the   Administrator   is   not   responsible   for   impact   of   program  participation on mortgage escrow calculations.     A   statement   that   neither   the   Administrator   nor   the   Township   is   responsible   for   credits  with respect to changes in property ownership     Posting   language   indicating   the   voluntary   nature   of   program   and   legal  indemnification language satisfactory to the Association  5. Township   of West Orange   Page   5   of   6   Reward Programs / MOU  IDENTITY OF PARTICIPATING MERCHANTS  The   Applicant   shall   coordinate,   receive   and   review   all   applications   by   local   businesses   to  become   a   Participating   Merchant.   In   the   event   that   the   Applicant   receives   an   application   to  become   a   Participating   Merchant   from   a   business   located   outside   the   Township,   the   Applicant  shall   review   same,   and   any   approvals   shall   be   subject   to   the   review   and   approval   of   the  Township’s   Chief   Financial   Officer   and/or   Business   Administrator,   which   approval   shall   not   be  unreasonably withheld.  DISCLOSURE OF PROGRAM FEES  It is understood that certain fees will be incurred in connection with administration of the  Shop   West Orange   Program, and a listing of such fees shall be submitted with the  Administrator ’ s application for a license to operate the Shop   West Orange   Program. Such fees  are currently as follows:  1.   Costs to   Participating   Merchant:     Hardware   Costs   are   a   $160,   one-time   cost   for   the   dial   up   processing   machine   ($230  dual   comm.   IP).   They   may   also   opt   for   processing   via   the   Administrator’s   Web   site   in  which   case   the   cost   is   $0,   or   $50   if   the   Participating   Merchant   wishes   to   purchase   a  card reader. The cost of equipment is subject to change.     $10 month ly   processing fee.     No   additional   costs   will   apply.   Administrator   agrees   to   waive   its   customary   $0.15  cents per swipe fee.  2.   Other costs :     For   providing   ongoing   services   as   A dministrator   of   the   Shop   West   Orange   Program,  Administrator   will   retain   twenty-five   (25%)   percent   of   the   Property   Tax   Reward   credit  provided by   Participating   Merchants to   Taxpayer   Consumers.     Example:   A   Participating   Merchant   chooses   a   ten   percent   (10%)   property   tax   credit  on   sales.   If   a   homeowner   makes   a   $100   purchase,   creating   a   $10   property   tax   credit,  the   homeowner   will   receive   $7.50   and   the   Administrator   will   retain   $2.50   for   program  expenses and administration fees.

ORDINANCE O-   -13  AN   ORDINANCE   AMENDING   CHAPTER   5   OF   THE   GENERAL   ORDINANCES   OF   THE  TOWNSHIP   OF   WEST   ORANGE,   AND   CREATING   SUBCHAPTER   33,   ENTITLED   “  LICENSING FOR   SHOP LOCAL REWARD PROGRAMS ”  WHEREAS ,   pursuant   to   Resolution   No.   87-13,   the   Township   Council   for   the   Township   of  West   Orange   formalized   its   support   of   a   “Shop   Local”   campaign   to   stimulate   the   patronage   of  local businesses located in the Township;  NOW,   THEREFORE,   BE   IT   ORDAINED   BY   THE   TOWNSHIP   COUNCIL   OF   THE  TOWNSHIP   OF   WEST   ORANGE ,   in   the   County   of   Essex   and   State   of   New   Jersey,   that   there  should   be   created   a   Chapter   5,   Section   33,   of   the   Revised   General   Ordinances   of   the   Township  of West Orange, which shall provide as follows:  5-33 LICENSING FOR   “ SHOP LOCAL ”   REWARD PROGRAMS  5-33.1   Purpose  The   purpose   of   this   Ordinance   is   to   provide   a   uniform   set   of   procedures   for   applications  by   and   licensing   of   an   Applicant   seeking   to   operate   and   administer   a   “Shop   Local”   program  within the Township.  5-33.2   Definitions  As   referred   to   in   this   Chapter   and   all   applicable   subsections,   the   following   terms   shall  have the following meanings:  “Shop   Local   Program”   shall   mean   any   program   whereby   Taxpayer   Consumers   make  purchases   at   Participating   Merchants   and   earn   property   tax   credits   to   satisfy   a   Taxpayer  Consumer’s   property   tax   obligations   to   the   Township ,   or   earn   credits   for   check   rebates   for  purchases made at Participating Merchants .  “Applicant”   shall   mean   the   entity   seeking   a   license   to   design,   operate   and   administer   a  “Shop   Local   P rogram ”   to   stimulate   local   commerce   by   providing   property   tax   credits   or   other  such   rewards   in   connection   with   purchases   by   Taxpayer   Consumers   made   at   Participating  Merchants,   as   defined   herein.   The   term   “Applicant”   may   also   refer   to   a   “Licensee”   after   a  license is awarded to an Applicant pursuant to the terms of this Chapter.  “ Participating   Merchant(s)”   shall   mean   business(es)   with   a   location   in   the   Township ,   or  located   outside   the   Township   but   not   competing   with   any   local   business,   which   voluntarily  register and enter into an agreement with Applicant to participate in the Shop Local Program.  “ Taxpayer   Consumer ( s ) ”   shall   mean   Township   property   owners ,   residents ,   residents   of  other   municipalities   who   work   in   the   Township,   or   any   individual   who   voluntarily   registers   and  enters   into   an   agreement   with   the   Applicant   to   participate   in   the   Shop   Local   Program   and  qualifies for receipt of tax credits or rebates .

2  5-33.3   License Required  No   person   or   entity   shall   operate   a   Shop   Local   Program   within   the   Township   unless   the  entity seeking to operate same is licensed pursuant to the terms of this Ordinance.  5-33.4   Licensing of Applicants  a.   Application   Information .   Applications   for   a   license   to   operate   a   Shop   Local   Program   shall  be   made   to   the   Township   Clerk   on   forms   provided   by   the   Township   Clerk.   The   form(s)   to  be   used   for   the   application   shall   be   approved   by   the   Township   Council   and   m a y   set   forth  additional   requirements,   terms   and   conditions,   other   than   those   enumerated   in   this  Ordinance,   which   the   Township   Council   deems   necessary   and   proper   to   the   efficient   and  effective administration of the Shop Local Program.  Applications   shall   be   verified   by   oath   or   affirmation.   Applications   by   a   corporation   shall  be   verified   by   all   officers   and   directors   and   all   persons   holding   more   than   ten-percent  (10%) of the corporation ’ s common stock, as well as the corporation itself.  b.   Review   of   License .   Upon   receipt   of   the   license   application,   the   Township   Clerk   shall  review   the   application   to   ensure   that   it   has   been   properly   completed.   The   Township  Clerk   shall   thereafter   submit   all   properly   completed   applications   to   the   Township   Council  for determination as to whether to grant or deny the license.  c.   Factors   considered .   In   determining   whether   to   grant   or   deny   the   license   to   the   Applicant,  the   Township   Council   shall   take   into   consideration,   among   any   other   factors   it   considers  relevant, the following:  1.   The Applicant ’ s ability to properly conduct the licensed activity .  2.   The   moral   character   of   the   Applicant,   whether   a   corporate   entity   or   other   such  partnership.   If   a   corporation,   the   Township   Council   shall   consider   the   moral  character   of   each   officer,   director,   manager,   supervisor   and/or   shareholder.   If   a  partnership,   the   Township   Council   shall   consider   the   moral   character   of   each  partner.  3.   The   business   responsibility   of   the   Applicant,   including   but   not   limited   to   community  service work or other such charitable endeavors.  4.   The   criminal   history   of   the   Applicant,   whether   an   entity   or   individual   owner,   director,  manager,   supervisor,   officer   or   shareholder .   A   copy   of   the   Applicant’s   criminal  history   record,   obtained   from   the   New   Jersey   State   Police,   shall   be   submitted   with  the   application.   If   the   Applicant   is   a   corporation,   a   criminal   history   record   from   the  New   Jersey   State   Police   shall   be   submitted   for   all   officers   and   directors   of   the  corporation,   all   shareholders   owning   more   than   ten   (10)   percent   of   common   stock ,  and   any   other   employee   who   will   be   an   authorized   signatory   on   any   bank   account  where   Taxpayer   Consumers’   monies   will   be   deposited .   If   the   Applicant   is   a  partnership,   a   criminal   history   record   from   the   New   Jersey   State   Police   shall   be  submitted for all partners.  5.   Prior   experience   in   the   financial   industry,   although   a   lack   of   prior   experience   shall   not  necessarily be a factor militating against issuance of a license.

3  d.   Issuance   of   License .   If   the   Applicant’s   request   for   a   license   is   approved   by   the   Township  Council,   the   Township   Clerk   shall   issue   a   license   to   the   Applicant   within   ten   (10)   business  days   of   the   approval.   The   contents   of   an   Applicant’s   license   to   operate   a   Shop   Local  Program shall contain the information required pursuant to Section 1.4 of this Chapter.  5-33.5   Term of License  The   first   license   granted   to   an   Applicant   shall   be   for   a   term   of   two   ( 2 )   year s   from   the   date  of   issuance   of   the   license   by   the   Township   Clerk   (the   “Initial   Term”),   after   Township   Council  approval .   After   the   expiration   of   the   Initial   Term ,   the   license   may   be   renewed   for   subsequent  term s   of   three   ( 3 )   years ,   provided   that   Applicant   submits   a   request   for   renewal   to   the   Township  Clerk   in   accordance   with   Section   5-33.7,   and   same   is   approved   by   the   Township   Council.   The  three   (3)   year   term   shall   commence   from   the   date   of   issuance   of   the   license   renewal   by   the  Township Clerk .  �-.6   Application and License Fees  a.   Application   Fee .   In   order   to   submit   an   application   for   a   license   pursuant   to   this  Ordinance,   the   Applicant   shall   remit   a   non-refundable   application   fee   of   $ 50   to   the  Township Clerk. This application fee shall not apply to applications for license renewals.  b.   License   Fee .   The   Applicant   shall   remit   a   $ 100   fee   for   the   initial   two   ( 2 )   year   term   of   its  license.   If   the   license   is   renewed   for   subsequent   three   ( 3 )   year   term(s)   in   accordance  with   Section   5-33.5 ,   the   Applicant   shall   pay   to   the   Township   a   $ 200   non-refundable  license fee.  �-.7   Renewals  All   license   renewals   shall   be   put   to   the   Township   Council   for   consideration.   Failure   to  request   a   license   renewal,   in   writing,   within   thirty   (30)   days   prior   to   expiration   of   a   license   term  shall render the Applicant ’ s license void at the conclusion of the term.  An   Applicant’s   license   may   be   renewed   contingent   upon   payment   of   the   applicable   non-  refundable license fee and the filing of:  1.   A   sworn   statement   filed   with   the   Township   Clerk   that   there   have   been   no   changes  in   the   information   contained   in   the   initial   application,   which   shall   be   filed   with   the  Township Clerk no later than thirty (30) days prior to the expiration of a term; or  2.   In   the   event   of   any   change   in   information   submitted   with   the   Applicant’s   initial  application,   submission   of   a   re new al   application   to   the   Township   Clerk   for  consideration   by   the   Township   Council   no   later   than   thirty   (30)   days   prior   to   the  expiration of a term.  5-33.8   Revocation and Suspension  An   Applicant’s   license   may   be   revoked   or   suspended   pursuant   to   the   terms   of   Section  1.9   of   this   Chapter.   In   addition   to   the   causes   for   revocation   or   suspension   specified   therein,   the  Applicant’s   license   may   be   revoked   or   suspended   by   the   Council   after   notice   and   a   hearing   for  any of the following reasons :

4  a.   Applicant   fails   to   timely   remit   payments   to   the   Township   to   satisfy   obligations   due   to   the  Township from Taxpayer Consumers .  b.   Applicant   does   not   satisfy   its   obligations   to   the   Township,   as   set   forth   in   the   terms   of   this  Ordinance   or   in   the   terms   and   conditions   set   forth   in   the   license   application   as   approved  by the Township Council.  c.   The   Township   learns   of   t he   existence   of   entry   of   a   judgment,   or   an   unsatisfied   judgment  of   record,   entered   against   the   licensee   or   Applicant   in   any   suit   arising   from   or   in   relation  to the operation of any Shop Local Program;  d.   Applicant   fails   to   comply   with   any   and   all   applicable   laws   of   the   State   of   New   Jersey,   the  ordinances   of   the   Township,   or   the   rules   and   regulations   adopted   in   accordance   with   this  Ordinance;  e.   The   Township   learns   of   the   intentional   misrepresentation   of   information   on   an  application for a license or a request for a renewal.  5-33.9   Shop Local   Program   As   Sole Responsibility of Applicant   and Applicant ’ s  Responsibility for Informing Taxpayer Consumers  a.   Sole   Responsibility   of   Applicant .   The   Shop   Local   Program   shall   be   administered   and  managed   solely   by   the   Applicant.   Upon   licensing ,   it   shall   be   the   Licensee’s   independent  responsibility   to   oversee   the   Shop   Local   Program,   register   Taxpayer   Consumers,   and/or  contract   with   interested   Participating   Merchants.   The   Applicant   shall   not   represent   in  any manner that it is an agent or representative of the Township.  b.   Written   descriptions   of   program .   It   shall   be   the   Applicant’s   sole   responsibility   to   ensure  that   Taxpayer   Consumers   have   access   to   a   written   description   of   the   gross   percentage  of   credits   that   will   be   paid   to   the   Township   towards   the   Taxpayer   Consumer’s   real  property   taxes   upon   a   purchase   made   at   all   Participating   Merchants.   Licensee   shall  make   its   best   effort   to   ensure   that   all   Participating   Merchant s   post   a   sign   in   its   front  window   or   in   another   conspicuous   location,   indicating   that   the   Participating   Merchant   is   a  “ Participating   Member   of   Shop   West   Orange   Property   Tax   Reward   Program .”   Every  Participating   Merchant   shall   be   responsible   for   making   available   to   Taxpayer   Consumers  patronizing   its   business   a   written   description   of   the   percentage   of   each   transaction   which  shall be paid to the Township and/or credited to the Taxpayer Consumer.  c.   Review   of   Participating   Merchant’s   Applications   To   Participate   In   Program .   The  Applicant   shall   coordinate,   receive   and   review   all   applications   by   local   businesses   to  become   a   Participating   Merchant.   In   the   event   that   the   Applicant   receives   an   application  to   become   a   Participating   Merchant   from   a   business   located   outside   the   Township,   the  Licensee   shall   review   same,   and   any   approvals   shall   be   subject   to   the   review   and  approval   of   the   Township’s   Chief   Financial   Officer   and/or   Business   Administrator ,   which  approval   shall not be unreasonably withheld .  Insurance and Indemnification  a.   Proof   of   Insurance .   In   conjunction   with   the   submission   of   an   Applicant’s   application   or  renewal   notice   to   operate   a   Shop   Local   Program,   an   Applicant   must   provide   proof   of   a  valid   and   current   general   commercial   liability   insurance   policy   with   a   company   duly  licensed   to   transact   business   under   the   insurance   laws   of   this   State.   The   policy   or

5  policies   shall   provide   coverage   for   a   sum   of   not   less   than   $1,000,000   against   loss   by  reason   of   general   commercial   liability,   not   less   than   $250,000   to   satisfy   any   claim   for  damages   to   property   negligently   caused   by   the   A pplicant,   and   not   less   than   $2,000,000   in  the   aggregate.   Applicant   shall   also   be   required   to   maintain   in   force,   for   the   duration   of  the   licensing   period,   insurance   policies   for   workers   compensation   coverage   for   its  employees,   officers,   agents,   and/or   partners,   as   required   by   applicable   workers  compensation   laws,   and   shall   provide   a   Certificate   of   Insurance   to   the   Township   to  evidence   such   coverage.   The   Applicant   must   provide   a   current   Certificate   of   Insurance  listing   the   Township   as   an   additional   insured.   All   insurance   must   cover   any   acts   of  defalcation of Township funds.  b.   Indemnification .   The   Applicant   shall   indemnify,   defend,   and   hold   harmless   the   Township,  its   officers,   agents   and   employees,   from   any   and   all   claims,   actions,   liability   or   costs,  including   reasonable   attorneys’   fees   and   other   costs   of   defense,   in   connection   with,  arising   out   of,   or   in   any   way   related   to   the   negligence   of   the   Applicant,   or   its   agents   or  employees, in the administration of the   Shop Local Program.  5-33.1 0   Fiduciary Policy   Requirement  a.   Fiduciary   Policy   R equire d .   The   Applicant   shall   provide   to   the   Township,   in   lieu   of   a   cash  or   other   bond,   proof   of   a   valid   fiduciary   policy   naming   the   Township   as   a   loss   payee   in  the   amount   of   $50,000.   The   amount   required   for   the   Fiduciary   Policy   may   be   amended  from time to time by Resolution of the Township Council.  b.   Amendments   to   Fiduciary   Policy .   The   Township   specifically   reserves   the   right   to   require  changes   to   the   fiduciary   policy   amount   during   a   license   term ,   based   upon   the   volume   and  amounts   of   sales   transactions.   The   type   of   guarantee   may   be   substituted   with   another  form of security acceptable to the Township, at the discretion of the Township Council.  5-33.11   Timing and Reporting  a.   Weekly   Deposits   Required .   A   Licensee   under   this   Chapter   must   maintain   a   dedicated  bank   account   for   funds   received   in   connection   with   the   Shop   Local   Program,   unless   the  Taxpayer   Consumer’s   property   tax   credit   funds   will   be   immediately   directed   to   and   held   in  the   Township’s   own   FDIC   insured   dedicated   bank   account,   in   which   case   the  Administrator   need   not   establish   such   a   bank   account.   A   Licensee   shall   hold   in   trust   and  every   week   shall   turn   over   all   funds   collected   from   the   Shop   Local   Program   on   behalf   of  Taxpayer   Consumers,   via   electronic   transfer,   to   the   Township’s   dedicated   bank   account.  The   cost   of   the   wire   transfer,   if   any,   shall   be   the   responsibility   of   the   Township   rather   than  the Applicant.  b.   Quarterly   Reporting   Required .   Along   with   the   weekly   deposits   required   under   this  subsection,   at   the   expiration   of   each   calendar   quarter,   a   Licensee   shall   provide   a   report  to   the   Township’s   Chief   Financial   Officer,   which   must   be   certified   and   provide   the  following   information:   (i)   name   of   Taxpayer   Consumer;   (ii)   amount   of   funds   collected;   and  (iii)   the   address   and/or   Block   and   Lot   number   of   the   Township   property   which   should   be  credited, if any.  c.   Payments   to   tenants   and   non-residents .   It   will   be   the   Licensee’s   sole   responsibility   to  hold   in   trust   and   remit   any   and   all   monies   to   be   paid   as   rebates   to   Taxpayer   Consumers  who   do   not   pay   property   taxes   to   the   Township,   including   but   not   limited   to   tenants,

6  renters,   non-resident   employees,   visitors,   or   other   such   individuals   authorized   to  participate and register in the Shop Local Program.  d.   Reports   to   Taxpayer   Consumers .   The   Licensee   shall   make   available   to   all   Taxpayer  Consumers   a   written   statement   of   credits   or   rebates   earned   by   the   Taxpayer   Consumer.  The   Licensee   may   provide   this   statement   via   e-mail,   or   make   same   accessible   via   its  Web site.  5-33.1 2   Property Tax Credits Provided Only Upon Receipt of Funds by the  Township  The   Township   will   only   provide   credits   to   a   Taxpayer   Consumer’s   property   tax   bill   after  payment   is   received   from   a   Licensee   with   respect   to   the   specific   property   tax   account.   If   the  Township   does   not   receive   payment   relating   to   a   property   tax   credit,   whether   or   not   such  rebates   were   collected   by   the   Licensee,   the   Taxpayer   Consumer   will   be   responsible   for   the   full  amount   of   the   property   tax   liability   as   if   the   credits   were   never   provided   by   the   Participating  Merchant.  5-33.1 3   Violations and Penalties  In   addition   to   suspension   and/or   revocation   of   any   license   issued   hereunder,   and   in  addition   to   any   applicable   State   penalties,   any   violation   of   the   provisions   of   this   Ordinance,   or  any   violation   of   the   terms   and   conditions   of   the   Applicant’s   application,   shall   be   punishable   as  provided   in   Chapter   I,   Section   1-5   of   the   Revised   General   Ordinances   of   the   Township   of   West  Orange.  5-33.1 4   Additional Conditions.  The   Township   shall   establish   the   necessary   reporting   requirement   for   any   payment   of  taxes   made   to   the   Township   by   the   vendor.   The   Township   shall   not   be   responsible   for   any  impact   of   program   participation   on   mortgage   escrow   calculations.   The   Township   shall   not   be  responsible for any changes in property ownership.  The   Applicant   shall   have   the   right   to   surrender   its   license   to   the   Township   at   any   time,   so  long   as   it   provides   ninety   (90)   days’   notice   to   the   Township   Clerk   and   Business   Administrator,   in  writing.   In   such   an   event,   the   Applicant   agrees   to   the   orderly   phasing   out   of   its   involvement   in  the   Shop   Local   Program,   including   but   not   limited   to   remitting   the   balance   of   any   and   all   funds  due   to   the   Township   in   connection   with   property   tax   credits   which   may   have   been   retained   by  the Applicant on behalf of Taxpayer Consumers.  AND   BE   IT   FURTHER   ORDAINED ,   that   the   remainder   of   Chapter   5   remains  unchanged.  BE   IT   FURTHER   ORDAINED ,   that   if   any   section,   paragraph,   subsection,   clause   or  provision   of   this   Ordinance   shall   be   adjudged   by   the   courts   to   be   invalid,   such   adjudication   shall  apply   only   to   the   section,   paragraph,   subsection,   clause   or   provision   so   adjudicated,   and   the  remainder of the Ordinance shall be deemed valid and effective.

7  BE   IT   FURTHER   ORDAINED ,   that   any   ordinances   or   parts   thereof   in   conflict   with   the  provisions of this Ordinance are repealed to the extent of such conflict.  BE   IT   FURTHER   ORDAINED ,   that   this   Ordinance   shall   take   effect   upon   passage   and  publication in accordance with law.  __________________________________  Honorable Susan McCartney  Council President  ___________________________________  Honorable Robert D. Parisi  Mayor  Adopted: August _________, 2013  Approved as to form on the basis of the facts provided:  _________________________________________  Richard D. Trenk  Township Attorney  740139 _ 1

8  LEGISLATIVE HISTORY  This   Ordinance   is   intended   to   provide   a   legislative   scheme   which   requires   that   any   entity  seeking   to   operate   any   type   of   “Shop   Local”   incentive   program   must   first   obtain   a   license   to   do  so.  On   May   7,   2013,   the   Township   Council   adopted   Resolution   No.   87-13,   formally  expressing   its   support   for   a   “Shop   Local”   property   tax   credit   program   (the   “Shop   Local  Program”).   The   Shop   Local   Program   proposes   to   authorize   licensed   third   party   administrators  (“TPA”)   to   organize   and   administer   a   local   economic   stimulation   plan   whereby   a   Township  resident   is   rewarded   with   property   tax   credits   when   it   makes   purchases   at   P articipating  M erchants.  As   currently   contemplated,   the   Shop   Local   Program   will   require   TPAs   to   register   and  contract   with   certain   interested   merchants,   which   would   offer   tax   credits,   processed   through   the  TPA,   to   residents   who   make   purchases   at   their   businesses.   The   TPA   will   receive   a   fee   from   the  Participating   M erchant   for   a   designated   portion   of   the   transaction   (in   an   exact   percentage  selected   by   the   Participating   M erchant),   and   a   portion   of   that   fee   would   then   be   provided   to   the  Township to satisfy the resident ’ s financial or tax obligations to the Township.  Other   municipalities   have   adopted   or   are   in   the   process   of   adopting   and   authorizing   such  programs.   These   municipalities   include   Marlboro,   Pequannock,   Voorhees,   Bayonne,   Caldwell  and   Manalapan.   The   New   Jersey   Department   of   Consumer   Affairs,   Division   of   Local  Government   Services,   has   advised   the   Township   that   there   is   no   prohibition   on   this   manner   of  satisfying a resident ’ s tax obligations.  This   proposed   Ordinance   serves   several   important   functions.   First,   it   creates   a   clear  procedure   to   govern   the   application   process   for   those   entities   seeking   to   operate   any   Shop Local  Program.   Once   a   conforming   application   has   been   submitted   to   the   Township   Clerk   and  reviewed   for   completeness,   the   Township   Council   has   the   authority   to   grant   or   deny   the   license  by resolution based on its review of several enumerated factors .

9  This   legislation   also   promotes   the   public   safety   and   welfare,   requiring   that   all   applicants  for   a   license   to   operate   a   Shop   Local   Program   meet   a   review   by   the   Township   Council   about   the  applicant’s   business   aptitude   and   criminal   history.   This   Ordinance   also   protects   the   public   by  requiring   the   licensee   to   post   a   bond,   essentially   guaranteeing   that   monies   credited   to   a  Taxpayer   C onsumer   for   making   purchases   at   local   businesses   actually   be   made   available   to   the  Township   as   a   credit   to   the   Taxpayer   C onsumer.   The   Ordinance   also   requires   the   applicant   to  file   proof   of   liability   insurance   which   names   the   Township   as   an   Additional   Insured,   and   further  requires   an   indemnification   in   favor   of   the   Township   to   ensure   that   there   is   limited   liability   in  connection   with   the   Shop   Local   Program.   Also,   given   the   innovative   nature   of   the   Shop   Local  Program,   this   legislation   authorizes   the   Township   Council   to   establish,   by   resolution,   a   form   of  application   to   be   submitted   by   the   prospective   licensee;   this   application   form   may   also   set   forth  additional   terms   and   conditions   which   the   Council   deems   necessary   and   proper   to   the   efficient  and effective administration of the Shop Local Program.  The   Authority   for   this   proposed   Ordinance   is   derived   from   N.J.S.A.   40:48-1,   which  authorizes   the   governing   body   of   every   municipality   in   the   State   of   New   Jersey   to   “make,  amend,   repeal   and   enforce   .   .   .   ordinances,   regulations,   rules   and   by-laws   not   contrary   to   the  laws   of   this   state   or   of   the   United   States,   as   it   may   deem   necessary   and   proper   for   the   good  government,   order   and   protection   of   persons   and   property,   and   for   the   preservation   of   the   public  health, safety and welfare of the municipality and its inhabitants. ”  747869 _ 2

documents.title: ORDINANCE AMENDING CHP. 2, SECTION 66.1A FEES FOR SERVICES
APPOINTMENTS TO OPEN SPACE+ RECREATION COMM.

pages.text: 146-13  July   16,   2013  RESOLUTION  WHEREAS,   the Township of West Orange is desirous of  awarding a contract for the providing of   recreational  services and social services to the residents of the  Township  of   West   Orange,   and for the   p eriod commencing January 1,  2013   and en ding December 31,   2013 ;  WHEREAS,   the Township Mountain Top   League,   is willing  to   provide such   services;  WHEREAS,   the aggregate of public funds to be expended  for the   provision of such   services is Nine Thousand Five  Hundred   (9,500.00)   Dollars and therefore, no public  advertisements for   the   bids is required.  NOW, THEREFORE, BE IT RESOLVED   by the   Township Council  of   the   Township   of   West Orange that the Mayor and Township  Clerk be and hereby are respectively authorized   to execute  and attest to a   contract,   in a form approved by   the Township  Attorney with the Mountain Top League, for the provision of  recreational services and   social   services to the residents  of   the   Township   of   West Orange.  Karen J. Carnevale   Susan   McCartney  Municipal Clerk   Council President  Adopted:   July 16,   2013

146-13  THIS A GRE EMENT, made this   day July   2013   by and  between:  TOWNSHIP OF WEST ORANGE, a municipal corporation of the  State of New Jersey, having its principal offices at 66   Main  Street,   West Orange, New Jersey,   hereinafter referred to as  "Township";   and  MOUNTAIN TOP LEAGUE,   a nonprofit corporation of the  State of New Jersey having its principal   offices at 12 Old  Indian   Road,   West   Orange, New Jersey, hereinafter referred  to   as "MTL".  W I T N   E S S   E T H:  WHEREAS,   the Township desires to provide certain  recreational services,   social services, activities, and  facilities to the residents of the Township of West Orange;  and  WHEREAS,   it is the purpose   of this   agreement to recite  the terms and conditions under which the   foregoing shall be  provided; and  NOW THEREFORE , in consideration of the   foregoing and of  the mutual covenants hereinafter   stated,   the parties do  hereby   agree as   follows:  1.   F or the period   of January 1, 2013   to

December 31, 2013   Contractor shall provide and make  available to West Orange residents, services and facilities  for participation by such   residents of West Orange.  2.   The   foregoing facilities   and services shall be  supervised and operated by personnel provided by MTL,  whose personnel   shall be solely the employees and volunteers  of   MTL   who is responsible   to pay   them, where appropriate, at  the sole discretion of   MTL.  3. MTL   shall provide, at MTL expense,  Workers   Compensation Insurance, covering   its employees  performing hereunder, and   public   liability insurance with  limits   of Five Hundred   Thousand ($500,000.00) Dollars to One  Million   ($1,000,000.00) Dollars for the areas and locations  where the recreational   facilities are conducted. Said  insurance shall   cover the   interest of the Township and shall  be   with   carriers satisfactory to   the Township and authorized  to   do business in New Jersey. Certificates evidencing the  foregoing shall   be   furnished to the Township upon execution  of   this   Agreement.  4.   MTL   agrees   to indemnify the Township and save  it   harmless, including   Township's employees, agents and  servants, from and   against any and all losses, claims,  liabilities   and   expenses which may arise   or be claimed  against   the   Township, its   agents, servants or employees   in  connection with   any phase   of this Agreement.  5.   Neither   this Agreement nor the monies granted

hereunder may be assigned.  6.   MTL   acknowledges that it   has read N.J.S.A. 10:2-1,  et   seq., of   the   Revised Statutes   of the State of New Jersey  (Discrimination   in   Employment on   Public Works, Contract  Provisions), the terms   of   which are incorporated herein   as  though   expressly set forth at length. MTL agrees to comply  with   and be   bound by the provisions thereof to the extent  held   applicable   to   volunteer organizations.  7.   It is understood that this Agreement shall be deemed  effective as of   January 1, 2013 .  8.   Township shall   pay to MTL for the services to be  provided hereunder   for   the full one-year   term, the sum of  Nine   Thousand Five   Hundred ($9,500.00) Dollars.  9.   The   manner   of payment shall be as determined jointly  by   the   President of MTL and Director of the Department of  Recreation of Township.  10. The parties hereto agree that the provisions of the  "New   Jersey   Prevailing   Wage Act"   shall apply hereto the  extent   held   applicable   to   a volunteer organization.  Accordingly, MTL agrees to comply with the provisions of  N.J.S.A. 34:11-56.25 through 34:11-56.33, the provisions of  which are hereby incorporated by   reference as though stated  at   length herein.   MTL   hereby declares that it is aware   of  the prevailing wage rates   on file in the   Township Hall of  Township pursuant to said   Act and agrees   to comply therewith  to   the   extent held   applicable to   volunteer organizations.

MTL further   agrees   as follows:  A.   All covered employees of MTL performing services  hereunder shall be paid not   less than the prevailing  wage rate as specified.  B.   MTL shall maintain accurate   records, showing name,   kind  of work performed   and actual hourly   rate of wage paid  to each covered employee employed hereunder.   Such  records shall   be maintained   for three(3) years from  date of payment and shall be available for   inspection  by the  Township's   Business Administrator or his designee on  request.  C.   MTL shall post the prevailing wage rate for each  covered employee performing   worker hereunder, as  determined   by   the   Commissioner of Labor and Industry of  the State of New Jersey, including the effective date  of any   changes thereof, in a prominent and   easily  accessible   place and   the principal Offices   of MTL.  D.   If it is found that any covered employee of MTL is   paid  less than the   required wage   rates Township   may, on  written notice to   MTL terminate MTL's rights to proceed  hereunder,   or   as to such portion of   services rendered  hereunder as to which there   has been a failure to pay  the required wages.   Township may then arrange to  prosecute the   rendering of the services hereunder by  other means, in which MTL shall be liable to Township

for costs and   damages incurred by Township.  E.   It is specifically understood that the provisions of  this paragraph shall   not apply to such persons engaged  by Contract render services   hereunder who are  performing   such services as   volunteers with out  remuneration.  F.   MTL agrees   to   be bound by the provisions of Schedule A  attached hereto and made of   part hereof.  IN   WITNESS WHEREOF, the parties have caused these  presents to   be signed by their proper corporate   officers and  their property corporate seals to be hereto affixed the   day  and year first above written.  ATTEST:   TOWNSHIP OF WEST ORANGE  ________________________   __________________________  Karen J. Carnevale, R.M.C.   BY: ROBERT   D.   PARISI ,MAYOR  ATTEST:   MOUNTAIN TOP LEAGUE  _________________________  SECRETARY   __________________________  PRESIDENT

146-13  " SCHEDULE   A "  (I)   During the   performance of this agreement MTL agrees  as   follows:  a.   The   MTL   will not discriminate against any employee  of   applicant for employment because of age, race, creed,  color,   national,origin, ancestry, marital status or sex. MTL  will  take   affirmative action to ensure that such applicants are  recruited and employed, and that   employees are treated  during

employment,   without regard to their age,   race, creed, color,  national origin, ancestry, marital status or sex. Such  action  shall include, but   not   be   limited to the   following:  employment,   upgrading,   demotion,   or transfer; recruitment or  recruitment   advertising; layoff or termination;   rates of pay  or   other forms of compensation; and selection for training,  including apprenticeship.   The contractor   agrees   to post   in  conspicuous   places, available to   employees and applicants  for  employment,   notices to   be   provided by the Public Agency  Compliance Officer   setting forth   provisions of this non-  discrimination clause;  b.   MTL   will, in all solicitations or advertisements for  employees placed by or   on   behalf   of the MTL, state that   all  qualified applicants will   receive consideration   for  employment without   regard   to age, creed,   color,   national  origin,   ancestry, martial   status   or sex;  c.   MTL   will send to each labor union or representative  or   workers with   which it has a collective bargaining  agreement or other   contract or understanding, a   notice,   to  be   provided   by the   agency   contracting officer advising the  labor union   or worker's representative of the contractor's  commitments   under this   act and shall post copies of the  notice   in conspicuous places available to employees and  applicants for employment.

d.   MTL agrees to comply with   any regulations promulgated  by   the   Treasure   pursuant to P.L.   1975,c.127, as   amended   and  supplemented from time   to   time.  (II)   MTL agrees to   attempt in good faith to   employ  minority and female workers consistent with the   applicable  county   employment goals prescribed by section 5.2 of the  Regulations   promulgated by the Treasurer   pursuant to P.L.  1975, c.127, as   amended and supplemented   from time to time  or   in accordance with a binding determination of the  applicable county employment goals determined by the  Affirmative   Action   Office   pursuant to section 5.2 of the  Regulations   promulgated by the Treasurer   pursuant to P.L.  1975, c. 127, as amended and supplemented from time to time.  (III)   MTL agrees to inform in writing appropriate  recruitment   agencies in the area, including employment  agencies, placements bureaus, colleges, universities, labor  unions,   that it   does not discriminate on   the basis of age,  creed,   color, national   origin, ancestry,   marital status   or  sex,   and that it will discontinue the use of any recruitment  agency   which engages in direct or indirect  or   indirect   discriminatory practices.  (IV)   MTL agrees to   revise any of its   testing procedure,  if   necessary, to assure that all   personnel testing conforms  with   the principles of   job-related testing, as established  by   the   statutes   and court   decision of the State   of New  Jersey   and as established   by applicable federal   law and

applicable federal   court decisions.  (V)   MTL   agrees   to review all   procedures relating to  transfer, upgrading, downgrading   and layoff to ensure that  all such actions are taken without regard to age, creed,  color,   national   origin, ancestry, marital status or sex, and  conform   with the applicable employment goals, consistent  with   the statutes and court decisions of   the State of New  Jersey,   and   applicable   federal Law and applicable federal  court decisions.

14 7 -13  AGREEMENT  THIS   AGREEMENT ,   made   this   day   of   July   201 3 ,   by   and  between:  TOWNSHIP   OF   WEST   ORANGE,   a   municipal   corporation   of the  State   of   New   Jersey,   having   its   principal   offices   at   66   Main  Street,   West   Orange,   New   Jersey,   hereinafter   referred   to   as  "Township";   and  WEST   ORANGE   COMMUNITY   HOUSE,   a   nonprofit   corporation of   the  State   of   New   Jersey   having   its   principal   offices   at   242   Main  Street,   West   Orange,   New   Jersey,   hereinafter   referred   to   as  "Contractor ."  W I T N   E S S   E T H:  WHEREAS ,   the   Township   desires   to   provide   certain  recreational   services,   social   services,   activities,   and  facilities to the residents of the Township of West Orange;   and  WHEREAS ,   it   is   the   purpose   of   this   agreement   to   recite   the  terms   and   conditions   under   which   the   foregoing   shall   be  provided; and  NOW   THEREFORE ,   in   consideration   of   the   foregoing   and   of   the  mutual   covenants   hereinafter   stated,   the   parties   do   hereby   agree  as   follows:  1.   F or   the   period   of   January   1,   201 3   to   December   31,  201 3   Contractor   shall   provide   and   make   available   to   West   Orange  residents,   services   and   facilities   for   participation   by   such  residents of West Orange.  2.   The   referenced facilities   and   services   shall   be  supervised   and   operated   by   personnel   provided   by   Contractor,

which   personnel   shall   be   solely   the   employees   of   Contractor,   who  is   responsible to pay them.  3.   Contractor   shall   provide,   at   Contractor's expense,  Workers   Compensation   Insurance,   covering   its   employees  performing   hereunder,   and   public   liability   insurance   with   limits  of   Five   Hundred   Thousand   ($500,000.00)   Dollars   to   One   Million  ($1,000,000.00)   Dollars   for   the   areas   and   locations   where   the  recreational   facilities   are   conducted.   Said   insura nce   shall   name  the   Township   and   shall   be   with   carriers   satisfactory   to   the  Township   and   authorized   to   do   business   in   New   Jersey.  Certificates   evidencing   the   foregoing   shall   be   furnished   to   the  Township upon execution   of this Agreement.  4. Contractor agrees to   indemnify the   Township and hold   it  harmless,   including   Township's   employees,   agents   and   servants,  from   and   against   any   and   all   losses,   claims,   liabilities   and  expenses   which   may   arise   or   be   claimed   against   the   Township,   its  agents,   servants   or   employees   in   connection   with   any   phase   of  this   Agreement.  5.   Neither   this   Agreement   nor   the   monies   to grow   hereunder  may be   assigned.  6.   Contractor   acknowledges   that   it   has   read N.J.S.A.   10:2-  1,   et   seq. ,   of   the   Revised   Statutes   of   the   State   of   New  Jersey(Discrimination   in   Employment   on   Public   Works,   Contract  Provisions),   the   terms   of   which   are   incorporated   herein   as  though   expressly   set   forth   at   length.   Contractor   agrees   to  comply   with   and   be   bound by the provisions thereof.

7.   It   is   understood   that   this   Agreement   shall   be deemed  effective   as of January 1, 2013 .  8.   Details   of   the   facilities   and   programs involved   herein  shall   be   furnished   to   the   Director   of   the   Department   of  Recreation   of   the   Township;   and,   a   year-end   report,   as   to   all   of  same,   shall   likewise   be   furnished   to   said   Director   prior   to  December 31, 2013 .  9.   All   programs   provided   hereunder   shall   be   under the   joint  overall   supervision   of   the   Director   of   the   Department   of  Recreation   of   the   Township   and   the   Director   of   Contractor.   They  shall   jointly   supervise   all   of   said   programs,   regarding   which  there   shall   be   the   right   of   modification   and   substitution   with  other   programs   subject   to   joint   approval   of   Director   of  Contractor   and   Director   of   the   Department   of   Recreation   of  Township.   The   location,   dates   and   scheduling   involved   shall   be  subject   to   the   joint   approval   of   Director   of   Contractor   and  Director of   Department   of   Recreation of Township.  10.   Township   shall   pay   to   Contractor   for   the   services to   be  provided   hereunder   for   the   full   one-year   term,   the   sum   of   Forty-  Five   Thousand ($45,000.00) Dollars.  11.   The   manner   of   payment   shall   be   as   determined jointly   by  the   Director   of   Contractor   and   Director   of   the   Department   of  Recreation   of   Township.   No   payment   hereunder   shall   issue   unless  the   Director   of   the   Department   of   Recreation   of   the   Township   has  filed   with   the   Business   Administrator   of   the   Township,   a   written  statement   certifying   that   the   payment   should   issue   based   upon  his   knowledge   of   performance   hereunder   or   performance   in

progress hereunder.  12.   The   parties   hereto   agree   that   the   provisions   of the   "New  Jersey   Prevailing   Wage   Act"   shall   apply   hereto.   Accordingly,  contractor   agrees   to   comply   with   the   provisions   of   N.J.S.A.  34:11-56.25   through   34:11-56.33,   the   provisions   of   which   are  hereby   incorporated   by   reference   as   though   stated   at   length  herein.   Contractor   hereby   declares   that   it   is   aware   of   the  prevailing   wage   rates   on   file   in   the   Township   Hall   of   the  Township   pursuant   to   said   Act   and   agrees   to   comply   therewith.  Contractor further   agrees   as follows:  A.   All   employees   of   contractor   performing services  hereunder   shall   be   paid   not   less   than   the   prevailing   wage   rate  as   specified.  B.   Contractor   shall   maintain   accurate   records,   showing  name,   kind   of   work   performed   and   actual   hourly   rate   of   wages  paid   to   each   employee   employed   hereunder.   Such   records   shall   be  maintained   for   three   (3)   years   from   date   of   payment   and   shall   be  available   for   inspection   by   the   Township's   Business  Administrator or his designee on   request.  C.   Contractor   shall   post   the   prevailing   wage   rate for   each  employee   performing   work   hereunder,   as   determined   by   the  Commissioner   of   Labor   and   Industry   of   the   State   of   New   Jersey,  including   the   effective   date   of   any   changes   thereof,   in   a  prominent   and   easily   accessible   place   and   the   principal   Office  of   Contractor.  D.   If   it   is   found   that   any   employee   of   Contractor is   paid  less   than   the   required   wage   rates   Township   may,   on   written

notice   to   Contractor,   terminate   Contractor's   rights   to   proceed  hereunder,   or   as   to   such   portion   of   services   rendered   hereunder  as   to   which   there   has   been   a   failure   to   pay   the   required   wages.  Township   may   then   arrange   to   prosecute   the   rendering   of   the  services   hereunder   by   other   means,   in   which   even t   Contractor  shall   be   liable   to   Township   for   all   costs   and   damages   incurred  by   Township.  E.   It   is   specifically   understood   that   the   provisions of  this   paragraph   shall   not   apply   to   such   persons   engaged   by  Contract or   to   render   services   hereunder   who   are   performing   such  services as   volunteers   without remuneration.  F.   Contractor   agrees   to   be   bound   by   the   provisions of  Schedule   “ A ”   attached hereto   and made a part hereof. 148-13  July 16, 2013  RESOLUTION  WHEREAS,   the Township of West Orange is desirous of  awarding a contract for the providing of   recreational  services and social services to the residents of the  Township  of   West   Orange,   and for the   p eriod commencing January 1,  2013  and e nding   December 31, 201 3 ;  WHEREAS,   the Townships Police Athletic League, is  willing   to provide   such services;  WHEREAS,   the aggregate of public funds to be expended  for the   provision of such   services is   Twelve   Thousand  ( 12,0 00.00)   Dollars and therefore, no public advertisements  for the   bids is   required.  NOW, THEREFORE, BE IT RESOLVED   by the   Township Council  of   the   Township   of   West Orange that the Mayor and Township  Clerk be and hereby are respectively authorized   to execute  and attest to a   contract,   in a form approved by   the Township  Attorney with the Police Athletic League, for the provision  of   recreational   services and social services to   the  residents of the Township   of West Orange.
