B. Additional Parking Requirements   and Standards :  1- In addition to the provision of one parking space per residential unit, to be  constructed on the project site, any redeveloper shall be required to provide a minimum  of   thirty   off site parking spaces   to service the residential units , not to be located in any  public street or roadway (right of way). These additional parking spaces shall be located  within close proximity   to the Redevelopment Plan Area.  2- In addition, any redeveloper must work   in cooperation   with the Township to  provide   additional parking opportunities to serve the Valley Road area, whether this be  through the identification and development of additional off-street parking or through a  financial contribution by a redeveloper to the Township to further the Township's parking  objectives in the vicinity of any redevelopment project constructed hereunder .  3-All parking areas shall be built in strict accordance with an approved parking  circulation plan, as approved by the West Orange Planning Board. The size, length and  width, of parking spaces shall be approved by the Township Engineer for compliance  with best practices.  4- Any proposed residential parking structure shall be built into the residential  building, having the residences located above. Adequate lighting, ventilation, and  security shall be provided.

19  5- The parking structure shall have the same facade as the building facade. This is  intended to promote an attractive uniform look to the building.  6- All parking shall be accessed by means of common driveways, from side  streets. Parking on the streets of West Orange, or side of buildings, shall be discouraged  and may be prohibited by an ordinance adopted by the Township Council.  7-Parking lot design shall consider pedestrian circulation. Pedestrian crosswalks  shall be provided and linked to the wider pedestrian network. Pavement textures shall be  required on pedestrian access ways, and strongly encouraged elsewhere in the parking lot,  as surfacing materials, or when used as accents.  8-Parking lots shall meet New Jersey ADA requirements for handicapped  parking. Parking lot lines shall be painted white; fire lanes and handicap stalls shall be  painted yellow. Parking lots shall be suitably built to provide shade and visual relief.  9-For parking lot(s) having two or more rows sufficient aisle space shall be  constructed to allow the safe passage of vehicles.  C .   Additional   Residential   Building and Site Design Standards: Buildings shall be  designed to be attractive   and   aesthetically pleasing, as the West Orange Planning Board  may deem appropriate ,   and using the following standards   applicable to the MUBR zone .  1- Buildings shall have a defined base and cap. The base may align with the sill  level of the first floor, the foundation edge, or the ground with foundation plantings.  The cap of the building includes the building cornice, parapet or eaves at the top of the  building wall.   The roof may be of a   “ flat design ” ,   but shall have decorative treatments  such as   “ dormers ”   or false windows to add dimension and elegance to the building. A  total   “ flat surface ”   type structure shall be avoided.   Long, blank wall expanses are  discouraged.  2 -Fire escapes are not permitted on the front   facade ( wall )   of a building.  3- All air conditioning units, HVAC systems, exhaust pipes or stacks, elevator  housing and satellite dishes and other telecommunications devices shall be screened from  public view, by using walls, roof elements, penthouse   type screening devices or  landscaping, designed to be architecturally compatible with the building ’ s style,  materials, colors and   details.

20  4 -Buildings   where a portion of the structure is built within four feet of the  adjoining property boundary line shall have the stories above   the first story   recessed, or  terraced, to provide an attractive side yard.   The use of terraces and faux window  treatments is encouraged to provide a unique architectural feel.  5-Building(s) shall have an attractive and easily identifiable entry with a canopy  or awning of an appropriate dimension   as approved by the West Orange Planning Board.  6 -Buildings sh all   have quality brick, stone, or other materials so as to appear  unique; promoting a   “ elegant,   charming, quaint, or   village-like ”   sense of place.  7 -Internally illuminated signs, particularly   “ lightbox ”   signs, are prohibited in the  entire   MUBR zone .  8 - Signs shall not obscure architectural elements such as clerestory windows nor  span across structural bays or columns. Signs shall be subordinate to the building ’ s  facade, not overwhelming ,   but rather in keeping with the building design.  9 - Buildings, including parking areas ,   shall be well illuminated, with the windows  of residences screened from direct or harsh lighting. Building and s ign illumination by  bare floodlight, blinking or flashing bulbs is   prohibited.  10 -Awnings are permitted, provided they add to the aesthetics of the building , as  solely determined by the West Orange Planning Board .  1 1 -No advertising or marketing s igns   or phrases shall be permitted anywhere on  any sign   or on   any residential   building   whatsoever.  1 2 -Way finding signage   is permitted and   shall be consistent with other streetscape  elements.  1 3 -Decorative lighting fixtures shall be used   on the building and   along the street  edge and within the private parking areas and walkways to establish a consistent lighting  design theme.   (This shall include building accent lighting.)  1 4 - “ Washing ”   the entire building facade in light is   prohibited .  D .   Landscaping Design Standards.  1-Extensive landscaping shall be provided for all   land   improvements .   All areas of  the   area   not be occupied by building, parking and other improvements shall be  intensively planted with trees, shrubs, grass and perennials.   Th e landscape plan and all  planting shall be approved by the West Orange Planning Board.

21  2-The landscape   plan and all   plantings shall be complement the building (s)   and  accentuate important features. Plants shall be chosen for year round interest including  color, flowers, bark interest or branching patterns. Hardy native plants shall be used  where possible.  3 -Existing healthy and mature trees shall be retained and incorporated into the  landscape plan where possible.  4 -The use of planters, window flower boxes and hanging baskets is encouraged to  provide seasonal color  5 -The landscaping shall be integrated with other site design features such as   the  waterway ,   pedestrian   paths, gazebos, street furniture and public art -all of which is  encouraged .  6 - T rees, a minimum of 3 ½-4 inch caliper   at the time of planting, shall   be planted  in designated landscape area s, as shown on the landscape plan to be approved by the  West Orange Planning Board. These landscape areas shall be located   in the front and rear  yards ,   and the side yard where practical.   The bottom branches shall be trimmed to a  minimum of seven (7 ’ ) feet from the ground to allow pedestrian passage.  7 -The following small flowering trees, or equivalent, are recommended for the  MUBR zone :  Carpinus betulus   “ Fastigata ”   (Pyramidal European Hornbeam)  Prunus Serrulata Kwanzan (Kwanzan Cherry)  Celtis Occidentalis (Hackberry)  Pyrus Calleryana   “ Redspire ” (Redspire Pear)  Crataegus Crusgalli var. inermis (Thornless Cockspur Hawthorne).  8 -Planting beds shall be chosen for year round interest including color, flowers,  fruit, bark interest or branching patterns.  E .   Sidewalk, Crosswalk, and Public Space Design Standards :  1 -   Sidewalks shall be wide enough to handle pedestrians and , together with the  area within the front or rear yard setback, be sufficient to   accommodate benches, planters  and street trees lights   where appropriate . Sidewalks along the   perimeter of a residential  building shall be a minimum of four   feet ( 4 ’ ) wide. Sidewalks shall have a similar design  and material content as walkways, as specified in   Paragraph 2 , which follows.

22  2 -   Pedestrian w alkways , located within a residential project   shall be raised and  curbed along buildings and within parking   areas   where suitable. Scored or decorative  concrete, having a limited number of raised areas, shall be the preferred material. All  walkways and paved areas not within the public right of way shall use appropriately  colored pavers, at the option of the   redeveloper . All specific designs shall be subject to  West Orange   Planning Board approval.  3 -   Barrier-free walkway systems   and entrances to the residential building   shall be  provided to allow pedestrian access to buildings ,   or uses ,   from parking lots and public  sidewalks.  F . Traffic Plan: A traffic circulation plan shall be   submitted   by the applicant to the West  Orange Planning Board for their review and approval.  G .   Loading and Service Areas : i ncluding   those areas used for   trash receptacles,  dumpsters and utility boxes shall be placed in visually unobtrusive locations in the side or  rear of the building(s). The service areas shall be in   or next to   ground level parking areas  and shall be located behind buildings or designed to fit into the streetscape. Chain link  fencing including the use of wood or plastic slats and wood stockade fencing is  prohibited for screening of trash and service areas.  H .   Walls and fences in all public areas shall be constructed to match the architectural  detail of the principal structure .   Fences and screen walls shall be limited to a maximum  height of four (4 ’ ) feet except they must be six (6 ’ ) feet in height when used as a buffer  for service area enclosures. Walls can be used as seating walls and for privacy   in open  areas .  I . The storage of any materials, products, debris, signs, or any bulk item on any   property  or   premises is prohibited .  1 1 . The East Branch of the Rahway River.  This waterway is currently stagnant and an attractive nuisance. Pedestrians may  wander onto the poorly lighted property and fall into the unprotected river. The river bed  is a dilapidated concrete structure and ha s   debris and standing water in it. Part of the  Harvard Press building   is constructed over the waterway. Harvard Development  Associates, LLC , as stated   in their DEP permit application intends to open up and clean  up the waterway. This is positive environmental measure, consistent with the objectives

23  of this Redevelopment Plan, specifically   O bjective s A and B, as well as   G oal B   of this  Redevelopment Plan   relating to cleaning up environmental contamination.  The re-design of th is   waterway should be sufficient to handle any potential  flooding concerns. Moreover, a maintenance plan should be developed so as to avoid this  portion of the river being filled in by silt and debris. The property owner or homeowners  association must be parties to such an agreement.  1 2 . The Transit Village   Concept .  When considered together with the previously mentioned City of Orange , Central  Valley   Redevelopment Plan a total of 310 residences may potentially be built on the  former Harvard Press site. More than 400 people will be living   t here, together with the  people already residing in the area, both in West Orange and Orange. These residents will  be conveniently situated near the Highland Rail Station.   A plan should be prepared by the  redeveloper to encourage use of this Rail Station.  Both municipalities should   work together to   explore   State of New Jersey   Transit  Village designation for this area and review the potential for Federal and State grants to  spur further smart growth oriented redevelopment.   This is consistent with   O bjective C  and   G oal B of this Plan.  1 3 . Other Statutory Considerations.  A.   N.J.S.A.40A:12A-7.a (3) requires the identification of those persons required to be  relocated.   Because the properties are generally vacant and underutilized ,   and   are  scheduled to be   acquired through a   pending   private transaction ,   n o such relocation is  being anticipated at this time.  B.   N.J.S.A.40A:12A-7. a (4) requires the identification of the properties to be  acquired under the Redevelopment Plan. No property is envisioned to be acquired  through action prompted by this Plan, including through the exercise of eminent domain  condemnation.   The property   is scheduled to be   acquired through a   pending   private  transaction.  C.   N.J.S.A.40A:12A-7. c and d requires this Redevelopment Plan describe its  relationship to pertinent municipal development regulations as included in the MLUL   and  be either substantially consistent with the municipal master plan or designed to effectuate  the master plan.   We have   shown   that this   R edevelopment Plan is consistent with the   West

24  Orange   Master Plan and the Redevelopment Plan of the City of Orange .  Although   the   proposed   project   is   inconsistent   with   the   current   Industrial   zoning  designation,   establishment   of   the   Mixed   Use   Business   Residential   zone   (MUBR)   will  further   protect,   stabilize,   and   facilitate   of   the   general   welfare   of   the   community.   It   should  also   be   noted   that   the   southern   boundary   of   the   Industrial   zone   and   the   proposed   MUBR  zone   is   Mitchell   Street   which   is   a   residential   neighborhood,   and   zoned   R-M,   Residential  Apartment.  The   purpose   of   zoning,   as   described   in   the   Municipal   Land   Use   Law   (MLUL )  N.J.S.A.   40:55D-1   et   seq.   is   a   multi-faceted   definition.   This   Redevelopment   Plan   meets  many of the purposes of land use planning including:  To   encourage   municipal   action   to   guide   the   appropriate   use   or   development   of  land, in a manner this will promote the public health, safety, and general welfare;  To   provide   sufficient   space   in   appropriate   locations   for   a   variety   of   agricultural,  residential,   recreational,   commercial   and   industrial   uses   and   open   space,   both  public   and   private,   according   to   their   respective   environmental   requirements   in  order to meet the needs of all New Jersey residents;  To   promote   the   conservation   of   historic   sites   and   districts,   open   space,   energy  resources   and   valuable   natural   resources   in   the   State   and   to   prevent   urban   sprawl  and degradation of the environment   through improper use of land; and,  To   encourage   coordination   of   the   various   public   and   private   procedures   and  activities   shaping   land   development   with   a   view   of   lessening   the   cost   of   such  development and to the more efficient use of land.  As   previously   stated ,   the   objectives   and   stated   purpose   of   this   Redevelopment  Plan   is   to   provide   for   the   environmental   clean   up   and   general   economic   development   of  the   area .   By   s pecifying   the   location   of   significant   new   residential   development   in   this  R edevelopment   Plan   Area   the   Township   will   prevent   the   improper   use   of   land   in   the  future   and protect significant natural resources in   the   Township   of West Orange .  Indeed   the   goals   and   objectives   of   this   Redevelopment   Plan   are   taken,   in   part,  from   the   West   Orange   Master   Plan   Reexamination   of   2004.   The   proposed   MUBR   zoning  district   is   consistent   with   the   MLUL   by   presenting   a   consistent   land   use   pattern   while   at  the   same   time   promoting   the   use   of   public   transportation   and   cleaning   up   an

25  environmentally contaminated site.  1 4 . Council On Affordable Housing (COAH) Requirements:  The State of New Jersey is in the process of revising its COAH requirements to  provide additional affordable housing throughout the State of New Jersey. Currently  COAH requires one affordable unit be built for every eight market units. However, the  ratio of the number of affordable units may increase to one affordable unit for every four  market units. This means that a minimum of eleven COAH units will have to be built,  within either a Redevelopment Project or elsewhere within the Township of West  Orange.   Perhaps more COAH units will be required under new Regulations being  considered   by the State of New Jersey .  To the extent that a Redevelopment Project creates a requirement for COAH  housing ,   the Redeveloper shall be responsible for satisfying any such COAH  requirement.  1 5 . Economic Impact:  This Redevelopment Plan provides a workable solution to turn around a blighted  and unproductive area of the Township of West Orange.   By having the residences  integrated with the retail businesses along Valley Road an economic engine could occur  and the rehabilitation   of adjacent areas could be encouraged.   This will improve property  values and increase the tax base of the   T ownship.  The properties in the proposed redevelopment area pa id   only   $21,539 in property  taxes in 2007. Assuming   89   new   market type   residences are built in the   West Orange  Valley Road R edevelopment   A rea   at a sales price of $250,000, a total value of  $22,250,000 results. At the 2007 assessed to market ( “ true ” ) value ratio of 24% this  results in a new total tax assessment of approximately $5,340,000. This generates  approximately $565,500   annually   i n   pr operty tax revenue.   This is an increase of over  $540,000. This represents significant revenue to the Township.  We must caution, however, that we are aware that a residential project may have  public school children living there, placing demands on the public school district.

26  Harvard Development   A ssociates, LLC had a study of the estimated number of school  aged children that can be expected to live in the proposed Redevelopment Plan area. The  study was prepared by Burgis Associates, Community Planning and Development  Consultants, dated January 2, 2008. Burgis estimates that between 9 and 13 school aged  children will   live in the proposed Redevelopment Plan Area. Their estimates may hold  true, we, however, believe that   up to thirty school aged children may live there.  At a per student cost of $14,000 annually, the school district will incur between  $140,000 (the Burgis estimate) and $420,000 annually. Notwithstanding the number of  school aged children that may live in the Redevelopment Plan Area the overall economic  impact appears to be positive.   We recommend that an economic impact study be prepared  in connection with any future redevelopment agreement   and/ or site plan application.  1 6 . Redevelopment Plan Implementation:  We have attempted to provide a workable   Redevelopment   Plan so that an  environmentally contaminated area and important waterway can be cleaned up. This Plan  also   takes advantage of the nearby public transportation system and integrates the area  with the surrounding areas, both in West Orange and Orange.  It is recommended that after appropriate review the Township Council should  proceed with preparation of the Redevelopment Plan Ordinance and request the   Plan be  review ed   by the West Orange Planning Board , pursuant to N.J.S.A.40A:12A-7 e .   The  Council may then negotiate a redevelopment agreement which includes a redevelopment  timetable, financing considerations, and additional parking provisions, if any.  Further, it is recommended that the West Orange Planning Board be authorized by  the Township Council to grant deviations from the Redevelopment Plan.   Deviations may  not be granted from the permitted uses   identified in Section   10 A of this Plan. The  building and bulk standards   identified in Section   10 A   of this Plan   may be deviated by  deminimus   amounts.  We look forward to presenting the results of our research and th e contents of this  Plan.  Prepared By  Robert L. Benecke  Kenneth Albert, PE, PP  Henry Coleman, PhD.

13 0 -13  June 25, 2013  RESOLUTION AUTHORIZING THE TOWNSHIP OF  WEST ORANGE TAX COLLECTOR TO PREPARE AND  MAIL ESTIMATED TAX BILLS IN ACCORDANCE WITH P.L. 1994, c.72  WHEREAS, the Essex County Board of Freeholders and the Township of West Orange  have not adopted the Essex County Budget and the Township of West Orange Budget for 20 13  and the West Orange Township Tax Collector will be unable to mail the Township ’ s 20 13   tax  bills on a timely basis;  WHEREAS, the Township of West Orange Tax Collector in consultation with the West  Orange Township Chief Financial Officer has computed an estimated tax levy in accordance  with N.J.S.A. 54:4-66.3, and they have both signed a certification showing the tax levies for the  previous year, the tax rates and the range of permitted estimated tax levies;  WHEREAS, in accordance with Chapter 72, P.L. 1994, the Township council requests  the Director of Division of Local Government Services to approve the estimated tax levy  exceeding 105% listed below. Approval will enable the Township to meet its financial  obligations, maintain the tax collection rate,   provide   uniformity for tax payments and save the  unnecessary cost of interest expenses on borrowing.  NOW, THEREFORE BE IT RESOLVED BY THE TOWNSHIP COUNCIL OF THE  TOWNSHIP OF WEST ORANGE, IN THE COUNTY OF ESSEX AND STATE OF NEW  JERSEY on this   2 5 th   day of   June   201 3 , as follows:  1.   The Township of West Orange Tax Collector is hereby authorized and directed to  prepare and issue estimated tax bills for the Township for the third installment of   2013  taxes. The Tax Collector shall proceed upon approval from the Director and take such  actions as are permitted and required by L. 1994, c.72 (N.J.S.A. 54:4-66.2 and 54.4-  66.3)  2.   The entire estimated tax levy for the 201 3   is hereby set at $   207,255,110.26 . In  accordance with law the third installment of 201 3   taxes shall not be subject to interest  until the later of August 10 or twenty fifth calendar day after the date the estimated tax  bills were mailed. The estimated tax bills shall contain a notice specifying the date on  which interest may begin to accrue.  CERTIFICATION  I, Karen J. Carnevale, Municipal Clerk of the Township of West Orange in the County of  Essex, do hereby certify that the foregoing is a true and correct copy of a resolution duly  adopted by the Township Council at a regular meeting held on the   2 5 th day of   June   201 3 .  IN WITNESS WHERE I have hereunto set my hand and affixed the seal of said  Township the   2 5 th   day of   June   201 3 .  Karen J. Carnevale   Victor Cirilo  Municipal Clerk   Council President  Adopted: June 25, 2013

documents.title: REFERRING HARVARD PRESS PLAN AMENDMENT TO PLANNING BOARD

pages.text: 131-13  June 25, 2013  RESOLUTION  WHEREAS,   the Township of West Orange desires to acquire two unimproved and  undeveloped parcels along Wiley Terrace known as Block 159.08, Lots 53 and 55 owned by the  Estate of Emile Karam and John T. and Patricia McCann respectively; and  WHEREAS,   the Township intends to use Planning Incentive Grant Funds administered  by the State of New Jersey, Department of Environmental Protection (NJDEP), Green Acres  Program to purchase these parcels; and  WHEREAS,   the Green Acres Program requires applicants to obtain the services of a  property appraisal company which is approved by them to develop a market value for the parcel  or parcels to be acquired; and  WHEREAS   , the Township obtained the professional appraisal services to value Block  159.08, Lot 53 and Block 159.08, Lot 55 of Hendricks Appraisal Company, LLC, Seven Hutton  Avenue, West Orange, New Jersey; and  WHEREAS,   the values of each parcel as determined by Mark Hendricks, CRE of  Hendricks Appraisal Company, LLC in his reports dated April 12, 2013 were questioned by each  property owner with particular regard to roadway and utility improvement costs and the manner  in which those costs were distributed to each of the two benefitting property owners; and  WHEREAS,   following a meeting of Township officials, the two property owners, and  Mark Hendricks of Hendricks Appraisal Company, LLC, the Township Engineer agreed to  develop a roadway and utility improvement plan for Wiley Terrace and prepare a detailed  estimate of the cost of the improvements including soft costs; and  WHEREAS,   based on the roadway and utility improvement plan, the Township  Engineer ’ s estimate dated February 11, 2013 to improve Wiley Terrace from Ridgeway Avenue  through the right of way line of a proposed cul-de-sac in the McCann property, a distance of 865  feet, is $465,370.00 or about $540.00 per linear feet; and

WHEREAS,   the Township and the property owners want Hendricks Appraisal Company,  LLC to consider the Township Engineer ’ s estimate of February 11, 2013 when valuing the  properties; and  WHEREAS,   for an additional fee, not to exceed $1,250.00 for appraisal each or  $2,500.00 total, Hendricks Appraisal Company will amend each appraisal for properties known  as Block 159.08, Lots 53 and 55; and  WHEREAS,   the Township Council concurs in the foregoing.  NOW, THEREFORE, BE IT RESOLVED   by the Township Council of the Township of  West Orange that a professional services contract with Hendricks Appraisal Company, LLC of 7  Hutton Avenue, West Orange, New Jersey is authorized to amend the two appraisals for  properties on Wiley Terrace known as Block 159.08, Lot 53 and Block 159.08, Lot 55 for a fee  not to exceed $1,250.00 each or $2,500.00 total.  BE IT FUTHER RESOLVED   that a copy of this Resolution be published in the West  Orange Chronicle within 10 days of approval in accordance with N.J.S.A. 40A:11-5(1)(a)(i).  Karen J. Carnevale, Municipal Clerk   Victor Cirilo, Council President  Adopted: June 25, 2013  I hereby   c ertify funds are available from:  Account Number  John   O.   Gross, Chief Financial Officer

pages.text: 132-13  June 25, 2013  RESOLUTION  WHEREAS,   the Township, to address flooding problems in the Mayfair District which  included Nestro Road, Midro Way, Mayfair Drive, Rosemont Terrace and Rosemont Drive,  prepared plans known as the Mayfair District Storm and Sanitary Sewer Improvements; and  WHEREAS   , these improvements were constructed in two phases with the sanitary  sewer improvements constructed initially in 2011 while permits from the New Jersey Department  of Environmental Protection (NJDEP) for the storm sewer phase which was constructed in 2012  and 2013 were pending; and  WHEREAS   prior to construction, in 2010 the Township acquired easements across 24  parcels to allow construction of both the sanitary sewer and storm sewer on private property;  and  WHEREAS   , by Agreement dated June 29, 2010 the Township acquired both temporary  and permanent utility easements across the property of Roger Thomas Williams and Mary P.  Williams, husband and wife, at 81 Nestro Road, Block 179.13, Lot 49 and in addition abandoned  and vacated certain existing easements; and  WHEREAS,   the permit received from the NJDEP in 2011 required additional storm  drains be constructed across the Williams ’ s property at 81 Nestro Road which required a  previously acquired temporary construction easement to become a permanent easement and  portions of previously vacated easements to become permanent; and  WHEREAS,   the Township prepared a new Easement Agreement and Deed of Easement  between Mary P. Williams, whose husband passed away in April 2013, and the Township which  will grant the Township a 1,318 square foot permanent utility easement in a portion of the  easterly   side yard and a portion of the rear yard of 81 Nestro Road, Block 179.13, Lot 49 for a  consideration of $4,750.00; and  WHEREAS   , this Agreement is acceptable to the property owner Mary P. Williams; and

WHEREAS   , the Township Council concurs in the foregoing.  NOW, THERFORE BE IT RESOLVED   by the Township Council of the Township of  West Orange that the attached Easement Agreement and Deed of Easement between Mary P.  Williams of 81 Nestro Road, West Orange, New Jersey, Block 179,13, Lot 49 which grants the  Township a 1,318 square foot permanent utility easements across portions of her property for a  consideration of $4,750.00 is hereby approved and Mayor Robert Parisi is authorized to execute  this Agreement and the Municipal Clerk, Karen Carnevale, shall attest his signature.  Karen J. Carnevale, Municipal Clerk   Victor Cirilo, Council President  Adopted: June 25, 2013  I hereby   c ertify funds are available from:  Account Number  John   O.   Gross, Chief Financial Officer

EASEMENT AGREEMENT AND DEED OF EASEMENT  MEMORANDUM OF AGREEMENT,   made this _____________day of  __________2013 by and between Mary P. Williams of 81 Nestro Road in the Township of West  Orange, County of Essex and State of New Jersey, hereinafter referred   to   Grantor; and  The Township of West Orange, a Municipality in the County of Essex and State of New  Jersey, with its principal office at 66 Main Street, West Orange, New Jersey hereinafter referred  to as Grantee.  WITNESSETH  WHEREAS,   the Grantor is the owner in fee of certain lands situate, lying and being in  the Township of West Orange, Essex County, New Jersey, described as Lot 49, in Block 179.13  as shown on the Tax Maps of the Township of West Orange and commonly known as 81 Nestro  Road; and  WHEREAS,   the Grantor and her late husband , Roger Thomas Williams,   who died  testate on April 9, 2013 ,   previously entered into an Easement Agreement and Deed of  Easement with the Township of West   O range dated June 29, 2010 and recorded with the Essex  County Register on November 30,   201 0 , Book 12284, Page 860, which granted the Township  permanent and temporary utility easement s   across the property of the Grantor for storm sewer  improvements associated with the   “ Mayfair Drive Sanitary and Storm Sewer Improvements ”  ( Th e   P lans)   prepared by Hatch Mott MacDonald of Millburn, New Jersey and revised August 24,  2005; and  WHEREAS,   subsequent to the execution and filing of the prior Easement Agreement  and Deed of Easement, the Township amended   T he   P lan s   to comply with New Jersey  Department of Environmental Protection Rules and Regulations which required additional storm  drain piping through the area of a temporary easement   on the easterly side of the Grantor ’ s  property   to connect to storm drain pipes which were located in the rear yard of the Grantor   in an  easement   which was previously vacated; and  WHEREAS,   this Easement Agreement and Deed of Easement will grant the Township a  permanent easement along the easterly side yard and a portion of the rear yard of   property of  the Grantor in Block 179.13, Lot 49 commonly known as 81 Nestro Road.  NOW THEREFORE,   in consideration of the sum of   Four Thousand Seven Hundred Fifty  Dollars   ($4,750.00) and other good and valuable consideration and of the foregoing and of the  mutual   covenants hereinafter   set forth, it is hereby agreed between the parties as follows:  __________________________________   ______________________________  Approved by:   Richard D. Trenk, Esq   Prepared by:   Leonard R. Lepore, P.E.  Township Attorney   Municipal Engineer

1.   The Grantor grants the Grantee a permanent utility easement across a portion of  Grant or ’ s property, Block 179.13, Lot 49 as depicted on a   “ Map Showing  Proposed   Permanent Utility   Easement, Through Lands of Roger T. Williams  and Mary P. Williams   Block 179.13, Lot 49, No. 81   Nestro Road, Township of  West Orange, New Jersey ” ,   prepared by Charles P. Stewart, P.L.S. ,   G. C.  Stewart Associates, Inc., Essex Fells, New   Jersey dated September 13, 2012  which map is   appended hereto as Schedule   “ A ” .  2.   The permanent utility easement is further described by a   “ Description of a  Permanent   Utility Easement Through Lot 49, Block 179.13, 81 Nestro Road ”  which is appended   hereto as Schedule B ” .  The receipt of the consideration is hereby acknowledged, and in further consideration of  the covenants and agreements herein contained on the part of the parties hereto, the Grantor  has given, granted, conveyed unto the Grantee, its successors and assigns for sewer purposes,  an easement in, on and through the lands of the Grantor hereinabove and more particularly  described in Schedules   “ A ”   and   “ B ” .  This Agreement shall be binding upon the respective heirs, executors, successors,  administrators, assigns and mortgagees of the parties hereto. It is understood that the effective  date of this   Agreement shall be the date of full execution and delivery thereto by and between  the parties hereto.  I N WITNESS WHEREOF , the said first party has hereunto set her hand and seal, and  said second party has cause d   its corporate seal to be hereto affixed and attested by Karen J.  Carnevale, its Clerk, and these presents to be signed by Robert Parisi, its Mayor , the day and  year first above written. pages.text: 133-13  June 25, 2013  RESOLUTION  WHEREAS   , High Tor Estates, Inc., a New Jersey Corporation with a principal place of  business formerly at 1640 Vauxhall Road, Union, New Jersey, the developer of a single family  subdivision tract known as High Tor Estates, was directed by the Township of West Orange to  design and install certain drainage improvements t hrough property of Jack M. and L inda W.  Rosen, husband and wife. Block 179.18, Lot 19 commonly known as 4 Howell Drive and through  property of Steven C. and Sheryl Delinko, husband and wife, Block 179.18, Lot 21   commonly  known as 12 Howell Drive   to address drainage problems that arose on their properties  subsequent to the lots being conveyed to   them by High Tor Estates, Inc. ; and  WHEREAS,   the Rosens and Delinkos and the Township agreed that upon installation of  drainage improvements across portions of their properties the Township would maintain these  improvements provided the Rosen s   and Delinkos granted the Township drainage easements  through their properties; and  WHEREAS,   the drainage improvements were installed by the developer, High Tor  Estates, Inc; and  WHEREAS,   the Township accepted all the improvements associated with the High Toe  Estates subdivision; and  WHEREAS,   Easement Agreements previously executed by the developer, the Rosens  and the Delinkos were not executed by the Township and subsequently   were not recorded; and  WHEREAS,   two Easement Agreements between the Rosens and the Township and the  Delinkos   and the Township   are attached; and  WHEREAS,   the Township of West Orange desires to correct this   matter   by executing  and recording new Easement Agreements; and  WHEREAS,   the Township Council concurs in the foregoing.

NOW, THEREFORE BE IT RESOLVED,   by the Township Council of the Township of  West Orange that   the   attached Easement Agreement s   between Jack M. and Linda W. Rosen,  husband and wife , of 4 Howell Drive,   West Orange, New Jersey for property known as Block  179.18, lot 19 and the Township of West Orange and between Steven C. and Sheryl Delinko,  husband and wife ,   of 12 Howell Drive, West Orange, New Jersey for property known as Block  179.18, Lot 21 are hereby approved and Mayor Robert Parisi is authorized to execute these  Agreement s   and the Municipal Clerk, Karen   Carnevale , shall attest to his signature. 134-13  June 25, 2013  RESOLUTION AUTHORIZING THE COLLECTOR OF TAXES  TO REBATE PAYMENT TO THE LISTED TAXPAYER  PURSUANT TO FINAL JUDGMENT BY THE TAX COURT  OF NEW JERSEY  WHEREAS, final judgment on tax appeals has been rendered by the Tax Court of New Jersey  on the petitions of the taxpayers listed on the attached schedule reduc ing the assessment for the year s   2005, 2006  2010, 2011,   & 2012 . documents.title: APPOINTING PAUL GRYGIEL (PHILLIPS PREISS GRYGIEL)-TEMPORARY PLANNING DIRECTOR

pages.text: 136 -13  June 25, 2013  RESOLUTION  WHEREAS,   the   Township   Ordinances   require   the   Township   to   employ   a   Planning  Director with regard to various land use and related issues; and  WHEREAS , the current Planning Director   remains   on medical leave; and  WHEREAS ,   the   Township   establishes   escrows   for   certain   commercial   and   other  development   projects   which   monies   are   dedicated   to   pay   various   expenses,   including   the  Planning Director; and  WHEREAS ,   the   Planning   &   Real   Estate   firm   of   Phillips,   Preiss,   Grygiel,   LLC,   of  Hoboken,   NJ   (the   “Phillips   Firm”)   responded   to   an   RFQ   for   Professional   Services   Through   a  Fair   and   Open   Process,   pursuant   to   Resolution   No.   46-13,   and   has   proposed   certain   hourly  rates which are competitive with and/or better   as compared   to other submissions; and  WHEREAS ,   on   or   about   July   17,   2012,   the   Township   Council   for   the   Township   of   West  Orange   adopted   Resolution   No.   155-12,   authorizing   the   Phillips   Firm   to   provide   planning  services due to the   medical leave   of the Township ’ s Planning Director; and  WHEREAS ,   on   or   about   December   18,   2012,   the   Township   Council   for   the   Township   of  West   Orange   adopted   Resolution   No.   250-12,   extending   the   term   of   the   Phillips   Firm’s   planning  services   to   the Township   for an additional six (6) months, through June 30, 2013 ; and  WHEREAS ,   there   continues   to   be   an   emergent   need   for   a   Professional   Services  Contract   with   the   Phillips   Firm   for   at   least   an   additional   six   (6)   months   in   order   to   provide  planning director services ; and  WHEREAS ,   Paul   Grygiel,   AICP,   PP   will   continue   to   take   the   lead   with   respect   to   the  Township ’ s Planning Director requirements and duties;  NOW ,   BE   IT   RESOLVED   BY   THE   TOWNSHIP   COUNCIL   OF   THE   TOWNSHIP   OF  WEST   ORANGE,   that   the   Mayor   shall   be   and   hereby   is   authorized   to   execute   the   attached  Professional   Services   Contract   with   the   Phillips   Firm ,   and   the   Township   Clerk   shall   be   and

hereby   is   authorized to   attest to the Mayor ’ s signature on same ; and it is further  RESOLVED   that   notice   of   this   award   shall   be   published   and   available   in   the   Clerk’s  office in accordance with applicable law.  Karen   J.   Carnevale   Victor Cirilo  Municipal   Clerk   Council President  Adopted:   June 25, 2013  I hereby certify funds are available from:  Account No.  John Gross,  Township Chief Financial Officer

- 1 -  136-13  PHILLIPS PREISS GRYGIEL LLC  PROFESSIONAL SERVICES CONTRACT  Client:   Township of West Orange, New Jersey  Project:   West Orange Development Application Review Services  Project Number:   N/A  Date:   July 1, 2013  The terms and conditions which govern the performance of services provided by Phillips Preiss Grygiel  LLC ( “ PPG ” ) to the Township of West Orange, New Jersey ( “ Client ” ) pursuant to this Agreement are set  forth below:  1.   Scope of Services   . PPG will review applications made to West Orange ’ s Planning Board and  Zoning Board of Adjustment for site plan, subdivision and/or variance approval. The scope of work  for each of these development reviews will include the following:     Review of filed application materials to determine whether the application can be deemed  complete for hearing by the Planning Board, Zoning Board of Adjustment and/or Technical  Advisory Committee, and if any submission waivers are warranted     Preparation of a completeness review memorandum     Attendance at Technical Advisory Committee meetings     Review of filed application materials to determine compliance with all applicable Zoning  Ordinance requirements and other development regulations     Analysis of all requested variances and/or exceptions from site plan or subdivision  regulations     Review of site plan, architectural plans and/or subdivision plat to identify any zoning or  planning issues     A site visit to the subject property and surrounding area     Discussion with Township of West Orange staff and other consultants, if necessary     Preparation of an application review letter, if necessary     Attendance at evening meetings of the Planning Board and Zoning Board of Adjustment  when an application for which a review letter was prepared is on the board ’ s agenda  The above work other than site visits and meetings will be conducted in PPG ’ s office. It is  assumed that application materials will be mailed to PPG by Township staff, although PPG will be  able to pick up materials in West Orange if a quick turnaround of a review letter is necessary. A  representative of PPG will also attend meetings in West Orange to meet with Township staff,  other consultants and/or development applicants, as needed.

- 2 -  The review of development applications can be paid for through escrows provided by  development applicants, consistent with state law and Section 25-55 of the West Orange Zoning  Ordinance. PPG will submit invoices to the Township for each development application in order to  manage the appropriate funding source for our service.  It is anticipated there will be a limited amount of billing for general planning services not related to  a specific application, which would be billed directly to the Township and would not be payable  through applicant escrows. If additional work, over and above that specified in the Scope of  Services, or that agreed to under this Agreement, is requested by the Client, such work shall be  performed by PPG at the hourly billing rates specified in paragraph 7 of this Agreement or any  increases to the hourly billing rates as set forth in paragraph 7. Such additional services shall only  be performed following approval from the Township Council.  Paul Grygiel, AICP, PP shall make himself available as needed on a flat fee basis as set forth  herein.  2.   Reimbursable Expenses   . The Client shall pay PPG the actual cost for reimbursable expenses,  including printing and reproduction (except as noted in paragraph 2), courier and express delivery  service, bulk/special mailings, and travel expenses, including lodging and meals, for work  involving travel to places more than fifty (50) miles from PPG's office in Hoboken, New Jersey..  3.   Non-Reimbursable Expenses   . The Client shall not be billed for the following ordinary  administration and overhead: clerical and bookkeeping time related to the project, all telephone  and facsimile costs, costs of reproduction of up to twenty (20) copies of no more than one (1) draft  and one (1) final copy of project memorandums or reports, and travel expenses involving travel to  places less than fifty (50) miles from PPG's office in Hoboken, New Jersey.  4.   Invoices and payment   . Invoices shall be sent on a monthly basis starting from the date of  execution of the agreement or the date upon which work is initiated on the project, whichever is  sooner. Invoices will be submitted to the Client normally by the middle of the month following the  month in which the services were rendered. Invoices will include a list of the personnel who  furnished services, their billing rates (as specified in paragraph 7) and the number of hours they  worked on the assignment. An invoice shall be considered PAST DUE if the invoice is not paid  within forty-five (45) days from the date of the invoice. All non-escrow reimbursable fees for  services rendered shall be at a flat fee rate, capped at $2,000.00 per month. In the event

- 3 -  additional payment is sought by PPG for work performed beyond the scope of services, such work  shall only be performed and payment shall only be rendered upon approval by the Township  Council.  5.   Suspension of Services   by PPG   . If the Client fails to make payments when due or otherwise is in  breach of this Agreement, PPG may suspend performance five (5) days from the date of providing  notice to the Client. PPG shall have no liability whatsoever to the Client for any costs or damages  as a result of such suspension. Following suspension of services for failure to make payment,  work on the project shall only be resumed when all invoices have been brought fully to date,  including all charges for late payment (including interest), has been replaced.  6.   Client's Request for Cessation of Work   . The Client may, at any time, request in writing that work  on the project be terminated. At such time the Client will be billed for all work performed by PPG  up to the time such notification is received at the offices of PPG.  7.   Hourly Billing Rates for PPG Staff  The following shall be the hourly billing rates for PPG staff:  Staff person/Title   Hourly Rate  Paul Phillips, Principal   $150  Richard Preiss, Principal   $150  Paul Grygiel, Principal   $140  Keenan Hughes, Principal   $125  Elizabeth Leheny, Principal   $125  Senior Associates   $125  Senior Urban Designer   $125  Associates   $115  Planners   $90  Graphic Designers   $85  Computer Specialists   $40  8.   Fee Duration   . This agreement shall be open for acceptance for sixty (60) days from the above  date. If the Agreement is signed after that date, PPG may adjust the proposed fees, including the  hourly billing rates, as specified in paragraph 7 prior to commencement of services.  The hourly billing rates set forth in paragraph 7 shall remain in effect for a period of six (6) months  from the above date. This agreement shall be for a six (6) month period beginning July 1, 2013  and ending December 31, 2013.

- 4 -  Where Lump Sum or Not-to-Exceed Contracts are signed, and the services provided by PPG  extend three (3) months beyond the termination date agreed to by PPG and the Client, the quoted  prices shall be subject to an increase in accordance with the Consumer Price's New Jersey  Metropolitan Area Cost of Living Index, using the date of the Agreement as a base. In the event  that a Lump Sum or Not-to-Exceed Contract item is partially completed at such time, the lump  sum or not-to-exceed price is to be adjusted, and the balance of the lump sum fee still to be billed  as of the termination date agreed to, shall be increased based upon the Cost of Living Index as  indicated above.  9.   Indemnification   . PPG agrees, subject to the provisions herein, to indemnify and hold the Client  harmless from any damage, liability or cost to the extent caused solely by PPG's negligent acts,  errors or omissions in the performance of professional services under this Agreement. PPG is not  obligated to indemnify the Client for the Client's own negligence or intentional acts.  10.   Governing Law   — Forum Selection   . The laws of the State of New Jersey, County of Essex will  govern the validity of this Agreement, its interpretation and performance. Any litigation arising in  any way from this Agreement shall be brought in the Courts of New Jersey, County of Essex.  11.   Entire Agreement   — Amendments   . This Agreement comprises the final and complete Agreement  between the Client and PPG. It supersedes all prior or contemporaneous communications,  representations, or agreements, whether oral or written, relating to the subject matter of this  Agreement. Execution of this Agreement signifies that each party has read the document  thoroughly, has had the opportunity to have questions explained by independent counsel, and is  satisfied with the terms and conditions contained herein and has authority to enter into this  Agreement. Amendments to this Agreement shall not be binding unless made in writing and  signed by both the Client and PPG.  12.   Term and Termination   . This Professional Services Contract may be terminated by the Township  of West Orange through its Business Administrator, or by PPG, on ten (10) days ’   written notice to  the other party.  13.   Headings.   Headings are set forth for the convenience of the parties only, and are not part of this  Agreement.

ORDINANCE   2378-13  AN   ORDINANCE   AMEND ING   AND   SUPPLEMENTING   CHAPTER   3,   SECTION   5   OF   THE  GENERAL ORDINANCES OF THE TOWNSHIP OF WEST ORANGE, ENTITLED   “ A GENDA ”  BE   IT   ORDAINED   BY   THE   TOWNSHIP   COUNCIL   OF   THE   TOWNSHIP   OF   WEST  ORANGE ,   in   the   County   of   Essex   and   Stat e   of   New   Jersey,   that   Chapter   3,   Section   5   of   the  Revised   General   Ordinances   of   the   Township   of   West   Orange   is   hereby   amended   and  supplemented as follows:  I.   PURPOSE  The purpose of this Ordinance is to   make clearer the Agenda and posting process.  II.   CHAPTER   3,   SECTION   5   SHALL   BE   AND   HEREBY   IS   AMENDED   AND  SUPERSEDED TO READ AS FOLLOWS:  An   agenda   for   each   regular   meeting   of   the   Township   Council   shall   be   prepared   by   the  Clerk   under   the   supervision   of   the   President   of   the   Council.   All   Ordinances,   Resolutions   and   any  supporting   written   material   for   consideration   at   the   next   Council   Meeting   shall   be   delivered   to  and   received   at   the   Clerk's   Office   by   3:00   p.m.,   on   the   Wednesday   next   preceding   the   meeting,  earlier   if   possible   and,   if   later,   shall   require   permission   of   the   Council   President   before  placement   on   the   agenda.   The   agenda   headings   shall   be   written   in   plain   English   and   each  Resolution   and   Ordinance,   unless   clearly   self-explanatory,   shall   include   a   brief   description   of   its  purpose   also   written   in   plain   English   by   the   department   submitting   it.   The   agenda   shall   be  completed   not   later   than   Noon   on   the   Thursday   preceding   each   regular   meeting   of   the   Council,  except   for   emergency   matters   which   may   be   added   to   the   agenda   at   any   time   before   the  meeting   with   permission   of   the   President   of   the   Council.   The   Clerk   shall   expeditiously   deliver   a  copy   of   the   Agenda,   all   Ordinances   and   Resolutions   and   all   submitted   supporting   materials,   to  each   Councilmember,   to   the   Mayor,   Business   Administrator,   and   to   the   Law   Department.   The  Clerk   shall   expeditiously   cause   the   agenda,   including   the   Ordinances,   Resolutions   and   all  submitted   supporting   materials,   to   be   posted   on   the   Township's   website   no   later   than   the  following   Friday   morning   in   order   that   such   materials   are   readily   available   to   the   general   public.  It   shall   be   the   responsibility   of   the   Administration   to   determine   if   any   submitted   supporting  materials   contain   information   which   is   privileged,   confidential,   or   for   some   lawful   reason   should  not   be   published   at   that   time.   Such   supporting   materials   shall   not   be   posted   on   the   Township’s  website   or   otherwise   disclosed   to   the   public   until   the   reasons   for   non-disclosure   no   longer   exist.  In   the   event   of   an   intervening   holiday,   the   deadlines   set   forth   here   shall   be   adjusted   to   provide  the   same   amount   of   time   for   preparation   and   notice   of   the   agenda.   In   the   event   of   additions,  deletions,   or   changes   of   any   kind   to   the   agenda,   or   any   item   on   it,   such   changes   shall   be  expeditiously   posted   to   the   Township's   website   with   a   notation   indicating   the   change   by   4:30  p.m.,   on   the   Monday   preceding   the   Council   meeting.   The   agenda   and   any   changes   to   it   shall  also   be   physically   posted   on   the   Clerk's   Bulletin   Board   in   the   public   hallway   at   Town   Hall.   A  conforming   agenda   shall   be   published   to   the   Township's   website   on   the   day   after   each   Council  meeting   to   indicate   those   items   which   were   not   acted   upon   due   to   removal   or   postponement   by  the Council at its meeting.   (1972 Code § 3-5; Ord. No. 2349-12)

2  III.   REPEAL OF CONFLICTING ORDINANCES  Any   Ordinances   of   the   Township,   which   are   in   conflict   with   this   Ordinance,   are   hereby  repealed to the extent of such conflict.  IV.   SEVERABILITY  If   any   part   of   this   Ordinance   shall   be   deemed   invalid,   such   parts   shall   be   severed   and  the invalidity thereof shall not affect the remaining parts of this Ordinance.  V.   EFFECTIVE DATE  This   Ordinance   shall   take   effect   upon   final   passage   and   publication   in   accordance   with  the law.  __________________________________  Honorable Victor Ci rilo  Council President  ___________________________________  Honorable Robert D. Parisi  Mayor  Introduced :   June 25, 2013  Adopted: July 16, 2013  Approved as to form on the basis of the facts provided:  _________________________________________  Karen J. Carnevale, Municipal Clerk

3  2378-13  LEGISLATIVE HISTORY  This   proposed   Ordinance   is   intended   to   further   clarify   the   process   and   procedure  regarding   the   delivery   and   availability   of   materials   to   be   considered   by   the   Township   Council   in  connection   with   each   Council   meeting.   There   has   been   Council   time   expended   discussing   what  materials   should   be   publicly   available,   and   in   order   to   more   expeditiously   address   matters   of  business   before   the   Council,   it   is   necessary   for   the   to   ensure   a   uniform   procedure   for   the   timely  submission and   publicly-available   posting of   agenda   materials.  This   proposed   Ordinance   would   also   require   the   Township’s   administration   to   timely  verify   which   materials   being   presented   to   the   Council   for   consideration   contain   privileged   or  confidential   information   which   should   not   be   publicly   disclosed .   Ultimately ,   this   Ordinance   is  necessary   and   proper,   intended   to   establish   clear   and   uniform   procedures   for   submission   and  consideration   of   agenda   materials   for   the   start   of   the   2013   Council   term ,   and   each   year  thereafter,   unless and   until the Ordinance is further amended.  743017v1

114-13  June   11, 2013  RESOLUTION  WHEREAS,   The Township Council of the Township of West Orange has the  authority pursuant to the applicable state statutes and municipal ordinances, to  appoint a Deputy Township Clerk; and  WHEREAS , the Township Council of the Township of West Orange is desirous  of reappointing JoAnn Behar to that position;  NOW, THEREFORE, BE IT RESOLVED , by the Township Council of the  Township of West Orange that JoAnn Behar be and hereby is reappointed Deputy  Clerk of the Township of West Orange for a two (2) year term   beginning on  July 1, 201 3   through June 30, 201 5 ; and  BE IT FURTHER RESOLVED , that she shall perform the duties of the  Township Clerk in the absence or disability of the Township Clerk or upon the  direction of the Township Clerk and she shall perform such other duties as m a y be  directed by the Township Council of the Township of West Orange, all in  accordance with the applicable state statutes and municipal ordinances; and  BE IT FURTHER RESOLVED , that JoAnn Behar is hereby appointed Assistant  Secretary to the Alcoholic Beverage Control for the Township of West Orange and  shall perform the duties of the Secretary of Alcoholic Beverage Control for the  Township of West Orange in the absence or disability of the Secretary of the  Alcoholic Beverage Control of the Township of West Orange.  Karen J. Carnevale, Municipal Clerk   Victor Cirilo , Council President  Adopted: June   11, 2013

115-13  June 11, 2013  RESOLUTION  WHEREAS,   the   Township   of   West   Orange   (“Township”),   through   the   Downtown   West  Orange   Alliance,   annually   sponsors   the   Downtown   Classic   5K   run   along   Main   Street   in   the  Township for charitable purposes ; and  WHEREAS,   the   Downtown   Classic   5K   run   is   scheduled   to   take   place   this   year   on  Sunday,   September 29,   2013   from approximately 9:30 am until 1:00 pm; and  WHEREAS,   the   Township   desires   to   enter   into   an   agreement   that   would   allow   the  National   Ovarian   Cancer   Coalition   (“NOCC”)   to   participate   in   and   benefit   from   this   year’s  Downtown Classic 5K run; and  WHEREAS,   pursuant   to   the   terms   of   the   proposed   agreement ,   the   Township   would  share   profits   with   the   NOCC ,   specifically   by   first   subtracting   direct   expenses   incurred   by   the  Township   or   the   Downtown   West   Orange   Alliance,   and   then   sharing   seventy   percent   (70%)   of  the   net   profits   generated   from   the   Downtown   Classic   5K   run   with   the   NOCC ,   with   the   remaining  thirty percent (30%) of net profits to be retained by the Township; and  WHEREAS,   the   NOCC   has   agreed   to   provide   its   own   publicity   and   fundraising   efforts   for  the event in coordination with the Downtown   West Orange   Alliance; and  WHEREAS,   the   NOCC   has   agreed   to   provide   the   Township   with   proof   of   liability  insurance   and   to   indemnify   and   hold   the   Township   harmless   from   any   claims,   suits,   damages,  or   judgments   resulting   wholly   or   in   part   from   any   acts   or   omissions   of   the   NOCC   or   their   agents,  employees,   guests,   licensees,   invitees,   assignees   or   successors   during   this   year’s   Downtown  Classic 5K run; and  WHEREAS,   annexed   hereto   as   Attachment   “A”   is   a   copy   of   the   Memorandum   of  Agreement to be executed by the Township and the   NOCC ;

NOW,   THEREFORE   BE   IT   RESOLVED,   by   the   Township   Council   of   the   Township   of  West   Orange   that   the   Mayor   be   and   hereby   is   authorized   to   execute   the   annexed   Memorandum  of Agreement; and the Municipal Clerk shall attest to the Mayor's signature on the Agreement.  ________________________   _________________________  Karen J. Carnevale   Victor Cirilo  Municipal Clerk   Council President  Adopted:   June 11, 2013

115-13  MEMORANDUM OF   AGREEMENT  THIS   MEMORANDUM   OF   AGREEMENT   (hereinafter   the   "Agreement")   is  made   and   entered   into   as   of   this   _____   day   of   June,   2013  ,   by   and   between   TOWNSHIP  OF   WEST   ORANGE   (the   “Township”) ,   having   an   address   of   60   Main   Street,   West  Orange,   New   Jersey   07052   and   the   NATIONAL   OVARIAN   CANCER   COALITION  (“NOCC”),   having   an   address   of   2501   Oak   Lawn   Avenue,   Suite   435,   Dallas,   Texas  75219.  WITNESSETH  WHEREAS ,   the   Township,   through   the   Downtown   West   Orange   Alliance,   annually  sponsors   the   Downtown   Classic   5K   run   along   Main   Street   in   the   Township   for   charitable  purposes; and  WHEREAS ,   the   Township   has   sponsored   this   run   for   many   years   and   each   year  several hundred people have attended; and  WHEREAS,   the   Township   now   desires   to   enter   into   an   agreement   that   would  allow   the   NOCC   to   participate   in   and   benefit   from   this   year’s   Downtown   Classic   5K   run,  which is scheduled to be held on   Sunday, September 29, 2013   (the   “ Event ” );  NOW,   THEREFORE,   in   consideration   of   the   covenants   and   agreements  hereinafter contained, the parties hereto agree as follows:  1.   The   Event   shall   be   held   on   Sunday,   September   29 ,   2013   from  approximately 9:30 a.m. to 1:00 p.m.  2.   The   Township   shall   and   hereby   does   agree   to   share   with   the   NOCC,   after  subtracting   any   direct   expenses   incurred   by   either   the   Township   and/or   the   Downtown  West   Orange   Alliance,   seventy   percent   (70%)   of   the   net   profits   generated   from   the   Event.  The   remaining   thirty   percent   (30%)   of   the   net   profits   shall   be   retained   by   the   Township.  It   is   anticipated   that   profits   will   be   generated   from   the   registration   fee   paid   by   participants  of   the   Event   and   any   donations   received   by   either   the   Downtown   West   Orange   Alliance  or   the   NOCC,   its   members   or   agents.   The   NOCC   shall   be   entitled   to   receive   an  accounting of the money raised from the Event prior to the disbursement of any funds.  3.   The   NOCC   will   provide   its   own   publicity   and   fundraising   efforts   for   the  Event   in   coordination   with   the   West   Orange   Downtown   Alliance.   The   West   Orange  Downtown   Alliance   reserves   the   right   to   approve   any   and   all   publicity/fundraising  material   that   is   distributed   in   connection   with   the   Event.   Any   use   of   the   NOCC   name,  logo,   or   stationery   in   mailings,   advertising,   or   for   the   media   shall   be   pre-approved   by   the  NOCC.

2  4.   The   NOCC   shall   provide   a   representative   who   will   be   available   and  present   at   the   Event   for   purposes   of   assisting   the   Downtown   West   Orange   Alliance   with  any fundraising, public relations and/or marketing issues.  5.   The   NOCC   shall   provide   proof   of   liability   insurance   with   a   minimum  combined   single   limit   of   One   Million   ($1,000,000.00)   dollars   for   bodily   injury   and  property   damage.   The   NOCC   shall   provide   an   original   Certificate   of   Insurance   to   this  effect   upon   signing   this   agreement.   The   NOCC’s   policy   shall   name   the   Township   of  West Orange and the   Downtown   West Orange Alliance as additional insureds.  6.   The   NOCC   agrees   to   defend,   indemnify,   hold   and   save   harmless   the  Township   a nd   the   West   Orange   Downtown   Alliance,   its   respective   members,   officers,  representatives,   employees   from   and   against   any   and   all   claims,   suits,   damages,  judgments   and   expenses   (including   reasonable   attorneys ’   fees)   resulting   wholly   or   in   part  from   any   acts   or   omissions   by   the   NOCC   or   the   NOCC ’s   agents,   employees,   guests,  licensees, invitees, assignees or successors   during the Event.  7.   It   is   understood   and   agreed   that   all   understandings   and   agreements  heretofore   had   between   the   Township   and   the   NOCC   regarding   the   Event   are   merged   in  this   Agreement,   which   alone   fully   and   completely   expresses   their   agreement,   and   that   the  same   is   entered   into   after   full   investigation,   neither   party   relying   upon   any   statement   or  representation not embodied in this Agreement, made by the other.  8.   This   Agreement   may   not   be   changed   or   terminated   orally.   It   shall  only be modified by written agreement.  9.   The   Parties   hereby   acknowledge   that   the   Agreement   shall   be   construed,  interpreted   and   enforced   in   accordance   with   the   laws   (excluding   conflicts   of   law  principles) of the State of New Jersey, and before the c ourts of the State of New Jersey.  10.   This   Agreement   may   be   executed   in   several   counterparts,   which   shall  constitute one and the same instrument.  IN   WITNESS   WHEREOF,   the   parties   have   executed   this   Agreement   the   day  and year first above written.  TOWNSHIP OF WEST ORANGE  Dated: ____________   Signed:  Robert D. Parisi, Mayor  Dated:   ____________   Attest:  Karen J. Carnevale, Municipal Clerk

116-13  June 11, 2013  RESOLUTION  WHEREAS   , in accordance with Directive Number 102 issued by the State Office  of Emergency Management March 15, 1990 each Municipality must have a Municipal  Emergency Management Coordinator; and  WHEREAS   , Peter   F.   Smeraldo , Jr. , Fire Chief,   has the necessary background  and expertise to perform the duties of Municipal Emergency Management Coordinator;  and  WHEREAS,   the Mayor has recommended the appointment of Peter   F.   Smeraldo ,  Jr.   as Municipal Emergency Management Coordinator   for a three year term expiring on  June   1 5 , 201 6 ;  NOW, THEREFORE, BE IT RESOLVED   , by the Mayor and Township Council of  the Township of West Orange, County of Essex,   and State   of New Jersey, that Peter  Smeraldo , Jr. , Fire Chief, is hereby appointed as Municipal Emergency Management  Coordinator.  BE IT FURTHER RESOLVED,   that a copy of this resolution be sent to the Essex  County Office of Emergency Management.  Karen J. Carnevale   Victor Cirilo  Township Clerk   Council President  Adopted:   June 11, 2013

117-13  June 11, 2013  RESOLUTION  WHEREAS,   on   or   about   December   6,   2011,   certain   litigation   captioned   Sadlon,   et   al.   v.  The   Pointe,   et   al. ,   was   commenced   before   the   Superior   Court   of   New   Jersey,   Law   Division:  Essex County, Docket No. ESX-L-9351-11 (the   “ Litigation ” ); and  WHEREAS,   the   Litigation   named   the   Township   as   a   co-defendant   based   upon   the  November 25, 1997 Developer ’ s Agreement; and  WHEREAS,   the Court denied a temporary restraining order; and  WHEREAS,   the Court directed mediation; and  WHEREAS,   Plaintiff asserted certain claims against the Township; and  WHEREAS,   Barbara Weisman, Esq. was designated mediator; and  WHEREAS,   the   mediator   conducted   various   sessions   and   participated   in   various  communications between the parties; and  WHEREAS,   pursuant   to   the   mediation   program,   the   mediation   expenses   are   split   evenly  among the parties; and  WHEREAS,   the mediator expended a total of   8.8   hours in this matter; and  WHEREAS,   the mediator ’ s total invoice is $ 3,078.00 ; and  WHEREAS,   the Township ’ s portion of the invoice is $1,026;  NOW,   BE   IT   HEREBY   RESOLVED ,   by   the   Township   Council   of   the   Township   of   West  Orange   that   the   Township   be   and   is   hereby   authorized   to   pay   Barbara   Weisman,   Esq.,   as  mediator,   the   sum   of   $1,026   in   full   satisfaction   of   all   mediation   expenses   in   this   matter;   and   it   is  further  RESOLVED   that   a   copy   of   this   Resolution   shall   be   advertised   and   available   in  accordance with applicable law.  ________________________________  K aren   J.   Carnevale, Municipal Clerk   Victor Cirilo ,   Council President
